annotations.label: plant embryo proper
annotations.definition: An embryonic plant structure (PO:0025099) that is the body of a developing plant embryo (PO:0009009) attached to the maternal tissue in an plant ovule (PO:0020003) by a suspensor (PO:0020108).
annotations.comment: The embryo proper is the entire embryo exclusive of the suspensor.

annotations.label: anther wall
annotations.definition: A microsporangium wall (PO:0025307) that is part of an anther (PO:0009066).
annotations.comment: Has an outer epidermis (exothecium) and an endothecium and may have additional layers. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

annotations.label: whole plant
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0005679) which is a whole organism.
annotations.comment: Examples include plant embryo (PO:0009009), megagametophyte (PO:0025279) and microgametophyte (PO:0025280).

annotations.label: in vitro plant structure
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0009011) that is grown or maintained in vitro.
annotations.comment: In vitro growth is defined as microbe-free growth in a sterile, culture environment, usually in the laboratory. An example of a sterile culture environment is growth on or in a nutrient medium in a Petri dish, culture flask or test tube. Microbes may be intentionally added to the culture environment, such as in co-culture with Agrobacterium.

annotations.label: cultured plant cell
annotations.definition: A plant cell (PO:0009002) that is grown or maintained in vitro.
annotations.comment: Includes isolated plant cells and small plant cell aggregates that proliferate while suspended in sterile liquid medium or spread on a solid agar medium.

annotations.label: plant protoplast
annotations.definition: A cultured plant cell from which the entire plant cell wall has been removed.
annotations.comment: The cell wall is generally removed using polysaccharide-degrading enzymes such as cellulase, pectinase and /or xylanase. The cultured plant cell may be in suspension culture or from callus culture on solid media.

synonyms.text: 植物プロトプラスト (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf-derived cultured plant cell
annotations.definition: A cultured plant cell that was derived from leaf tissue.
annotations.comment: May be derived from cultured plant callus or plant protoplast that was induced from a segment of leaf. In the case of the plant protoplast, after the re-establishment cell walls.

synonyms.text: 葉由来植物培養細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: root-derived cultured plant cell
annotations.definition: A cultured plant cell that was derived from root tissue.
annotations.comment: May be derived from cultured plant callus or plant protoplasts induced from a segment of root. In the case of the plant protoplast, after the re-establishment plant cell walls.

synonyms.text: 根由来植物培養細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: cultured plant callus
annotations.definition: A plant callus (PO:0005052) grown or maintained in vitro.
annotations.comment: Cultured plant callus may induced to form from plant protoplasts or cultured plant cells in suspension culture. After supplementation with the appropriate plant growth regulators, further differentiation may lead to the development of plant tissues, plant organs and ultimately, a whole plant.

annotations.label: cultured plant embryo
annotations.definition: A plant embryo (PO:0009009) that is grown or maintained in vitro.
annotations.comment: Includes isolated and maintained mature or immature zygotic embryos, somatic embryos or haploid embryos (derived from male gametophyte).

annotations.label: cultured somatic plant embryo
annotations.definition: A somatic plant embryo grown and maintained in vitro.
annotations.comment: Cultured somatic plant embryos are commonly induced after the formation of callus from an explant and treatment with plant growth regulators.

synonyms.text: 培養植物体細胞不定胚 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: apical hook
annotations.definition: Hook-like structure which develops at the apical part of the hypocotyl in dark-grown seedlings in dicots.

synonyms.text: 頂端かぎ状屈曲構造 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: cauline leaf
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf (PO:0009025), borne on the stem (PO:0009047).
annotations.comment: In Arabidopsis, refers to the leaves that are borne on the elongated inflorescence branches. May ocur singly or pairs/whorls of leaves.

annotations.label: rosette leaf
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf (PO:0009025) born in a circular pattern around a stem (PO:0009047) with compressed stem internodes (PO:0020142).
annotations.comment: Arabidopsis, vegetative development is characterized by the production of the rosette leaves.

annotations.label: cotyledon primordium
annotations.definition: A phyllome primordium (PO:0025128) that develops from a cotyledon anlagen (PO:0025432) and is part of a vegetative shoot apex (PO:0025223) and is committed to the development of a cotyledon (PO:0020030).

annotations.label: lateral root primordium
annotations.definition: A root primordium (PO:0005029) that is part of a root (PO:0009005) and is committed to the development of a lateral root (PO:0020121).
annotations.comment: A lateral root primordium may arise from cells of a pericycle (PO:0006203) in a root, as in most seed plants (pericyclic lateral root primordium; PO:0025492), or from cells of an endodermis (PO:0000252) or other tissue in ferns and some seed plants (non-pericyclic lateral root primordium: PO:0025493). Transition from lateral root primordium to lateral root occurs with the formation of a root cap (PO:0020123), shortly after the development of a root apical meristem (PO:0020147), which can occur before the lateral root penetrates the exterior of the parent root.

synonyms.text: portion of lateral root primordium tissue (exact)

annotations.label: vascular leaf primordium
annotations.definition: A phyllome primordium (PO:0025128) that develops from a vascular leaf anlagen (PO:0025431) and is part of a vegetative shoot apex (PO:0025223) and is committed to the development of a vascular leaf (PO:0009025).

synonyms.text: portion of vascular leaf primordium tissue (exact)

annotations.label: ovule primordium
annotations.definition: A floral structure primordium (PO:0025477) that is committed to the development of an ovule (PO:0020003).
annotations.comment: The transition from ovule primordium to ovule occurs when an integument (PO:0020021) begins to develop from the protoderm (PO:0006210).

annotations.label: gynoecium primordium
annotations.definition: A floral structure primordium (PO:0025477) that is committed to the development of a gynoecium (PO:0009062).
annotations.comment: Use carpel primordium (PO:0004703) for the primordium (PO:0025127) of an individual carpel (PO:0009030).

annotations.label: petal primordium
annotations.definition: A phyllome primordium (PO:0025128) that develops from a petal anlagen (PO:0025484) and is committed to the development of a petal (PO:0009032).

annotations.label: plant cuticle
annotations.definition: A portion of plant substance (PO:0025161) composed of cutin (PO:0025385), a polyester matrix of hydroxy- and hydroxy epoxy fatty acids C16 and C18 chains, embedded and overlayed with cuticular wax (PO:0025386) and forming a layer on the surface of a plant epidermis (PO:0005679).
annotations.comment: The plant cuticle is produced in and secreted by epidermal cells (PO:0004013) of leaves (PO:0025034), stems (PO:0009047), fruits (PO:0009001), and other aerial plant structures. It has as parts an inner cuticle proper (PO:0025387) and an outer layer of epicuticular wax (PO:0025388).

annotations.label: root tip
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) which is the apical portion of the root (PO:0009005), and includes the root apical meristem (PO:0020147) and root cap (PO:0020123).

annotations.label: primary root tip
annotations.definition: The portion of the primary root including the meristem and root cap.

synonyms.text: punta de la ra&#237z primaria (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: lateral root tip
annotations.definition: A root tip (PO:0000025) of a lateral root (PO:0020121).

synonyms.text: punta de la ra&#237z lateral (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: septum
annotations.definition: A collective organ part structure composed of two or more layers of various tissues that is a partition that divides a cavity formed by the fusion of two or more organs.
annotations.comment: A septum is formed by the fusion of the walls of two adjacent organs (ovary walls or anther walls), for example, the partition between two sections of an orange, seen in cross-section.

annotations.label: lenticel
annotations.definition: A portion of periderm tissue (PO:0005046) characterized by the presence of intercellular spaces between the cells of the phellem (PO:0004003) and phelloderm (PO:0005050)
annotations.comment: Lenticels are produced by the cork cambium (PO:0005599) and facilitate gas exchange.

annotations.label: tetrad of microspores
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue consisting of four microspores that remained joined together shortly after meiosis. Each cell will give rise to a male gametophyte.

synonyms.text: 小胞子の四分子   小胞子四分子 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: fruit valve
annotations.definition: A valve that is a part of a fruit that splits apart when the fruit dehisces.
annotations.comment: A valve may develop from an entire carpel (as in Brassicaceae) or from part of a carpel (as in Fabaceae). If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: vascular system
annotations.definition: A maximal portion of vascular tissue (PO:0009015) in a whole plant (PO:0000003), collective plant structure (PO:0025497), multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025496), or cardinal part of multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025498).
annotations.comment: A vascular system consist of the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a plant structure. May include other portions of plant tissue or plant cells (PO:0009002) of other types, such as parenchyma (PO:0005421) or fiber cells (PO:0025407).

annotations.label: cotyledon vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that is part of a cotyledon (PO:0020030).

annotations.label: leaf vascular system
annotations.definition: A phyllome vascular system (PO:0025206) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a vascular leaf (PO:0009025).

synonyms.text: sistema vascular de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: shoot axis apex
annotations.definition: A shoot axis (PO:0025029) that is the most distal part of a shoot system (PO:0009006) and has as parts a shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) and the youngest primordia (PO:0025127).

annotations.label: primary endosperm cell
annotations.definition: Cell produced by the fusion of sperm cell nucleus and two polar nuclei of the central cell (or diploid secondary endosperm nucleus, in cases where two polar nuclei fuse together before double fertilization). The first cell of the endosperm, often triploid.

annotations.label: shoot axis vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a shoot axis (PO:0025029).
annotations.comment: Can occur in a stem or branch.

synonyms.text: sistema vascular del eje del epiblasto (epiblastema) (Spanish, exact)
シュート軸維管系 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: adventitious root nodule
annotations.definition: Enlargement or swelling at position of dormant adventitious root primordium (on the stem), inhabited by nitrogen-fixing bacteria.
annotations.comment: Unlike the root nodules, these contain functioning chloroplasts in the nodule cortex and are therefore capable of carbon fixation. Note: stem nodule is commonly used term for this structure, although the nodules are formed at positions of dormant adventitious root primordia.

synonyms.text: n&#243dulo de la ra&#237z adventicia (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: epidermal cork cell
annotations.definition: One of the two types of short cells in the epidermis of grasses and bamboos. It is usually paired with silica cell.
annotations.comment: Developmentally different from periderm cork cell (i.e., phellem), produced by cork cambium. If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025).

annotations.label: shoot-borne root
annotations.definition: A root (PO:0009005) that develops from shoot axis (PO:0025029) tissue.

synonyms.text: epiblasto (epiblastema) que se origina en la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)
シュート細胞由来の根  (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: crown root
annotations.definition: A shoot-borne nodal root (PO:0003005) formed at a stem base (PO:0008039).

annotations.label: prop root
annotations.definition: A shoot-borne nodal root (PO:0003005) formed at a stem node (PO:0020141) above the ground and growing directly into the soil.
annotations.comment: This term is perhaps best defined functionally to prop the plant up, but sometimes prop roots may also act as breathing roots. Compare to pneumatophore (PO:0025357), which is a lateral root that grows upwards from below the soil or water surface.

synonyms.text: 支柱根、支持根、支柱気根 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: embryo root
annotations.definition: A root (PO:0009005) that is initiated in a developing plant embryo (PO:0009009).

annotations.label: seminal root
annotations.definition: An embryo root (PO:0000045) formed within the scutellar node (PO:0004708) of the plant embryo (PO:0009009) in some monocotyledonous plants.

annotations.label: leaf lamina epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of leaf epidermis (PO:0006016) that is part of a leaf lamina (PO:0020039).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: epidermis de la l&#225mina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: leaf lamina vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a leaf lamina (PO:0020039).

synonyms.text: sistema vascular de la l&#225mina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: leaf lamina abaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of leaf lamina epidermis (PO:0000047) that covers the abaxial/lower surface of a leaf lamina (PO:0020039).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: epidermis abaxial de la l&#225mina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: leaf lamina adaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of leaf lamina epidermis (PO:0000047) that covers the adaxial/upper surface of a leaf lamina (PO:0020039).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: epidermis adaxial de la l&#225mina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: petiole epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of leaf epidermis (PO:0006016) that is part of a petiole (PO:0020038).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

annotations.label: petiole vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a petiole (PO:0020038).

annotations.label: petiole cortex
annotations.definition: A portion of cortex (PO:0005708) that is part of a petiole (PO:0020038).

annotations.label: petal vascular system
annotations.definition: A phyllome vascular system (PO:0025206) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a petal (PO:0025218).

annotations.label: bud
annotations.definition: An undeveloped shoot system (PO:0009006).

annotations.label: flower bud
annotations.definition: A bud that develops into a flower.

annotations.label: inflorescence bud
annotations.definition: A bud that develops into an inflorescence.

annotations.label: vegetative bud
annotations.definition: A bud (PO:0000055) that develops into a vegetative shoot system (PO:0025607).

annotations.label: root initial cell
annotations.definition: An initial cell (PO:0004011) that is part of a root apical meristem (PO:0020147) and by division gives rise to two cells, one of which remains in the meristem, while the other is produces different root cells.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula inicial de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: root cortex-endodermis initial cell
annotations.definition: A root initial cell (PO:0000059) that produces root cortex (PO:0000258) and root endodermis (PO:0005059). In direct contact with quiescent center (PO:0020149).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula inicial del cortex/endodermis de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)
根皮層内皮始原細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: lateral root cap epidermal initial cell
annotations.definition: A root initial cell (PO:0000059) that produces lateral root cap cells and root epidermis (PO:0006036).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula inicial del caliptra de la ra&#237z lateral (Spanish, exact)
側根冠表皮始原細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: stomatal initial cell
annotations.definition: An plant epidermal initial cell (PO:0000349) that undergoes several rounds of uneven division, each time giving rise to a larger daughter cell that differentiates into an epidermal ground cell and a smaller daughter cell that remains meristematic, until the meristematic cell (PO:0004010) differentiates into a guard mother cell (PO:0000351).

annotations.label: leaf procambial cell
annotations.definition: A procambial cell (PO:0025258) that is part of a leaf procambium (PO:0025259).
annotations.comment: Leaf procambial cells may be derived from the shoot apical meristem or can arise throughout the mesophyll of a developing leaf.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula procambial de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: xylem pole pericycle cell
annotations.definition: A pericycle cell that is adjacent to the protoxylem of a vascular bundle.
annotations.comment: Xylem pole pericycle cells are cells in the pericycle that are adjacent to the protoxylem pole of a vascular bundle. Pericycle cells that are adjacent to other xylem cells in the vascular bundle are not considered xylem pole pericycle cells. Xylem pole pericycle cells may retain some meristematic activity and are the site of lateral root initiation.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula del polo xilem&#225tico del periciclo (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: phloem pole pericycle cell
annotations.definition: A pericycle cell that is adjacent to the protophloem of a vascular bundle.
annotations.comment: Phloem pole pericycle cells are cells in the pericycle that are adjacent to the protophloem pole of a vascular bundle. Pericycle cells that are adjacent to other phloem cells in the vascular bundle are not considered phloem pole pericycle cells. Phloem pole pericycle cells are distinct from xylem pole pericycle cells in that they display cytoplasmic characteristics of a more differentiated status.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula del polo floem&#225tico del periciclo (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: epithem cell
annotations.definition: A parenchyma cell of the mesophyll of a hydathode found between the xylem endings and the epidermis.
annotations.comment: Proposed to be involved in the retrieval of solutes from the xylem sap.

annotations.label: proteoid root
annotations.definition: A root (PO:0009005) that forms one or more clusters of closely spaced lateral roots (PO:0020121) of limited growth along its length.
annotations.comment: The terms proteoid root, cluster root, and root cluster have all been used in the literature to describe either the proteoid root axis or clusters of rootlets, and caution should be taken when comparing studies because the definitions of these terms vary (Watts and Evans, 1999; Plant Physiology).

annotations.label: companion cell
annotations.definition: A parenchyma cell (PO:0000074) that is adjacent to a sieve tube element (PO:0000289) and arises from the same phloem mother cell (PO:0000400) as the sieve tube element.
annotations.comment: Companion cells are connected to sieve tube elements through plasmodesmata and function in phloem loading of small molecules, including photosynthate (either sucrose or raffinose-family oligosaccharides). May participate in phloem sucrose loading (GO:0009915). Molecules can be loaded into the companion cells from surrounding cells through either symplastic (via plasmodesmata) or apoplastic (transmembrane) transport. Unlike sieve tube elements, companion cells retain their nuclei and other organelles at maturity. A sieve tube element may have one or more or no companion cells associated with it. This term should not be used for other types of parenchyma cells (PO:0000074) that do not arise from the same phloem mother cell as a sieve tube element. Companion cells are found in angiosperms, as compared to albuminous cells (PO:0025412), which are found in non-angiosperm vascular plants. Companion cells may be specialized as transfer companion cells (PO:0025458), ordinary companion cells (PO:0025459), or intermediary companion cells (PO:0025460). Multiple types of companion cells may be found in the same plant.

annotations.label: parenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A relatively unspecialized ground tissue cell with a nucleate protoplast.

annotations.label: collenchyma cell
annotations.definition: An elongated plant cell with unevenly thickened non-lignified primary walls that is alive at maturity.

synonyms.text: 厚角組織 細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: chlorenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A parenchyma cell containing chloroplasts; a component of leaf mesophyll and other green parenchyma tissue.

synonyms.text: 同化組織、葉緑組織 細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: sclerenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A ground tissue cell (PO:0025030) with a lignified secondary cell wall.
annotations.comment: May be dead at maturity. The secondary cell wall of a sclerenchyma cell is often thicker than other cell types in the same plant structure, but it is not always easy to distinguish from tracheary elements (PO:0000290) or sclerified parenchyma cells (PO:0000074). Compare with collenchyma cells (PO:0000075) that are always alive at maturity, have irregularly thickened primary cell walls, and do not have lignified secondary cell walls. Collenchyma cells provides flexibility while sclerenchyma cells provide hardness. Sclerenchyma cells include plant fiber cells (PO:0025407) and sclerid cells (PO:0025418).

synonyms.text: 厚膜組織 細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: transfer cell
annotations.definition: A cell with wall ingrowths (or invaginations) that increase the surface of the plasmalemma.
annotations.comment: Appear to be specialized for short-distance transfer of solutes. Transfer cells can occur anywhere there is a junction between two individuals, such as between a sporophyte and a gametophyte, or between a parasitic plant and its host.

annotations.label: fusiform initial cell
annotations.definition: An elongated cambial initial cell (PO:0000295) with approximately wedge-shaped ends that is part of the vascular cambium (PO:0005598) and gives rise to axial cells.

annotations.label: ray initial cell
annotations.definition: A cambial initial cell (PO:0000295) that gives rise to ray cells of the secondary xylem (PO:0005848) and secondary phloem (PO:0005043).

synonyms.text: 放射組織始原細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: plant sperm cell
annotations.definition: A gamete (PO:0025006) produced by an antheridium (PO:0025125) or a microgametophyte (PO:0025280).
annotations.comment: The sperm cell is usually smaller than the egg cell. Male gametes may be defined as the motile gamete, rather than the smaller gamete. Plant sperm cells lack a cell wall.

annotations.label: shoot axis epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot system epidermis (PO:0006035) that is part of a shoot axis (PO:0025029).
annotations.comment: Can occur on a stem or branch.

synonyms.text: epidermis del eje del tallo (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: socket cell
annotations.definition: A shoot epidermal cell (PO:0025165) that surrounds a trichome (PO:0000282) and provides its support.

synonyms.text: cell located at the base of the trichome (related)

annotations.label: abscission zone
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that has as part a separation layer (PO:0006211) and a protective layer (PO:0006212), and is involved in the abscission (GO:0009838) of a fruit (PO:0009001), flower (PO:0009046), leaf (PO:0025034) or other plant structure (PO:0009011).

annotations.label: synergid
annotations.definition: Cell, usually two in number, in the micropylar end of the embryo sac of angiosperms associated with the egg in the egg apparatus.

annotations.label: free nuclear endosperm
annotations.definition: A portion of endosperm (PO:0009089) tissue in which at least the first karyokinesis is not followed by cytokinesis.

annotations.label: cellular endosperm
annotations.definition: A portion of endosperm (PO:0009089) in which the first karyokinesis event is accompanied by cytokinesis.

annotations.label: micropylar endosperm
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the region of the endosperm closest to the micropyle of the seed.

annotations.label: chalazal cyst
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the part of the endosperm (PO:0009089) nearest the chalazal region of the plant ovule (PO:0020003) and forming a cyst-like structure.
annotations.comment: Not in current literature. More like an attribute.

annotations.label: nectary epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot system epidermis (PO:0006035) that is part of a nectary (PO:0009035) .

annotations.label: shoot axis cortex
annotations.definition: A portion of cortex (PO:0005708) that is part of a shoot axis (PO:0025029).
annotations.comment: Can occur in a stem or branch.

synonyms.text: cortex del eje del epiblasto (epiblastema) del tallo (Spanish, exact)
portion of shoot axis cortex tissue (exact)

annotations.label: central zone
annotations.definition: An area of densely packed cells in the shoot apex that divide infrequently.
annotations.comment: Functions as a population of stem cells that replenishes the meristem.

annotations.label: peripheral zone
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) which is the organogenic region of the meristem, characterized by higher rates of cell division.

annotations.label: rib zone
annotations.definition: The region of the shoot apical meristem that lies below the central zone and is flanked by the peripheral zone.

annotations.label: flower meristem
annotations.definition: A portion of reproductive shoot apical meristem tissue (PO:0008028) that gives rise to the floral organs (PO:0025395).
annotations.comment: In Zea mays and other grasses, the floret meristem is part of a spikelet and develops into a specific type of floret. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the spikelet type that the meristem is part of. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet (PO:0009051), ear spikelet (PO:0006320), ear pedicellate spikelet (PO:0006348), ear sessile spikelet (PO:0006349), tassel spikelet (PO:0006309), tassel pedicellate spikelet (PO:0006312), tassel sessile spikelet (PO:0006311).

annotations.label: inflorescence meristem
annotations.definition: A meristem that gives rise to an inflorescence.

annotations.label: axillary bud meristem
annotations.definition: A shoot system meristem (PO:0006079) formed in an axil (PO:0025224).

annotations.label: functional megaspore
annotations.definition: A megaspore(s) that forms an embryo sac.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), ear floret (PO:0006354), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352).

annotations.label: degenerate megaspore
annotations.definition: In monosporic and bisporic megasporogenesis: the megaspore(s) that would not participate in megagametogenesis.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), ear floret (PO:0006354), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352).

synonyms.text: 退化大胞子、胞子形成に寄与しない大胞子 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: endodermis
annotations.definition: A portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059) that is the inner most layer of a cortex (PO:0005708) and has a casparian strip in its anticlinal cell walls.
annotations.comment: Forms a sheath around the vascular system. It may have secondary walls later, part_of cortex.

annotations.label: root hair cell
annotations.definition: A root epidermal cell (PO:0025164) that develops from a trichoblast (PO:0000262) and has as part a root hair (GO:0035618).
annotations.comment: Compare with non-hair root epidermal cell (PO:0000263). This term is for an entire epidermal cell that contains a root hair extension. To describe only the cell extension, see root hair (GO:0035618) or root hair tip (GO:0035619).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula del pelo de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: root cortex
annotations.definition: A portion of cortex (PO:0005708) that is part of a root (PO:0009005).
annotations.comment: In dicots and gymnosperms, the cortex consists of mainly parenchyma cells. In monocots sclerenchyma develops in addition to parenchyma.

annotations.label: trichoblast
annotations.definition: A root epidermal cell (PO:0025164) that is the smaller cell produced by the asymmetric division of an epidermal intial cell (PO:0000349) and gives rise to a root hair (GO:0035618).

synonyms.text: 根毛形成細胞、トリコブラスト (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: non-hair root epidermal cell
annotations.definition: A root epidermal cell (PO:0025164) that develops from an epidermal initial (PO:0000349) and does not have as part a root hair (GO:0035618).
annotations.comment: Compare to root hair cell (PO:0000256), which develops from a trichoblast (PO:0000262).

synonyms.text: 非毛根形成細胞、アトリコブラスト (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of primary xylem (PO:0000272) tissue that has as part one or more protoxylem tracheary elements (PO:0025575) embedded in parenchyma (PO:0005421) tissue.
annotations.comment: Develops while the vascular tissue (PO:0009015) is still elongating. When protoxylem tracheary elements (PO:0025575) are destroyed they may become obliterated by surrounding parenchyma cells (PO:0000074). Protoxylem has smaller elements than the metaxylem (PO:0000372).

annotations.label: xylem fiber cell
annotations.definition: A fiber cell (PO:0025407) that is part of a portion of xylem (PO:0005352) tissue.
annotations.comment: It may be difficult to distinguish xylem fiber cells, especially fiber tracheids (PO:0000355), from tracheary elements (PO:0000290). Fiber tracheids have bordered pits with smaller pit cavities than the vessel members (PO:0002003) or tracheids (PO:0000301) of the same wood and a distinct pit cavity leading to the cell lumen through the cell wall.

annotations.label: trichome
annotations.definition: A unicellular or multicellular plant structure (PO:0009011) that forms a non-sclerified outgrowth from the epidermis (PO:0005679).
annotations.comment: This class is the union of unicellular trichome (PO:0025537) and multicellular trichome (PO:0025162). A trichome can vary in length and shape and may be branched or unbranched.  It may be glandular or non-secretory.

annotations.label: idioblast
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that markedly differs in form, size, or contents from other cells in the same tissue.
annotations.comment: Often contains deposit of minerals or products of secondary metabolism. May have a specialized function.

annotations.label: subsidiary cell
annotations.definition: A shoot epidermal cell (PO:0025165) associated with a stomatal pore (PO:0008032) and at least morphologically distinguishable from the other epidermal cells.

synonyms.text: 助細胞,副細胞（気孔）(auxiliary cell, synergid) (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: sieve tube element
annotations.definition: A sieve element (PO:0025406) that is part of a sieve tube (PO:0025416) and has as parts sieve areas aggregated into sieve plates (GO:0097218).
annotations.comment: Sieve tube elements have sieve areas that are specialized into those with wide pores in sieve plates and those with narrow pores in lateral sieve areas. Sieve tube elements may have inclined end walls with compound or simple sieve plates. Typical of angiosperms. Differ from sieve cells (PO:0025415), which have less pronounced specialization of sieve areas and no sieve plates.

annotations.label: tracheary element
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that has a lignified cell wall with secondary thickening and bordered pits.
annotations.comment: Tracheary elements are dead at maturity, are more or less elongated, and have pits of various types in the cell wall. Tracheary elements function in the conduction of water and dissolved substances such as mineral nutrients from the roots to the shoot system. Tracheary elements can be distinguished from fiber cells (PO:0025407) by the presence of bordered pits, although there are intermediate forms called fiber tracheids (PO:0000355).

annotations.label: guard cell
annotations.definition: A shoot epidermal cell (PO:0025165) that is one of a pair of cells flanking the stomatal pore (PO:0008032).
annotations.comment: They cause the opening and closing of the pore by changes in turgor.

annotations.label: cambial initial cell
annotations.definition: An initial cell (PO:0004011) that is part of the vascular cambium (PO:0005598) or cork cambium (PO:0005599), and by periclinal divisions, produces cells to the outside or inside of the cambial axis.

annotations.label: tracheid
annotations.definition: A tracheary element (PO:0000290) that has no perforations in its cell wall.
annotations.comment: May have any of the kinds of secondary wall thickenings found in tracheary elements. The area of contact between two tracheids has only bordered pits, whereas in vessel members (PO:0002003), it has openings in the cell wall called perforations, organized into perforation plates (term has been requested in GO).

annotations.label: nectary parenchyma
annotations.definition: A portion of parenchyma tissue (PO:0005421) that is part of a nectary (PO:0009035).

annotations.label: shoot epidermal pavement cell
annotations.definition: A shoot epidermal cell (PO:0025165) that is relatively unspecialized at maturity.
annotations.comment: May have sinuous anticlinal cell walls that overall give a jigsaw like appearance to the epidermis. Pavement cells are epidermal cells that are not directly associated with a stomatal complex or trichome at maturity, but they share similar developmental origins.

annotations.label: plant epidermal initial cell
annotations.definition: An initial cell (PO:0004011) that is part of an plant epidermis (PO:0005679) and gives rise to specialized cells.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula inicial de la epidermis (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: guard mother cell
annotations.definition: A shoot epidermal cell (PO:0025165) that divides to produce the guard cells (PO:0000293).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula madre de la c&#233lula guardiana (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: myrosin cell
annotations.definition: A cell containing glucosinolates ("mustard oil glucosides") and myrosinases, enzymes hydrolyzing the glucosinolates.

annotations.label: passage cell
annotations.definition: A ground tissue cell that is part of an exodermis or an endodermis and remains thin walled when the associated cells develop thick secondary walls.

annotations.label: fiber tracheid
annotations.definition: A xylem fiber cell (PO:0000274) with bordered pits with pit cavities.
annotations.comment: Fiber tracheids generally have pointed ends and bordered pits that have lenticular to slit-like apertures. If both libriform fiber cells (PO:0004520) and fiber tracheids are present, the libriform fiber cells usually have thicker walls. Fiber tracheids have bordered pits with smaller pit cavities than the vessel members (PO:0002003) or tracheids (PO:0000301) of the same wood and a distinct pit cavity leading to the cell lumen through the cell wall.

annotations.label: metaxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of primary xylem (PO:0005849) tissue that has as part one or more metaxylem tracheary elements (PO:0025576) embedded in parenchyma (PO:0005421) tissue and may contain xylem fiber cells (PO:0000274).
annotations.comment: Metaxylem has larger tracheary elements (PO:0000290) than the protoxylem (PO:0000272), and develops after the formation of the protoxylem, once elongation of the plant axis (PO:0025004) has completed.

annotations.label: phloem mother cell
annotations.definition: A cambial initial cell (PO:0000295) which is destined to differentiate into secondary phloem.
annotations.comment: Phloem islands can be formed in the middle of the xylem.

annotations.label: plant zygote
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) which is a whole plant (PO:0000003) in the plant zygote stage (PO:0001097).
annotations.comment: A plant zygote is a whole plant that is a single cell formed by fertilization (syngamy).

synonyms.text: 植物接合子、接合体 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: megasporocyte
annotations.definition: A sporocyte (PO:0006204) that produces four haploid (1n) megaspores (PO:0020019).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), ear floret (PO:0006354), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352).

annotations.label: fruit development stage
annotations.definition: A multi-tissue plant structure development stage (PO:0025571) that has as primary participant a single fruit (PO:0009001).
annotations.comment: Defined by characteristic morphological, structural, histological or other visible features of a fruit. Includes as subclasses fruit formation stage (PO:0025501) and fruit ripening stage (PO:0025502). May have as part a seed development stage (PO:0001170). This stage applies to an individual fruit. For development stages of a whole plant (PO:0000003) based on fruit development, use whole plant fruit development stage (PO:0025500) or its subclasses.

annotations.label: vascular leaf anlagen formation stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf initiation stage (PO:0007040) during which the vascular leaf founder cells (PO:0025592) of the shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) form a vascular leaf anlagen (PO:0025431).
annotations.comment: The initial stage of leaf development when cells in the shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) are already determined to form a vascular leaf primordium (PO:0000017), but the primordium is not yet morphologically distinguishable.

synonyms.text: vascular leaf founder cell formation stage (exact)

annotations.label: anther development stage
annotations.definition: Stages of development of the anther defined by characteristic morphological, cytological, histological or other visible features.

annotations.label: A archesporial cells visible stage
annotations.definition: An initial stage during which archesporial cells arise in four corners of L2 layer. Change in shape of primordia to more oval.

annotations.label: B enlarged microsporocyte stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which the microsporocytes become conspicuous.

annotations.label: pollen development stage
annotations.definition: A microgametophyte development stage (PO:0025602) during which the pollen (PO:0025281) develops.

annotations.label: C callose wall formation in pollen mother cells stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which diploid pollen mother cells become separated from each other and from tapetum by a callose wall.

annotations.label: D pollen mother cell meiosis stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which pollen mother cells undergo meiosis to form tetrads of microspores.

annotations.label: F microspore release stage
annotations.definition: Stage of microgametophyte development during which the callose wall between the tetrads breaks down and haploid microspores are released and lie freely in locules.

annotations.label: A microsporogenous mass stage
annotations.definition: An initial stage during which the archesporial cells divide to give rise to primary parietal and sporogenous cells.

annotations.label: G early unicellular microspore stage
annotations.definition: Stage of microgametophyte development during which the microspores round up and small vacuoles appear in their cytoplasm. The microspore walls thicken due to the formation of the exine.

annotations.label: H late unicellular microspore stage
annotations.definition: Stage of microgametophyte development during which a large vacuole is formed in each microspore causing a rapid increase in size and a displacement of the nucleus to one side.

annotations.label: I first mitotic division stage
annotations.definition: Stage of microgametophyte development during which the asymmetric, first mitotic division of the microspores occurs producing a large vegetative cell and small generative cell, located near pollen wall.

annotations.label: K second mitotic division stage
annotations.definition: A pollen development stage (PO:0001007) during which the generative cell (PO:0020097) undergoes mitotic division to form two male gametes, the pollen sperm cells (PO:0025121).
annotations.comment: In taxa with mature two-celled pollen (PO:0025281), the second mitotic division takes place after pollen germination (GO:0009846), in the germinating pollen tube cell (PO:0025195). Note: This term overlaps with the GO BP term: generative cell mitosis (GO:0055047): The process in which the generative cell divides by mitosis to form two haploid cells. These will subsequently differentiate into sperm cells.

annotations.label: L mature pollen stage
annotations.definition: Stage of microgametophyte development during which the pollen reaches mature stage and desiccates.
annotations.comment: Mature pollen stages is reached at three-celled pollen in some angiosperms (maize, rice and Arabidopsis), while in others, the maturepollen has two haploid cells (tobacco, snapdragon).

annotations.label: M germinated pollen stage
annotations.definition: The final stage in the life cycle of the male gametophyte. Upon rehydration, mature pollen grain forms a tubular outgrowth, the pollen tube, which carries the male gametes.

annotations.label: B formation of primary parietal and sporogenous cells stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which the primary parietal and primary sporogenous layers are derived from archesporial cells.

annotations.label: vascular leaf primordium formation stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf initiation stage (PO:0001051) during which a vascular leaf primordium (PO:0000017) forms in the peripheral zone (PO:0000225) of the vegetative shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148).
annotations.comment: The vascular leaf primordium (PO:0000017) develops from the vascular leaf anlagen (PO:0025431).

annotations.label: hood-shaped primordium stage
annotations.definition: Stage of leaf development when a hood-shaped primordium is formed, partially enclosing P1 and SAM, as in Poaceae.

annotations.label: formation of ligule primordium stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf expansion stage (PO:0001052) when conical P3 leaf completely encloses SAM, and the ligule (PO:0020105) primordium is visible.

annotations.label: rapid elongation of vascular leaf lamina stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf expansion stage (PO:0001052) during which a rapid elongation of the leaf lamina (PO:0020039) occurs.

annotations.label: rapid elongation of leaf sheath stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf expansion stage (PO:0001052) during which a rapid elongation of the leaf sheath (PO:0020104) occurs.
annotations.comment: Occurs in the Poaceae.

annotations.label: C four anther lobes formed stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which the four-lobed anther pattern with two developing stomium regions (notch) are formed.

annotations.label: D locules established stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which the four clearly defined locules are established. All anther cell types present and microspore mother cells appear.

annotations.label: E tetrad stage
annotations.definition: Stage immediately following meiosis, during which the tetrads of microspores are still hold together, enclosed by the callose wall.

annotations.label: 4 root elongation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which a root is elongating.
annotations.comment: In some plant species this stage is subdivided into distal and proximal elongating zones.

annotations.label: E anther wall tapetum degeneration initiated stage
annotations.definition: An anther development stage (PO:0001004) during which degeneration of the anther wall tapetum (PO:0009071) is initiated, as the deposition on the surface of the microspore (PO:0020048) primexine proceeds.
annotations.comment: Microspores within single locule are at unicellular stage.

annotations.label: F bilocular anther stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which anther becomes bilocular, after degeneration and breakage of septum below stomium.

annotations.label: G anther dehiscence stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which the dehiscence of anthers occurs. Anther breaks along stomium and pollen is released.

annotations.label: H anther senescence stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which the senescence of anther and stamen occurs. Shrinkage of cells and anther structure takes place.

annotations.label: dry seed stage
annotations.definition: A seed development stage (PO:0001170) during which the mature seed reaches a low moisture content.

annotations.label: chalazal and micropylar domain establishment stage
annotations.definition: The initial stage of endosperm development during which the chalazal and micropilar domains are established.

annotations.label: functional specialization of the endosperm stage
annotations.definition: Stage of endosperm development during which the functional specialization of the maturing endosperm occurs.

annotations.label: sepal differentiation and expansion stage
annotations.definition: Stage of calyx development defined by differentiation and expansion of sepal.

annotations.label: fully expanded sepal stage
annotations.definition: Stage of calyx development when sepal is fully expanded.

annotations.label: lemma development stage
annotations.definition: A phyllome development stage (PO:0025579) that has as a primary participant a lemma (PO:0009037).

annotations.label: palea development stage
annotations.definition: A phyllome development stage (PO:0025579) that has as a primary participant a palea (PO:0009038).

annotations.label: lodicule development stage
annotations.definition: A phyllome development stage (PO:0025579) that has as a primary participant a lodicule (PO:0009036).

annotations.label: leaf development stage
annotations.definition: A phyllome development stage (PO:0025579) during which the development of vascular leaves (PO:0009025) and non-vascular leaves (PO:0025075) occurs.

annotations.label: vascular leaf initiation stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf development stage (PO:0025570) which begins with the onset of the vascular leaf anlagen formation stage (PO:0001003) and ends with the termination of the vascular leaf primordium polarity determination stage (PO:0001052).
annotations.comment: Localized cell divisions in the peripheral zone (PO:0000225) transition from anticlinal to periclinal divisions.

annotations.label: vascular leaf expansion stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf development stage (PO:0025570) during which vascular leaf size increases primarily through cell expansion.
annotations.comment: During this stage leaf shaping (GO:0010358) occurs which is defined as the developmental process that pertains to the organization of a leaf in three-dimensional space once the structure has initially formed.

annotations.label: vascular leaf post-expansion stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf development stage (PO:0025570) that begins when the vascular leaf (PO:0009025) is fully expanded and ends with the onset of the vascular leaf senescence stage (PO:0001054).

synonyms.text: fully expanded leaf stage in Arabidopsis (related)

annotations.label: vascular leaf senescent stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf development stage (PO:0025570) that begins with the formation of a abscission zone at the base of a vascular leaf (PO:0009025) and ends with leaf separation and death.

annotations.label: lemma primordia visible stage
annotations.definition: Stage of lemma development that begins when the lemma primordia arise.
annotations.comment: The lemma primordium forms in 1/2 alternate phylotaxy in rice spikelet.

annotations.label: lemma differentiation and expansion stage
annotations.definition: Stage of lemma development defined by differentiation and expansion of lemma.

annotations.label: fully expanded lemma stage
annotations.definition: Stage of lemma development when lemma is fully expanded.

annotations.label: palea primordia visible stage
annotations.definition: Stage of palea development that begins when the palea primordia arise.
annotations.comment: The palea primordium forms in 1/2 alternate phylotaxy in rice spikelet.

annotations.label: J bicellular pollen stage
annotations.definition: Stage of microgametophyte development during which the small generative cell moves inward, becoming completely surrounded by the cytoplasm of the vegetative cell.

annotations.label: palea differentiation and expansion stage
annotations.definition: Stage of palea development defined by differentiation and expansion of palea.

annotations.label: fully expanded palea stage
annotations.definition: Stage of palea development when palea is fully expanded.

annotations.label: lodicule primordia visible stage
annotations.definition: Stage of lodicule development that begins when the lodicule primordia arise.
annotations.comment: Two lodicules form on the side of the lemma in rice spikelet.

annotations.label: N second mitotic division stage in germinating pollen
annotations.definition: Stage of microgametophyte development during which the generative cell undergoes a mitotic division to form two male gametes, the sperm cells.

annotations.label: lodicule differentiation and expansion stage
annotations.definition: Stage of lodicule development defined by differentiation and expansion of lodicule.

annotations.label: plant embryo cotyledonary stage
annotations.definition: A plant embryo development stage (PO:0007631) that occurs during the interval between the beginning of rapid elongation of the plant embryo axis (PO:0019018) and plant embryo cotyledons (PO:0025470), and the onset of a plant embryo true leaf formation stage (PO:0001095), when present, or a mature plant embryo stage (PO:0001081).
annotations.comment: During this stage, the plant embryo (PO:0009009) continues to undergo a process of developmental growth (as a result of both cell division (GO:0051301) and cell expansion (GO:0016049), which ends with the onset of a mature plant embryo stage (PO:0001081). For species that develop true leaves in the plant embryo, (vascular leaves (PO:0009025) that develop after the cotyledons (PO:0020030) or coleoptile (PO:0020033)), growth may continue in the plant embryo true leaf formation stage (PO:0001095). For other species, the plant embryo transitions directly from a plant embryo cotyledonary stage (PO:0001078) to a mature plant embryo stage (PO:0001081).

annotations.label: fully expanded lodicule stage
annotations.definition: Stage of lodicule development when lodicule is fully expanded.

annotations.label: mature plant embryo stage
annotations.definition: A plant embryo development stage (PO:0007631) during the interval between when a plant embryo (PO:0009009) has stopped developmental growth involved in morphogenesis (GO:0060560) and one of the following: the beginning of the seed germination stage (PO:0007057) in seed plants, formation of the first vascular leaf (PO:0009025) in pteridophytes, the beginning of development of a sporangium (PO:0025094) in bryophytes, or the beginning of the formation of a plant organ (PO:0009008) such as a root (PO:0009005), shoot axis (PO:0025029), or vascular leaf (PO:0009025) in a cultured plant embryo (PO:0000010).
annotations.comment: In some seed plants, this occurs some time after the fruit ripening stage (PO:0025502) and after the dispersal of the seed (PO:0009010). This stage may be very short in plants that do not enter a plant embryo dormant stage (PO:0025377).

annotations.label: inflorescence development stage
annotations.definition: A reproductive shoot system development stage (PO:0025530) that has as primary participant a inflorescence (PO:0009049).

annotations.label: plant embryo coleoptilar stage
annotations.definition: A plant embryo development stage (PO:0007631) that occurs during the interval between the beginning of elongation of the plant embryo axis (PO:0019018), with the formation of a plant embryo coleoptile (PO:0025286), a plant embryo shoot apical meristem (PO:0006362) and root apical meristem (PO:0020147), and ends with the onset of a plant embryo true leaf formation stage (PO:0001095), when present, or a mature plant embryo stage (PO:0001081).
annotations.comment: This stage occurs in grasses and other monocots.

annotations.label: plant embryo true leaf formation stage
annotations.definition: A plant embryo development stage (PO:0007631) that occurs during the interval between the beginning of the formation of the first vascular leaf primordium (PO:0000017) in the plant embryo proper (PO:0000001), and the onset of a mature plant embryo stage (PO:0001081).
annotations.comment: A true leaf is vascular leaf (PO:0009025) that forms after the formation of the cotyledons (PO:0020030) or a coleoptile (PO:0020033). Not all species form true leaves in the plant embryo development stage (PO:0007631). If it occurs, a plant embryo true leaf formation stage is preceded by either a plant embryo cotyledonary stage (PO:0001078) or a plant embryo coleoptilar stage (PO:0001094), depending upon the species.

annotations.label: primary endosperm cell stage
annotations.definition: One cell stage that takes place following the fertilization, produced by the fusion of sperm cell nucleus and one or more polar nuclei.

annotations.label: plant zygote stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte development stage (PO:0007134) that occurs during the interval between fertilization and the first cell division of the plant zygote (PO:0000423).
annotations.comment: Precedes the sporophyte vegetative stage (PO:0007134). Refers to the GO biological process term fertilization (GO:0009566).

annotations.label: formation of aleurone and starchy layers stage
annotations.definition: Differentiation of aleurone layer, the outermost endosperm tissue, and the internal starchy tissue.

annotations.label: seed development stage
annotations.definition: A multi-tissue plant structure development stage (PO:0025571) that has as primary participant a seed (PO:0009010).
annotations.comment: Includes the stages of seed development, from fertilization to the dry or quiescent state. May have as part an embryo development stage.

annotations.label: plant proembryo stage
annotations.definition: A plant embryo development stage (PO:0007631) that occurs during the interval between the first cell division of a plant zygote (PO:0000423) and the onset of the plant globular embryo stage (PO:0001185).
annotations.comment: In the case of a nucellar (adventitious) plant embryo (PO:0004537), somatic plant embryo (PO:0025302), microspore-derived cultured plant embryo (PO:0025305), or other embryos that arise through apogamy, the plant proembryo stage (PO:0001180) begins after the division of a single cell that is not a zygote. The plant proembryo stage occurs prior to the differentiation of the three main tissue systems (vascular system (PO:0000034) epidermis (PO:0005679); and the portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059)).

annotations.label: plant embryo globular stage
annotations.definition: A plant embryo development stage (PO:0007631) that occurs during the interval between when a plant embryo proper (PO:0000001) increases in diameter through cell division, but retains radial symmetry (with the concomitant formation of a protoderm (PO:0006210)), and the onset of a plant embryo bilateral stage (PO:0004507).
annotations.comment: During the plant embryo globular stage, differentiation of the three main tissue systems (vascular system (PO:0000034); epidermis (PO:0005679); and the portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059)) occurs.

annotations.label: sepals enclosing meristem stage
annotations.definition: Stage of calyx development that begins when the developing sepals enclose the floral meristem.

annotations.label: sepals enclosing flower bud stage
annotations.definition: Stage of calyx development that begins when the sepals enclose the floral bud.

annotations.label: stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot system epidermis (PO:0006035) that has as parts two guard cells (PO:0000293) and a stomatal pore (PO:0008032). May also contain subsidiary cells (PO:0000284).

annotations.label: embryo basal cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that is the lower-most cell formed after the first division of the plant zygote (PO:0000423) and is part of a plant embryo (PO:0009009).
annotations.comment: The zygote divides to form an embryo apical cell and an embryo basal cell.

annotations.label: xylem vessel member
annotations.definition: A tracheary element (PO:0000290) that is part of a xylem vessel (PO:0025417) and has as parts perforation plates.
annotations.comment: Commonly thick walled, with pointed ends and bordered pits that have lenticular to slit-like apertures. If both libriform fibers (PO:0004520) and fiber tracheids are present, the libriform fibers usually have thicker walls. Fiber tracheids have bordered pits with smaller pit cavities than the vessel members (PO:0002003) or tracheids (PO:0000301) of the same wood and a distinct pit cavity leading from the pit cavity to the cell lumen through the thick cell wall.

synonyms.text: 木質部道管状要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: silk scar
annotations.definition: A cardinal part of multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025498) which is composed of the shriveled remains of the silk (PO:0006488), and the base of the style (PO:0009074), and is located on the distal end of a developing or mature kernel (caryopsis fruit; PO:0030104).

annotations.label: transition zone
annotations.definition: A region of the root between the meristem and the region of rapid elongation in which isodiametric growth (cell expansion) occurs.

synonyms.text: 移行領域、移行帯、移行域 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: shoot-borne nodal root
annotations.definition: A shoot-borne root (PO:0000042) that forms at a shoot axis node (PO:0005004).

annotations.label: root vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a root (PO:0009005).
annotations.comment: This term was brought back from OBSOLETE node (original reason for OBSOLETE: synonym of term stele). Since stele encompasses both vascular system and the pith, in roots with pith, vascular system and stele are not synonymous.

synonyms.text: sistema vascular de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: primary root differentiation zone
annotations.definition: A root differentiation zone that is part of a primary root.

synonyms.text: zona de diferenciaci&#243n de la ra&#237z primary (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: lateral root differentiation zone
annotations.definition: A root differentiation zone that is part of a lateral root.

synonyms.text: zona de diferenciaci&#243n de la ra&#237z lateral (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: root cap of primary root
annotations.definition: A root cap that is part of a primary root tip.

synonyms.text: caliptra de la ra&#237z primary (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: root cap of lateral root
annotations.definition: A root cap that is part of a lateral root tip.

synonyms.text: caliptra de la ra&#237z lateral (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: central root cap of lateral root
annotations.definition: A central root cap that is part of a root cap of lateral root.

synonyms.text: caliptra de la ra&#237z central de la ra&#237z lateral (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: lateral root cap of lateral root
annotations.definition: A lateral root cap that is part of a root cap of lateral root.

synonyms.text: caliptra de la ra&#237z lateral de la ra&#237z lateral (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: central root cap of primary root
annotations.definition: A central root cap that is part of a root cap of primary root.

synonyms.text: caliptra de la ra&#237z central de la ra&#237z primaria (Spanish, exact)
一次根の中心根冠 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: lateral root cap of primary root
annotations.definition: A lateral root cap (PO:0020131) that is part of a root cap (PO:0020123) of a primary root (PO:0020127).

synonyms.text: caliptra de la ra&#237z lateral de la ra&#237z primaria (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: root nodule
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is an outgrowth of a root (PO:0009005) and is inhabited by nitrogen-fixing bacteria.
annotations.comment: Root nodules are a symbiosis between a plant (often, but not exclusively, of the Fabaceae) and bacteria of the genus Rhizobium, Bradyrhizobium, or Azorhizobium.

annotations.label: stolon
annotations.definition: A branch (PO:0025073) that is prostrate or trailing, and produces  adventitious nodal roots (PO:0003005) and sometimes erect shoots.
annotations.comment: The stolon develops from an axillary bud (PO:0004709) and produces adventitious roots (PO:0003005) at the nodes and a terminal aerial stem. That stem then sends out one or more stolons from its axillary buds and the process is reiterated. Stolons may be above ground, as with strawberries, or below ground as in potatoes.

synonyms.text: ストロン、ほふく茎 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: bulliform cell
annotations.definition: An enlarged more or less thin-walled leaf pavement cell, present, with other similar cells, in longitudinal rows in leaves of monocots.
annotations.comment: Also called motor cell because of its presumed participation in the mechanism of rolling and unrolling of leaves. If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

annotations.label: phellem
annotations.definition: A portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059) centrifugally derived from cork cambium (PO:0005599), non-living at maturity, and having suberized walls.
annotations.comment: Protective in function because the walls are highly impervious to water.

annotations.label: mesophyll cell
annotations.definition: A chlorenchyma cell that is part of a mesophyll.

annotations.label: silica cell
annotations.definition: Cells with deposits of silica in them. One of the two types of short cells in the epidermis of grasses and bamboos. It is usually paired with epidermal cork cell.
annotations.comment: One of two types of short cells in the epidermis of grasses. If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025).

synonyms.text: ケイ酸細胞、珪酸細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: meristematic cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) synthesizing protoplasm and producing new cells by division and with only a primary cell wall.

annotations.label: initial cell
annotations.definition: A meristematic cell (PO:0004010) that by division gives rise to two cells, one of which remains meristematic, while the other is added to the plant body.
annotations.comment: Initial cells are often part of a portion of tissue that is composed mostly of somewhat older, differentiating cells. The division of an initial cell is often uneven, with the smaller daughter cell remaining meristematic.

annotations.label: plant epidermal cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that is part of a portion of epidermis (PO:0005679).

annotations.label: formation of vascular leaflet primordia stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf expansion stage (PO:0001052) at which a leaflet primordium (PO:0025481) is initiated on the leaf rachis (PO:0020055) of a compound leaf (PO:0020043).

annotations.label: gynoecium differentiation and expansion stage
annotations.definition: Stage of gynoecium development defined by differentiation and expansion of carpels.

annotations.label: closure of carpel(s) stage
annotations.definition: Stage of gynoecium development defined by the closure of the carpel(s).
annotations.comment: Closure of the carpel can occur in various ways, by fusion or secretion.

annotations.label: stigma cell differentiation stage
annotations.definition: Epidermal cells at the top of developing style differentiate stigma.

annotations.label: ovule differentiation and expansion stage
annotations.definition: Stage of ovule development defined by differentiation and expansion of ovules.

annotations.label: fertilized ovule stage
annotations.definition: The earliest stage of seed development immediately following double fertilization.

annotations.label: developing seed stage
annotations.definition: Stage of seed development characterized by seed growth and differentiation.

annotations.label: plant embryo bilateral stage
annotations.definition: A plant embryo development stage (PO:0007631) that occurs during the interval between the transition from radial to bilateral symmetry in a plant embryo proper (PO:0000001) and the onset of either the plant embryo cotyledonary stage (PO:0001078) or the plant embryo coleoptilar stage (PO:0001094).
annotations.comment: A plant embryo bilateral stage is commonly known as the heart stage in dicotyledonous plants and the transition stage in monocots. Preceded by a plant embryo globular stage (PO:0001185).

annotations.label: glandular trichome
annotations.definition: A trichome (PO:0000282) that secretes or sequesters a portion of plant substance (PO:0025161).

annotations.label: seed trichome
annotations.definition: A trichome that develops from seed coat epidermis.

synonyms.text: 種子毛（毛茸、糸状体、毛状突起体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: heartwood
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) that lacks living secondary xylem parenchyma cells (PO:0004525).
annotations.comment: Heartwood has ceased to function in storage and conduction, and the reserve materials have been removed or converted into heartwood substances. It is generally darker colored than sapwood (PO:0004513) and is found in the center of a shoot axis (PO:0025029) or root (PO:0009005), interior to the sapwood. Heartwood forms at the end of a cell death stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025466).

annotations.label: sapwood
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) that has as parts living secondary xylem parenchyma cells (PO:0004525).
annotations.comment: Sapwood contains reserve materials and may function in the conduction of water and minerals (xylem sap (PO:0025539)). It is generally lighter colored than the heartwood (PO:0004512) and is located between the heartwood and the vascular cambium (PO:0005598) of a shoot axis (PO:0025029) or root (PO:0009005). Sapwood forms during a secondary xylem development stage (PO:0025427).

annotations.label: growth ring
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) that has as parts a single layer of early wood (PO:0004515) and a single layer of late wood (PO:0004516) from the same growth season and is part of a shoot axis (PO:0025029) or root (PO:0009005).
annotations.comment: Appears as a ring in a transverse section of plant axis (PO:0025004). May be an annual ring (one growth ring per year) or a false annual ring (more or less than one growth ring per year).

annotations.label: early wood
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) that is a part of a growth ring (PO:0004514) formed during the early part of a growing season, has lower density than late wood (PO:0004516) of the same growth ring, and has as parts early wood tracheary elements (PO:0025461) with generally larger lumens than the late wood tracheary elements (PO:0025462) of the same growth ring.
annotations.comment: The transition from early wood to late wood may be gradual and is not always easy to distinguish.

annotations.label: late wood
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) that is a part of a growth ring (PO:0004514) formed during the later part of a growing season, has higher density than early wood (PO:0004515) of the same growth ring, and has as parts late wood tracheary elements (PO:0025462) with generally smaller lumens than the early wood tracheary elements (PO:0025461) of the same growth ring.
annotations.comment: The transition from early wood to late wood may be gradual and is not always easy to distinguish.

annotations.label: growth ring boundary
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) that includes the last-formed late wood (PO:0004516) of one growth ring (PO:0004514) and the earliest-formed early wood (PO:0004515) of the next growth ring.
annotations.comment: Used to describe the boundary area between two adjacent growth rings, which may be distinct or indistinct.

synonyms.text: l&#237mite del anillo de crecimiento (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: bark
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) outside the vascular cambium (PO:0005598) or the xylem (PO:0005352); in older trees may be divided into dead outer bark and living inner bark, which consists of secondary phloem (PO:0005043).

annotations.label: phloem fiber cell
annotations.definition: A fiber cell (PO:0025407) that is part of a portion of phloem (PO:0005417) tissue.

annotations.label: libriform fiber cell
annotations.definition: A xylem fiber cell (PO:0000274) with simple pits.
annotations.comment: Libriform fiber cells have simple (unbordered) pits while fiber tracheids (PO:0000355) have bordered pits. Many intermediate forms exist and are generally classified as fiber tracheids (PO:0000355). If both libriform fiber cells and fiber tracheids are present, the libriform fiber cells usually have thicker walls. Libriform fiber cells have a slit-like aperture toward the cell lumen, but no pit cavity.

annotations.label: septate fiber cell
annotations.definition: A plant fiber cell (PO:0025407) with thin transverse walls (septa), which are formed when mitosis occurs within the cell after development of a secondary cell wall (GO:0009531).
annotations.comment: A septate fiber septum is composed of the adjacent primary cell walls of two internal cells that arise from mitosis within the septate fiber cell after it has formed a secondary cell wall (GO:0009531).

annotations.label: outer vascular cambium
annotations.definition: Cambium at the outer margin of the xylem cylinder.

synonyms.text: 外側維管 形成層 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: included vascular cambium
annotations.definition: Cambium occurring within xylem.

annotations.label: included phloem
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary phloem tissue produced by included vascular cambium.

annotations.label: secondary xylem parenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A parenchyma cell (PO:0000074) that is part of a portion of secondary xylem parenchyma (PO:0004532).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula parenquim&#225tica de la madera (Spanish, exact)
二次木部柔組織細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: axial secondary xylem parenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A secondary xylem parenchyma cell (PO:0004525) that is part of a portion of axial secondary xylem parenchyma (PO:0004533).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula parenquim&#225tica axial de la madera (Spanish, exact)
軸方向二次木部柔組織細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: ray secondary xylem parenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A parenchyma cell (PO:0000074) that develops from a ray initial (PO:0000082) and is part of a portion of ray secondary xylem parenchyma (PO:0004534).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula parenquim&#225tica de rayo de la madera (Spanish, exact)
放射状二次木部柔組織細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: upright ray secondary xylem parenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A ray secondary xylem parenchyma cell (PO:0004527) that is oriented with its longest dimension parallel to the plant axis (PO:0025004) in which it is found.

synonyms.text: rayo par&#233nquim&#225tico vertical de la madera (Spanish, exact)
直立放射状二次木部柔組織細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: procumbent ray secondary xylem parenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A ray secondary xylem parenchyma cell (PO:0004527) that is oriented with its longest dimension radial to the plant axis (PO:0025004) in which it is found.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula procumbente del rayo de la madera (Spanish, exact)
平伏放射二次木部柔組織細胞   (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: tile cell
annotations.definition: Empty upright ray cell of approximately the same height as the procumbent ray cell and occurring in indeterminate horizontal series usually interspersed among the procumbent cells.

annotations.label: sheath cell
annotations.definition: Cell on the margin of, and tending to form a sheath around, the procumbent cells of a multi-seriate ray as seen in tangential section.

annotations.label: secondary xylem parenchyma
annotations.definition: A portion of parenchyma (PO:0005421) tissue that is part of a portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) and has as parts secondary xylem parenchyma cells (PO:0004525).

annotations.label: axial secondary xylem parenchyma
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary xylem parenchyma (PO:0004532) that is part of an axial system (PO:0025410) and has as parts axial secondary xylem parenchyma cells (PO:0004526).

synonyms.text: par&#233nquima axial de la madera (Spanish, exact)
軸方向二次木部柔組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: ray secondary xylem parenchyma
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary xylem parenchyma (PO:0004532) that is part of a ray system (PO:0025411) and has as parts ray secondary xylem parenchyma cells (PO:0004527).
annotations.comment: Occurs in bands or rays of variable width and height that extend radially in the secondary xylem (PO:0005848) and secondary phloem (PO:0005043) of a plant axis (PO:0025004).

synonyms.text: par&#233nquima de rayo de la madera (Spanish, exact)
放射状二次木質部柔組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: fruit placenta
annotations.definition: A portion of placenta tissue (PO:0025078) that is part of a fruit (PO:0009001) and to which seeds (PO:0009010) are attached.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: infructescence fruit pedicel
annotations.definition: A fruit pedicel (PO:0030114) that bears a fruit (PO:0009001) in an infructescence (PO:0006342).
annotations.comment: An infructescence fruit pedicel is the ultimate shoot axis (PO:0025029) in an infructescence (PO:0006342).

annotations.label: nucellar plant embryo
annotations.definition: A somatic plant embryo derived directly from nucellus cells.
annotations.comment: Common in Citrus.

synonyms.text: 珠心由来の不定胚 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: indeterminate nodule
annotations.definition: A root nodule characterized by a persistent meristem, presence of vascular transfer cells, and cell division in the inner cortex with non-dividing infected cells and bacteria.
annotations.comment: Nodule is persistent and may continue to grow after a physiological stress.

annotations.label: determinate nodule
annotations.definition: A root nodule characterized by a dividing infected cells and bacteria, vascular transfer cells absent and cell division is in the outer cortex.
annotations.comment: Nodules are not persistent.

annotations.label: fruit columella
annotations.definition: A columella (PO:0025230) that forms the central axis of a fruit.
