annotations.label: whole plant fruit formation stage 10 to 30%
annotations.definition: A whole plant fruit formation stage (PO:0007042) that spans the interval when the average size of fruits (PO:0009001) on a whole plant (PO:0000003) has reached 10% to 30% of final size.
annotations.comment: This terms is best used for plant with more or less synchronous fruit development and can only be used of the final size of the fruit is known. This stage is to be used for the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003). For an individual fruit, use fruit size 10 to 30% stage (PO:0025505).

synonyms.text: 7.02 Pod 30% of final length in soybean (related)

annotations.label: whole plant fruit ripening stage
annotations.definition: A whole plant fruit development stage (PO:0025500) that begins when the first fruit (PO:0009001) on a whole plant (PO:0000003) begins the process of ripening (GO:0009835) in a way which, in case there are other fruits, the majority of the other fruits start to ripen continuously therewith, and ends with earliest of the following: (1) the whole plant begins a sporophyte senescent stage (PO:0007017), (2) the whole plant begins a sporophyte dormant stage (PO:0007132), or (3) all fruits on the whole plant are finished ripening.
annotations.comment: Succeeds a whole plant fruit formation stage (PO:0007042). This term is to be used for a stage of development of a whole plant. For the stages of development of an individual fruit, see fruit ripening stage (PO:0025502). Although individual fruits are undergoing fruit ripening (GO:0009835), which is a type of senescence, during this stage, the whole plant is not yet undergoing multicelluar organism senescence (GO:0010259). An iteroparous species generally will have only one fruit ripening stage in its life, while a semelparous species may have multiple fruit ripening stage in its life. A whole plant fruit ripening stage may be followed by a sporophyte senescent stage, as in case (1) or by a sporophyte dormant stage (PO:0007132), as in case (2). In evergreen species and species that fruit multiple times during a growing season, a plant may have multiple fruit development stages without entering dormancy or senescence, as in case (3). In plants with continuous (i.e., indeterminate) fruit development (GO:0010154), new fruits may continue to develop after the first fruit has begun to ripen and the whole plant fruit formation stage (PO:0007042) has ended. In some plants, fruit may continue to ripen after the whole plant has entered a sporophyte dormant stage or sporophyte senescent stage and left the whole plant fruit ripening stage. Some plants may never enter a whole plant fruit ripening stage, because fruits only begin to ripen after the plant has entered a sporophyte dormant stage or sporophyte senescent stage. Although the use of synonyms like "10-dough stage in sorghum" properly refer to the development of an individual fruit, they are used as here to extrapolate to a whole plant.

synonyms.text: dough stage kernels yellowish, about 55% dry matter (related)

annotations.label: BO.03 late boot stage
annotations.definition: The flag leaf sheath swollen.
annotations.comment: In rice the flag leaf sheath is more than 10cm out of the penultimate leaf sheath. Booting is a term used specifically for grasses (Poaceae).

annotations.label: booting stage
annotations.definition: An inflorescence detectable phase characterized by a swelling of the flag leaf sheath, caused by an increase in the size of the inflorescence as it grows up the leaf sheath.
annotations.comment: Booting is a term used specifically for grasses (Poaceae).

annotations.label: radicle emergence stage
annotations.definition: A root development stage (PO:0007520) during which a radicle (PO:0020031) emerges from the seed coat (PO:0009088).
annotations.comment: The actual point of emergence from the seed coat (PO:0009088) may not be observed if the seed is underneath a growth medium. In a fruit (PO:0009001) with a persistent pericarp (PO:0009084), emergence from the seed coat may not be observed. If the fruit is a caryopsis, the dry pericarp is fused with the seed coat, and emergence from the pericarp is simultaneous with emergence from the seed coat. This term is used only for seed plants.

synonyms.text: 0.2 radicle emergence from seed in maize (related)
05 radicle emergence from seed coat in soybean (related)

annotations.label: whole plant flowering stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte reproductive stage (PO:0007130) during which a flower (PO:0009046) on a whole plant (PO:0000003) is open.
annotations.comment: This term is to be used for a stage of development of a whole plant. For the stages of development of an individual flower, see flower development stage (PO:0007615).

annotations.label: sporophyte senescent stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte development stage (PO:0028002) during which a sporophyte participates in multicellular organism senescence.
annotations.comment: The sporophyte senescent stage is often preceded by the sporophyte reproductive stage, and it ends with death of the sporophyte, either as a result of senescence or some other cause. The sporophyte senescent stage may succeed the sporophyte reproductive stage in monocarpic plants that die after reproducing, such as annuals. This stage is distinct from sporophyte dormant stage, in which organs may senesce, but some parts of the plant remain alive. Multicellular organism senescence/aging (GO:0010259) includes loss of functions such as resistance to disease, homeostasis, and fertility, as well processes like cellular senescence, organ senescence, and wear and tear. The POC curators have requested to the GOC that they revise the definition of GO:0010259, to make it more appropriate for plants.

annotations.comment: This terms is best used for plant with more or less synchronous fruit development and can only be used of the final size of the fruit is known. This stage is to be used for the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003). For an individual fruit, use fruit size 30 to 50% stage (PO:0025506).

annotations.comment: This terms is best used for plant with more or less synchronous fruit development and can only be used of the final size of the fruit is known. annotations.label: inflorescence emergence stage
annotations.definition: An inflorescence detectable phase during which an inflorescence emerges from sheathing leaves or bracts.
annotations.comment: Includes emergence of a Zea mays inflorescence from a flag leaf sheath, following booting. In US cornbelt maize (including B73) the tassel fully emerges about 2-3 days prior to silk emergence from husk leaves. At this time the ear and husk may still be enclosed within its leaf sheath depending on the variety and environmental conditions.

annotations.label: whole plant fruit formation stage
annotations.definition: A whole plant fruit development stage (PO:0025500) that begins when a fruit (PO:0009001) on a whole plant (PO:0000003) that is not currently participating in a whole plant fruit development stage begins a fruit initiation stage (PO:0025503) and ends with the onset of a whole plant fruit ripening stage (PO:0007010).
annotations.comment: This term is to be used for a stage of development of a whole plant (PO:0000003). For the stages of development of an individual fruit, see fruit development stage (PO:0001002). An iteroparous plant will only have one fruit formation stage in its life,  while a semelparous plant may have multiple whole plant fruit formation stages during its life. In plants with continuous (i.e., indeterminate) fruit development (GO:0010154), new fruits may continue to develop after the first fruit has begun to ripen and the fruit ripening stage of whole plant (PO:0007042) has begun. Although the use of synonyms like "07-milk stage in rice" properly refer to the development of an individual fruits, they are used as here to extrapolate to a whole plant.

annotations.label: hypocotyl emergence stage
annotations.definition: A seedling development stage (PO:0007131) during which the hypocotyl (PO:0020100) emerges from the seed coat (PO:0009088).
annotations.comment: This term is used only for seed plants. The actual point of emergence from the seed coat (PO:0009088) may not be observed if the seed (PO:0009010) is underneath a growth medium. In a fruit (PO:0009001) with a persistent pericarp (PO:0009084), emergence from the seed coat may not be observed.

synonyms.text: 0.07.01 Cotyledons and hypocotyl emerge in soybean (related)
0.07.02 Hypocotyl emerges above soil in soybean (related)
hypocotyl and cotyledon emergence in Arabidopsis (related)

annotations.label: BO.02 mid boot stage
annotations.definition: The flag leaf sheath is just visibly swollen.
annotations.comment: In rice the flag leaf sheath is 5-10cm out of the penultimate leaf sheath. Booting is a term used specifically for grasses (Poaceae).

synonyms.text: flag leaf sheath just visibly swollen (related)

annotations.label: coleoptile emergence stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte vegetative stage (PO:0007134) during which the coleoptile (PO:0020033) emerges from the seed coat (PO:0009088).
annotations.comment: Occurs in Poaceae and some other monocotyledons.  May occur simultaneously with coleoptile emergence from the growth medium.  The actual point of emergence from the seed coat (PO:0009088) may not be observed if the seed (PO:0009010) is underneath a growth medium. In a fruit (PO:0009001) with a persistent pericarp (PO:0009084), emergence from the seed coat may not be observed. If the fruit is a caryopsis, the dry pericarp is fused with the seed coat, and emergence from the pericarp is simultaneous with emergence from the seed coat. This terms is used only for seed plants.

synonyms.text: 0.3 coleoptile emergence from seed in maize (related)

annotations.label: IL.01 1/4 inflorescence length reached stage

annotations.label: whole plant inflorescence detectable stage
annotations.definition: A reproductive growth stage during which an inflorescence is detectable.
annotations.comment: This includes the stage when an inflorescence starts to develop (and is detectable only by assay or with a microscope), to the stage where it is visible to the naked eye. This includes the booting stage in the grasses (Poaceae). There is no one-to-one correspondence between some of the phases of inflorescence formation in members of Poaceae (e.g., booting) with that of other families.

The actual point of emergence from the seed coat (PO:0009088) may not be observed if the seed is underneath a growth medium, especially in plants with hypogeal germination. annotations.label: sporophyte reproductive stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte development stage that occurs during the interval between the initiation of a sporangium and the onset of senescence.
annotations.comment: A whole plant may continue developmental vegetative growth (GO:0080186) after it has entered the reproductive stage, and it may revert to a stage in which it is no longer producing  sporangia, but once it has initiated a single sporangium, it remains in the reproductive stage until senescence or death by some other cause.

annotations.label: seedling development stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte vegetative stage (PO:0007134) that succeeds the seed germination stage (PO:0007057) and terminates with the development of the first adult vascular leaf (PO:0006340).
annotations.comment: This terms is used only for seed plants, although non-seed plants may have a comparable phase during which they produce juvenile or transition leaves.

annotations.label: sporophyte dormant stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte development stage during which a sporophyte participates in a dormancy process.
annotations.comment: A dormancy process (GO:0022611) is a developmental process in which dormancy (sometimes called a dormant state) is induced, maintained or broken. Dormancy is a suspension of most physiological activity and growth that can be reactivated. This term only applies to a whole plant in the sporophyte stage, not to the dormant stage of a seed, bud, or other plant structure. Dormancy of a plant may be preceded by the senescence of its parts such as leaves in woody plants or most of the shoot system herbaceous perennials and by the formation of dormant buds. The end of dormancy in a sporophyte is marked by resumed growth of buds and/or growth of vascular cambium. The dormant stage may be a response to environmental conditions such as seasonality or extreme heat, drought, or cold. Examples of a sporophyte dormant stage are the resurrection plants Selaginella lepidophylla (a lycopod) and Craterostigma plantagineum (an angiosperm) while they are dessicated, and deciduous woody plants and herbaceous perennials or biennials that have a dormant stage in response to drought or cold.

annotations.label: leaf production stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the leaves produced by SAM are visible above the ground in a seedling or mature plant.
annotations.comment: This stage is recommended over rosette growth or stem elongation as it is most easily observed and least ambiguous.

annotations.label: sporophyte vegetative stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte development stage (PO:0028002) that occurs during the interval between the first division of a plant zygote (PO:0000423) and the formation of a sporangium (PO:0025094).
annotations.comment: Succeeds a plant zygote stage (PO:0001097). A sporophyte may begin to senesce or die from other causes before producing sporangia. Developmental vegetative growth (GO:0080186) can also occur during the reproductive stage.

annotations.label: 4 growth directionality stage
annotations.definition: The stage involved in the directionality of expansion growth.

annotations.label: root cap formation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the root cap becomes evident.
annotations.comment: The number and origin of root cap initials that form the root cap varies among species. The root cap initials become independent from the more internally located root meristem cells by an increase in the thickness of the cell wall that separates them.

synonyms.text: root cap formation in crown root. (related)
root cap formation in lateral root. (related)
root cap formation in primary root. (related)

annotations.label: crown root primordium formation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the initial cells of crown root primordium are formed in the innermost ground meristem cells by one or two periclinal divisions.
annotations.comment: Crown root primordia formed endogenously opposite the collateral bundles in maize.

annotations.label: 1 root primordium formation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which root primordium originates and develops at specific locations in embryonic and post embryonic roots.

annotations.label: primary root primordium formation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which a group of cells or initials that will differentiate into the primary root become organized in the embryo .

annotations.label: root vascular cylinder differentiation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the root vascular cylinder becomes evident.
annotations.comment: The first identifiable region in the differentiating vascular cylinder is the pericycle. In the xylem the differentiation of the tracheary elements begins in a centrifugal direction but is completed in the centripetal direction. The first phloem elements mature before the first xylem elements.

annotations.label: root emergence stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the fully formed root emerges.

annotations.label: lateral root primordium formation stage
annotations.definition: This stage at which the transverse divisions in pericycle cells form the initials of the lateral root primordium.

annotations.label: root cortex differentiation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the root cortex becomes evident.
annotations.comment: Repetitive periclinal divisions and radial enlargement of cells form the cortex layers, the innermost layers become the endodermis with the differentiation of casparian strips.

annotations.label: root epidermal differentiation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the root epidermis becomes evident.
annotations.comment: Root hairs come to full development beyond the region of elongation zone, approximately at the level where the xylem begins to mature.

annotations.label: lateral root emergence stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the lateral root emerges.
annotations.comment: Starch grains can be seen in the root cap after emergence.

annotations.label: crown root emergence stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the crown root emerges.
annotations.comment: Crown root stages are based on rice plant.

annotations.label: 5 root hair formation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the root hairs becomes evident.

annotations.label: root development stage
annotations.definition: Stages of development of a root defined by characteristic morphological, structural, histological or other visible features.
annotations.comment: At present the stages are described for primary, lateral and crown roots and stages for adventitious root formed by wounding or hormone treatment is not described. Finer resolution of root stages has been kept in the obsolete node as they are not consistently used in the consulted literature. These terms will be reintroduced into the ontology when there is a specific user request.

annotations.label: 3 establishment of tissue systems stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which distinct tissues become organized.

synonyms.text: cell differentiation stage of root development (related)
establishment of fundamental organization in adventitious root (related)
establishment of fundamental organization in primary root. (related)
establishment of fundamental organization lateral root. (related)

annotations.label: 2 root meristem formation stage
annotations.definition: The stage at which the root meristem becomes evident.
annotations.comment: Characteristic differences in meristem organization have been related to taxonomic groups. Terms for developmental zones formation are available in the obsolete node, and they will be reintroduced in the ontology when there is a specific user request for them.

annotations.label: floral organ differentiation stage
annotations.definition: A floral organ formation stage (PO:0025585) which begins with the differentiation of floral organs (PO:0025395) from the floral organ primordium (PO:0025477) and ends with the beginning of the flowering stage (PO:0007616).
annotations.comment: During this stage, the process of floral organ development (GO:0048437) occurs, defined as the process whose specific outcome is the progression of the floral organ over time, from its formation to the mature structure.

annotations.label: floral organ meristem development stage
annotations.definition: A floral organ formation stage (PO:0025585) which begins with the development of the floral organ meristem (PO:0000229) from a portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) and ends with the beginning of the floral organ primordium development stage (PO:0007602).
annotations.comment: During this stage, the process of floral meristem growth (GO:0010451) occurs. This process is defined as the increase in size or mass of a floral meristem, a population of undifferentiated cells in a plant that gives rise to a flower.

annotations.label: floral organ primordium development stage
annotations.definition: A floral organ formation stage (PO:0025585) which begins with the appearance of the floral organ primordium (PO:0025477) and ends with the beginning of the floral organ differentiation stage (PO:0007600).
annotations.comment: During this stage, the process of floral meristem determinacy (GO:0010582) occurs, where a floral meristem becomes determinate (i.e. ceases to produce lateral organs and may or may not terminally differentiate). This stage may be indicated by a slight indentation to the inflorescence meristem.

annotations.label: calyx development stage
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure development stage (PO:0025578) that has as a primary participant a calyx (PO:0009060).

annotations.label: corolla development stage
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure development stage (PO:0025578) that has as a primary participant a corolla (PO:0009059).

annotations.label: androecium development stage
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure development stage (PO:0025578) that has as a primary participant an androecium (PO:0009061).

annotations.label: gynoecium development stage
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure development stage (PO:0025578) that has a primary participant a gynoecium (PO:0009062).

annotations.label: sepal primordium visible stage
annotations.definition: Stage of calyx development that begins when the sepal primordia arise.

annotations.label: petal primordium visible stage
annotations.definition: Stage of corolla development that begins when the petal or petal-stamen primordia arise.

annotations.label: petal differentiation and expansion stage
annotations.definition: Stage of corolla development defined by differentiation and expansion of petal.

annotations.label: fully expanded petal stage
annotations.definition: Stage of corolla development when petal is fully expanded.

annotations.label: stamen primordium visible stage
annotations.definition: Stage of androecium development that begins when the stamens or petal-stamen primordia arise.

annotations.label: flower development stage
annotations.definition: A reproductive shoot system development stage (PO:0025530) that occurs in an individual flower (PO:000904) during the interval between the onset of a flower meristem transition stage (PO:0025588) and the end of a flowering stage (PO:0007616).
annotations.comment: This term and its sub-classes apply to an individual flower (PO:000904). During this stage, a flower undergoes the process of flower development (GO:0009908), defined as the process whose specific outcome is the progression of the flower over time, from its formation to the mature structure.  For the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003), use whole plant flowering stage (PO:0007016).

annotations.label: flowering stage
annotations.definition: A flower development stage (PO:0007615) that begins when pollen (PO:0025281) is released by anther dehiscence (GO:0009901) and/or the stigma (PO:0009073) is receptive and ends with the process of pollination (GO:0009856) and/or floral organ senescence (GO:0080187).
annotations.comment: In most species, flowering stage (anthesis) is when the flower is open and ends with pollination.  During this stage, the process of anther dehiscence (GO:0009901) usually occurs, defined as the dehiscence of an anther to release the pollen grains contained within it. This term applies to an individual flower (PO:000904).

annotations.label: carpel primordium visible stage
annotations.definition: Stage of gynoecium development that begins when the carpel primordia arise.

annotations.label: ovule development stage
annotations.definition: Stages of development of the ovule defined by characteristic morphological, structural, histological or other visible features.

annotations.label: ovule primordium visible stage
annotations.definition: Stage of ovule development that begins when the ovule primordia arise.

annotations.label: megagametophyte development stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) during which the megagametophyte (PO:0025279) develops.
annotations.comment: Although megaspore and megagametophyte development are normally separate, one spore producing one gametophyte, in some flowering plants a single gametophyte may be produced from two or four spores.

annotations.label: integument initiation stage
annotations.definition: Stage of ovule development that begins when the integument primordia are visible.

annotations.label: A megaspore mother cell enlarges stage
annotations.definition: The stage of megagametophyte development when the megaspore mother cell enlarges.

annotations.label: B meiosis of megaspore mother cell stage
annotations.definition: The stage of megagametophyte development when the megaspore mother cell undergoes meiosis.

annotations.label: C tetrad of megaspores stage
annotations.definition: The product of meiosis of the megaspore mother cell.

annotations.label: D megaspore degeneration stage
annotations.definition: The stage of megagametophyte development when non-functional megaspores degenerate.
annotations.comment: In some plants more than one megaspore contributes to the megametophyte.

annotations.label: E two nucleate megagametophyte stage
annotations.definition: The stage of megagametophyte development when there are two nuclei.

annotations.label: F four nucleate megagametophyte stage
annotations.definition: The stage of megagametophyte development when there are four nuclei.

annotations.label: G eight nucleate megagametophyte stage
annotations.definition: The stage of megagametophyte development when there are eight nuclei.
annotations.comment: Some plants do not form an eight nucleate megametophyte.

annotations.label: plant embryo development stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte vegetative stage (PO:0007134) that occurs during the interval between the first cell division of a plant zygote (PO:0000423) and one of the following: the beginning of the seed germination stage (PO:0007057) in seed plants, formation of the first vascular leaf (PO:0009025) in pteridophytes, the beginning of development of a sporangium (PO:0025094) in bryophytes, or the beginning of the formation of a plant organ (PO:0009008) such as a root (PO:0009005), shoot axis (PO:0025029), or vascular leaf (PO:0009025) in a cultured plant embryo (PO:0000010).
annotations.comment: The plant embryo stage generally starts after the first division of a plant zygote (PO:0000423), but in the case of a nucellar (adventitious) plant embryo (PO:0004537), somatic plant embryo (PO:0025302), microspore-derived cultured plant embryo (PO:0025305), and other embryos that arise through apogamy, it begins after the division of a single cell that is not a zygote. The end of the embryo stage varies among taxa. No plant species undergoes all of the sub-stages of plant embryo development stage listed here.

annotations.label: seed maturation stage
annotations.definition: Stage during which the seed storage products (storage proteins, lipids and starch) are being accumulated, either in the cotyledons or in the endosperm. Also, LEA proteins are accumulated and subsequently, a desiccation tolerant state is reached at the end of this stage.

annotations.label: endosperm development stage
annotations.definition: Stages of development of the endosperm.

annotations.label: fruit distal end
annotations.definition: A cardinal part of multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025498) that is the end of a fruit (PO:0009001) distal from a fruit pedicel (PO:0004536).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: fruit proximal end
annotations.definition: A cardinal part of multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025498) that is the end of a fruit (PO:0009001) proximal to a fruit pedicel (PO:0004536).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: fruit vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue (PO:0009015) in their specific arrangement in a fruit (PO:0009001).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: fruit nucellus
annotations.definition: A nucellus that is part of a fruit.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: nucellar epidermis
annotations.definition: A megasporangium exothecium (PO:0025312) that is the morphologically distinct outer layer of the nucellus (PO:0020020).

annotations.label: nucellar projection
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the morphologically distinct portion of the nucellus overlying the vascular strands in the grass caryopsis.

annotations.label: modified aleurone
annotations.definition: A portion of aleurone layer (PO:0005360) which is morphologically distinct and is located adjacent to the nucellar projection (PO:0008007).

synonyms.text: 特殊化した糊粉、修飾アリューロン (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: abaxial nucellar projection
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the abaxial/lower portion of the nucellus (PO:0020020) overlying vascular strands in a caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104).

annotations.label: adaxial nucellar projection
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the adaxial/upper portion of the nucellus (PO:0020020) overlying vascular strands in a caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104).

annotations.label: plant embryo vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that is part of a plant embryo (PO:0009009) .

annotations.label: stigma vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a stigma (PO:0009073).

annotations.label: anther vascular system
annotations.definition: A phyllome vascular system (PO:0025206) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in an anther (PO:0009066).

synonyms.text: sistema vascular de la antera (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: filament vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue (PO:0009015) in their specific arrangement in a filament (PO:0009067).

annotations.label: hypocotyl vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that is part of a hypocotyl (PO:0020100).

annotations.label: vegetative shoot apical meristem
annotations.definition: A shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) that gives rise to the apical growth of vegetative tissues and organs.

synonyms.text: meristema vegetativo apical del epiblasto (epiblastema) (Spanish, exact)
栄養シュート頂端分裂組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf sheath pulvinus
annotations.definition: A more or less abrupt swelling, especially at the apex or base of a leaf sheath.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: vaina del pulv&#237nulo de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: transition vascular leaf
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf that is part of an heteroblastic series, and is characterized by anatomical features that are intermediate between juvenile and adult leaves.
annotations.comment: Many species have juvenile leaves at the base of the stem, adult leaves at the apex, and transition leaves in between. In maize, juvenile leaves are at the tip of the stalk (stem), which differentiates first, with adult leaves at the base, while other grasses may have other arrangements. Transition leaves may have mosaics of juvenile and adult tissues, as in Brachypodium.

synonyms.text: 維管束植物の幼葉と成熟葉の移行期の葉 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf lamina base
annotations.definition: The region of the leaf lamina proximal to the stem and usually connected to it by means of a petiole.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: base de la l&#225mina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: central zone of the leaf lamina
annotations.definition: Region that includes the widest or majority portion of leaf lamina and that does not include leaf apex and leaf lamina base.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: zona central de la l&#225mina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉身(葉片)の中央帯 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: tertiary leaf vein
annotations.definition: A leaf lamina vein (PO:0020138) arising from a secondary leaf vein (PO:0020140).
annotations.comment: Vein orders only apply to hierarchically branching vein patterns, not to dichotomously branching vein patterns, as found in some ferns and gymnosperms.

annotations.label: quaternary leaf vein
annotations.definition: A leaf lamina vein (PO:0020138) arising from a tertiary leaf vein (PO:0008021).
annotations.comment: Vein orders only apply to hierarchically branching vein patterns, not to dichotomously branching vein patterns, as found in some ferns and gymnosperms.

annotations.label: higher order leaf vein
annotations.definition: A leaf lamina vein (PO:0020138) arising from a quaternary (PO:0008022) or other higher order leaf vein.
annotations.comment: Vein orders only apply to hierarchically branching vein patterns, not to dichotomously branching vein patterns, as found in some ferns and gymnosperms.

synonyms.text: fifth and higher order leaf vein (related)

annotations.label: petiole proximal end
annotations.definition: The region of the petiole proximal to its stem attachment site.

annotations.label: central zone of the petiole
annotations.definition: Regions of the petiole that does not include petiole distal end and petiole proximal end.

annotations.label: petiole distal end
annotations.definition: The region of the petiole distal to its stem attachment site or proximal to the leaf lamina.

annotations.label: petiole adaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of petiole epidermis (PO:0000051) that covers the adaxial/upper surface of a petiole (PO:0020038).

synonyms.text: 葉柄向軸側 表皮 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: reproductive shoot apical meristem
annotations.definition: A shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) that gives rise to the apical growth of reproductive tissues and organs.

synonyms.text: meristema apical reproductivo del epiblasto (epiblastema) (Spanish, exact)
生殖シュート頂端 分裂組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: petiole abaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of petiole epidermis (PO:0000051) that covers the abaxial/lower surface of a petiole (PO:0020038).

synonyms.text: 葉柄背軸側 表皮 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: trichome cell
annotations.definition: A plant cell (PO:0009002) that is part of a multicellular trichome (PO:0000282).

synonyms.text: 毛（毛茸、糸状体）細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: stomatal pore
annotations.definition: A plant anatomical space that forms an opening in an epidermis, flanked by two guard cells.
annotations.comment: The size of the stomatal pore is variable and controlled by movement of the guard cells in response to environmental cues.

annotations.label: plerome
annotations.definition: A meristem forming the core of the axis composed of the primary vascular tissues and associated ground tissue, such as pith and interfascicular regions.

annotations.label: periblem
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue that forms the cortex.

annotations.label: seedling
annotations.definition: A whole plant (PO:0000003) in the seedling development stage (PO:0007131).
annotations.comment: A young plant derived from a newly germinated seed, up until the development of the first true leaf.

annotations.label: stem base
annotations.definition: The basal part of stem.

annotations.label: lodicule primordium
annotations.definition: A phyllome primordium (PO:0025128)that develops from a lodicule anlagen (PO:0025483) and is committed to the development of a lodicule (PO:0009036).

annotations.label: stem internode elongation zone
annotations.definition: A shoot axis internode elongation zone  (PO:0025100) that is part of a stem internode (PO:0009047).

synonyms.text: zona de elongaci&#243n del internodo del tallo (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: stem internode differentiation zone
annotations.definition: A shoot internode differentiation zone that is part of a stem internode.
annotations.comment: Primary, not secondary, tissue differentiation occurs in a differentiation zone.

synonyms.text: zona de diferenciaci&#243n del internodo del tallo (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: scutellum vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that is part of a scutellum (PO:0020110).

annotations.label: scutellar epithelium
annotations.definition: A continuous layer of cylindrical cells covering the endosperm side of the scutellum. Thought to act as a secretory and absorptive organ.

annotations.label: pulvinus
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) at the apex or base of a petiole, petiolule, leaf sheath, or branch that is differentiated from the remaining organ parts by its enlarged cross section.
annotations.comment: Often glandular. Often involved in movement of a plant structure in response to touch, gravity or light. In panicoid grasses a swollen region in the stem above the intercalary meristem. Such a definition would also include stems of Chloranthaceae, Acanthaceae, Caryophyllaceae, Amaranthaceae, etc. [source: APweb:Glossary].

annotations.label: fruit
annotations.definition: A multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025496) that develops from a gynoecium (PO:0009062), or a single carpel (PO:0009030), and at maturity may have as parts one or more seeds (PO:0009010).
annotations.comment: A fruit (PO:0009001) may contain additional plant structures (PO:0009011) that were part of a flower (PO:0009046) and mature along with the gynoecium, such as a receptacle (PO:0009064). A fruit may develop without fertilization in cases of parthenocarpy, apomixis, or other hormone-induced conditions and may not always contain seeds (PO:0009010). When annotating to fruit (PO:0009001) that are referred to as ‘multiple’, or ‘compound’, please annotate directly to the appropriate plant structure, such as receptacle, hypanthium (PO:0009065) or infructescence (PO:0006342). Fruits only occur in angiosperms.

annotations.label: plant cell
annotations.definition: A cell which is a plant structure (PO:0009011).
annotations.comment: Applies to cells that are living or dead at maturity (e.g., fiber cell or tracheid) and includes any external encapsulating structures (if present) such as the plasma membrane and the plant-type cell wall.  Definition of cell CL:0000000: "A material entity of anatomical origin (part of or deriving from an organism) that has as its parts a maximally connected cell compartment surrounded by a plasma membrane. The definition of cell is intended to represent all cells, and thus a cell is defined as a material entity and not an anatomical structure, which implies that it is part of an organism (or the entirety of one)". Definition of plant-type cell wall (GO:0009505): "A more or less rigid structure lying outside the cell membrane of a cell and composed of cellulose and pectin and other organic and inorganic substances."  Exact synonym: cellulose and pectin-containing cell wall.

annotations.label: root
annotations.definition: A plant axis (PO:0025004) that lacks shoot axis nodes (PO:0005004) and usually grows indeterminately.
annotations.comment: Roots function in the absorption of water and inorganic nutrients, anchoring the plant body to the substrate and supporting it, storage of food and nutrients, and vegetative reproduction. The roots of most vascular plant species enter into symbiosis with soil-borne microorganisms. Roots are usually positively geotropic and found underground, although there are many exceptions such as the aerial roots of orchids. Roots often form secondary thickening from the root lateral meristem (PO:0006308). Commonly thought of as one of the three basic parts of the plant body, along with the shoot axis (PO:0025029) and leaves (PO:0025034).

annotations.label: shoot system
annotations.definition: A collective plant organ structure (PO:0025007) that produces shoot-borne portions of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) and the plant structures (PO:0009011) that arise from them.
annotations.comment: The shoot system is generally used to refer to the above-ground plant parts, although some plants have parts of their shoot system underground. For example, a rhizome (PO:0004542), bulb (PO:0025356), a corm (PO:0025355) or a subterranean tuber (PO:0004547), as in Solanum tuberosum (potato) or yam, are all part of the shoot system.

synonyms.text: シュート系、苗条系 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: portion of plant tissue
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0009011) that consists predominantly of similarly specialized plant cells (PO:0009002) of one or more types.
annotations.comment: A portion of plant tissue may contain one or several types of cells that are organized in a specific spatial arrangement into a structural unit (which includes a mass of callus) and may include an intercellular matrix. May include other types of isolated cells, such as idioblasts.

synonyms.text: porci&#243n de un tejido vegetal (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: plant organ
annotations.definition: A multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025496) that is a functional unit, is a proper part of a whole plant (PO:0000003), and includes portions of plant tissue (PO:0009007) of at least two different types that derive from a common developmental path.
annotations.comment: Examples include stem (PO:0009047), leaf (PO:0025034), and root (PO:0009005). May include individual plant cells (PO:0009002) that are not part of a portion of plant tissue (e.g., idioblasts, PO:0000283). A plant organ may have one or more different plant organs as parts, such as a sporophyll (PO:0009026) that may have as part a sporangium (PO:0025094) or a carpel (PO:0009030) that may have as part a plant ovule (PO:0020003).

annotations.label: plant embryo
annotations.definition: A whole plant (PO:0000003) that participates in the plant embryo stage (PO:0007631).
annotations.comment: A plant embryo is generally formed after the first division of a plant zygote (PO:0000423), but in the case of a nucellar (adventitious) plant embryo (PO:0004537), somatic plant embryo (PO:0025302), microspore-derived cultured plant embryo (PO:0025305), and other embryos that arise through apogamy, it begins after the division of a single cell that is not a zygote. The end of the embryo stage varies among taxa; the beginning of a seed germination stage (PO:0007057) in seed plants, formation of the first vascular leaf (PO:0009025) in pteridophytes, the beginning of development of a sporangium (PO:0025094) in bryophytes, or the beginning of the formation of a plant organ (PO:0009008) such as a root (PO:0009005), shoot axis (PO:0025029), or vascular leaf (PO:0009025) in a cultured plant embryo (PO:0000010).

annotations.label: seed
annotations.definition: A multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025496) that develops from a plant ovule (PO:0020003) and has as parts a plant embryo (PO:0009009) enclosed in a seed coat (PO:0009088).
annotations.comment: A seed generally develops from an ovule (PO:0020003) after fertilization, but may develop without fertilization in the case of apogamy (e.g., adventitious embryos or somatic embryos). A seed is a reproductive unit of seed plants (gymnosperms, angiosperms, and fossil pteridosperms).

annotations.label: plant structure
annotations.definition: A plant anatomical entity (PO:0025131) that is, or was, part of a plant, or was derived from a part of a plant.
annotations.comment: 'Part' includes both proper parts and the whole plant. CARO:0000003 'connected anatomical structure' is defined as: Material anatomical entity that is a single connected structure with inherent 3D shape, generated by coordinated expression of the organism's own genome.

annotations.label: plant structure development stage
annotations.definition: A stage in the life of a plant structure (PO:0009011) during which the plant structure undergoes developmental processes.
annotations.comment: Refers to GO:0032502 'developmental process', which includes growth, differentiation, and senescence, and BFO:0000003 'ocurrent'.

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo de estructura vegetal (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: portion of meristem tissue
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) in which plant cells (PO:0009002) have retained their embryonic characteristics, or have reverted to them secondarily, and that divide to produce new cells that can undergo differentiation to form mature tissues, i.e. they have a capacity for morphogenesis and growth.

annotations.label: portion of vascular tissue
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that has as part one or more tracheary elements (PO:0000290) or sieve elements (PO:0025406).
annotations.comment: Functions in conduction and support. In shoot axes (PO:0025029), vascular tissue is often found as part of a shoot axis stele (PO:0025198) or as scattered vascular bundles (PO:0005020). May include other types of tissues or cells, such as parenchyma (PO:0005421) or fiber cells (PO:0025407).

synonyms.text: 維管束細胞の一部 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: inflorescence branch pulvinus
annotations.definition: A pulvinus (PO:0009000) of an inflorescence branch (PO:0009081).

synonyms.text: pulv&#237nulo ramificado de una inflorescencia (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: basal endosperm transfer cell
annotations.definition: A transfer cell that is part of a basal endosperm transfer layer.
annotations.comment: Responsible for transferring nutrients to an embryo by symplastic or apoplastic methods.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula de transferencia del endosperma basal (Spanish, exact)
基底胚乳転送細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: meristem L1 layer
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) that is the outermost layer of a shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) and gives rise to a shoot system epidermis (PO:0006035).
annotations.comment: The cells of the meristem L1 layer divide in a strictly anticlinical fashion.

annotations.label: meristem L2 layer
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) directly beneath a meristem L1 layer (PO:0009020) in a shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) which gives rise to ground tissue (PO:0025059).
annotations.comment: Cells in the first subepidermal layer, the L2, divide in a largely anticlinal fashion, forming a completely separate population of cells from the other cell layers. Within developing organs, the meristem L2 layer divides anticlinally and periclinally.

annotations.label: meristem L3 layer
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) beneath a meristem L2 layer (PO:0009021) in a shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) which gives rise to internal tissues of leaf and stem.
annotations.comment: The meristem L3 layer cells frequently divide in various orientations.

annotations.label: leaf axil
annotations.definition: An axil that is the space between a shoot axis and a leaf that branches from the shoot axis.

annotations.label: vascular leaf
annotations.definition: A leaf (PO:0025034) in a vascular plant.
annotations.comment: Has vascular tissue. From APweb Glossary: In angiosperms, commonly thought of as one of the three basic parts of the seed plant body, a structure usually of determinate growth, without secondary thickening, and of superficial origin, often flattened and photosynthetic in part, and in the axil of which is found a bud. Occurs in the sporophytic phase of a plant life cycle.

synonyms.text: 維管束のある葉, または維管束植物の葉 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: sporophyll
annotations.definition: A leaf-like organ on which one or more sporangia are borne.

annotations.label: megasporophyll
annotations.definition: A structure upon which (or in the axil of which) one or more megasporangia are borne; in flowering plants, a carpel.

annotations.label: microsporophyll
annotations.definition: A structure on which one or more microsporangia are borne; in flowering plants, the stamen.

annotations.label: stamen
annotations.definition: A microsporophyll bearing one or more microsporangia.
annotations.comment: Part of tassel floret.

annotations.label: carpel
annotations.definition: A megasporophyll, almost always at the center of a flower, its margins more or less fused together or with other carpels to enclose the ovule(s).
annotations.comment: In some cases, a gynoecium (PO:0009062) may consist of only one carpel, in which case annotations should go on carpel (PO:0009030). The word pistil is used synonymously with gynoecium, but if a gynoecium consists of a single carpel, then pistil may be used synonymously with carpel. If carpels are fused, the gynoecium is a syncarpous gynoecium, as in Poaceae, Brassicaceae and Solanaceae. If carpels are free, the gynoecium is an apocarpous gynoecium, as in Fabaceae. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), ear floret (PO:0006354), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352), tassel floret (PO:0006310), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

annotations.label: sepal
annotations.definition: A phyllome (PO:0006001) that is usually green, and is part of the calyx (PO:0009060).
annotations.comment: In some species the sepals (PO:0009031) may not be green for example many members of the Liliaceae family.

annotations.label: petal
annotations.definition: A phyllome (PO:0006001) that is part of the corolla (PO:0009059), and is usually colored (not green).
annotations.comment: In some species the petal (PO:0009032) may not be colored.

annotations.label: tepal
annotations.definition: A phyllome (PO:0006001) that is part of a perianth (PO:0009058) where it is not possible to distinguish between the petals (PO:0009032) and sepals (PO:0009031).
annotations.comment: There is no differentiation between the petals (PO:0009032) and the sepals (PO:0009031) in color, placement, and size (e.g. some species of Magnolia).

annotations.label: flower bract
annotations.definition: A bract (PO:0009055) that subtends a perianth (PO:0009058) that is part of a flower (PO:0009046).

annotations.label: nectary
annotations.definition: A portion of secretory tissue (PO:0005656) which is a multicellular glandular structure secreting a liquid containing organic substances, including sugars.

annotations.label: lodicule
annotations.definition: A phyllome that is part of a grass floret and is one of two or three tiny scales or flaps of tissue outside the stamens.
annotations.comment: May represent a reduced floral structure. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type, if known. Choose the most specific term possible from: ear floret (PO:0006354), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006353), lower floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006351), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

annotations.label: lemma
annotations.definition: The lower, usually larger, of the pair of flower bracts (PO:0009034) enclosing a (PO:0009082) spikelet floret.
annotations.comment: A lemma (PO:0009037) and palea (PO:0009038) enclose an individual spikelet floret (PO:0009082). The lemma is usually the larger of the two. Since it is not clear what the lemma and palea correspond to in conventional flowers, no assumptions of homology are made. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea species an annotation can also be made to the more specific floret type, if known. Choose the most specific term possible from: ear floret (PO:0006354), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006353), lower floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006351), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316). Found in  Found in Poaceae, Cyperaceae and other Poales.

synonyms.text: lemma del florete del penacho (borla) o de la espiga (Spanish, exact)
lemma of lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (narrow)
lemma of lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (narrow)
lemma of lower floret of sessile spikelet of ear (narrow)
lemma of lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (narrow)
lemma of upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (narrow)
lemma of upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (narrow)
lemma of upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (narrow)
lemma of upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (narrow)

annotations.label: palea
annotations.definition: The upper (distal), usually smaller, of the pair of flower bracts (PO:0009034) enclosing a (PO:0009082) spikelet floret.
annotations.comment: A lemma (PO:0009037) and palea (PO:0009038) enclose an individual spikelet floret (PO:0009082). The palea is usually the smaller of the two. Since it is not clear what the lemma and palea correspond to in conventional flowers, no assumptions of homology are made. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea species an annotation can also be made to the more specific floret type, if known. Choose the most specific term possible from: ear floret (PO:0006354), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006353), lower floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006351), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316). Found in  Found in Poaceae, Cyperaceae and other Poales.

synonyms.text: palea of lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (narrow)
palea of lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (narrow)
palea of lower floret of sessile spikelet of ear (exact)
palea of lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (narrow)
palea of upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (narrow)
palea of upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (narrow)
palea of upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (narrow)
palea of upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (narrow)

annotations.label: glume
annotations.definition: One of a pair of inflorescence bracts that is part of a spikelet and subtends the two florets.
annotations.comment: Found in Poaceae and Cyperaceae. Glumes subtend the pair of florets while a lemma or palea subtend the individual florets.

annotations.label: sterile lemma
annotations.definition: A flower bract in the position of a lemma, but not enclosing floral organs.
annotations.comment: An undeveloped androecium or gynoecium may or may not be detectable. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding spikelet type, if known. Choose the most specific term possible from: ear spikelet (PO:0006320), ear pedicellate spikelet (PO:0006348), ear sessile spikelet (PO:0006349), tassel spikelet (PO:0006309), tassel pedicellate spikelet (PO:0006312), tassel sessile spikelet (PO:0006311).

annotations.label: fertile lemma
annotations.definition: A lemma that encloses a functional flower, with androecium, gynoecium or both.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding spikelet type, if known. Choose the most specific term possible from: ear spikelet (PO:0006320), ear pedicellate spikelet (PO:0006348), ear sessile spikelet (PO:0006349), tassel spikelet (PO:0006309), tassel pedicellate spikelet (PO:0006312), tassel sessile spikelet (PO:0006311).

annotations.label: prophyll
annotations.definition: A phyllome that is the first organ or one of the first two organs formed at the base of an axillary shoot, often smaller and/or in a distinctively different position from leaves formed subsequently.
annotations.comment: A prophyll is not classified as a vascular leaf, because it may not have a fully developed vascular system.

annotations.label: bracteole
annotations.definition: A prophyll (PO:0009042) which is borne singly or in pairs on a pedicel (PO:0030112).

annotations.label: perigynium
annotations.definition: Sac-like bract subtending the pistillate flower, as in Carex.

synonyms.text: ペリギニウム、果胞、造卵器群やそれから発生した胞子体の周辺の組織が発達し肉厚で筒状となった構造 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: involucral bract
annotations.definition: An inflorescence bract (PO:0009054) of an involucre (PO:0009100).
annotations.comment: Involucre are found in plant families such as Asteraceae or Compositeae.

annotations.label: flower
annotations.definition: A determinate reproductive shoot system (PO:0025082) that has as part at least one carpel (PO:0009030) or at least one stamen (PO:0009029) and does not contain any other determinate shoot system (PO:0009006) as a part.
annotations.comment: The characteristic reproductive structure of angiosperms. May have as part one or more petals, sepals or tepals. May contain one or more pistillode (PO:0009078), staminode (PO:0009077) or other aborted organs that don't show up in mature form.

annotations.label: stem
annotations.definition: A shoot axis (PO:0025029) that is the primary axis of a plant.
annotations.comment: A stem often bears leaves and buds. It is usually above ground, and is more or less negatively geotropic.

annotations.label: inflorescence
annotations.definition: A reproductive shoot system (PO:0025082) that has as parts all of the shoot axes (PO:0025029) and flowers (PO:0009046; must have two or more) distal to the most distal leaf (PO:0009025).
annotations.comment: An inflorescence is a reproductive shoot system from which at least two flowers develop. The growth pattern of an inflorescence may be monopodial (single main shoot axis), sympodial (growth only occurs from axillary meristems), or both. An inflorescence axis (PO:0020122) can be determinate or indeterminate. An inflorescence flower pedicel (PO:0009052) is the ultimate axis in an inflorescence. The flower (PO:0009046) of many plants, for example Magnolia spp., is solitary.

annotations.label: spikelet
annotations.definition: A reproductive shoot system (PO:0025082) that is the ultimate and congested, higher order inflorescence axis (PO:0009081) of the grasses.
annotations.comment: It consists of one to many closely-packed spikelet florets (PO:0009082) and associated glumes (PO:0009039).

annotations.label: inflorescence flower pedicel
annotations.definition: A flower pedicel (PO:0030113) that bears a flower (PO:0009046) in an inflorescence (PO:0009049).
annotations.comment: An inflorescence flower pedicel is the ultimate shoot axis (PO:0025029) in an inflorescence (PO:0009049).

annotations.label: peduncle
annotations.definition: A shoot axis that extends from the last foliage leaf on a stem or branch until the next distal node.
annotations.comment: In plants with single flowers, the peduncle ends with a flower. In plants with inflorescences, the peduncle is the basal end of the primary inflorescence axis, and it ends where the inflorescence begins to branch. The peduncle marks the axial transition from vegetative to reproductive growth. In branching inflorescences, the second order inflorescence axis from the point at which it leaves the main axis up to the point at which it branches again may be called the second order peduncle. Higher order peduncles are also possible.

annotations.label: inflorescence bract
annotations.definition: A bract (PO:0009055) subtending a branch of the inflorescence (PO:0009049) or borne on the inflorescence axis (PO:0020122) below any branch (PO:0025073) or flower (PO:0009046).
annotations.comment: Includes the leaf subtending and acting as outer envelope of an ear in Zea mays.

annotations.label: bract
annotations.definition: A phyllome that subtends a reproductive structure.
annotations.comment: Usually different from leaves. Often used to refer to a floral bract, but includes other kinds of bracts.

annotations.label: flower nectary
annotations.definition: A nectary (PO:0009035) occurring on a floral organ (PO:0025395) or other part of a flower (PO:0009046).
annotations.comment: A flower nectary may occur on a sepal (PO:0009031), petal (PO:0009032), stamen (PO:0009029), plant ovary (PO:0009072), or receptacle (PO:0009064).

annotations.label: extrafloral nectary
annotations.definition: A nectary (PO:0009035), not occurring on a floral organ (PO:0025395) or other part of a flower (PO:0009046).
annotations.comment: An extrafloral nectary may occur on vegetative plant structures (PO:0009011) such as a stem (PO:0009047), leaf (PO:0009025), petiole (PO:0020038), stipule (PO:0020041) or  flower pedicel (PO:0009052).

annotations.label: perianth
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure (PO:0025023) that includes as parts the corolla (PO:0009059) and/or the calyx (PO:0009060); or one or more tepals (PO:0009033).
annotations.comment: The perianth can consist of only the corolla (PO:0009059), only the calyx (PO:0009060) or it may have both structures in a complete flower.  If the petals (PO:0009032) and sepals (PO:0009031) are indistinguishable from one another, the perianth consists of just a collective tepal structure  (PO:0025021), composed of one or more tepals (PO:0009033), for example in tulip or lily flowers.

annotations.label: corolla
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure (PO:0025023) that is composed of one or more petals (PO:0009032), comprising the inner whorl of non-reproductive floral organs (PO:0025395) and surrounds the androecium (PO:0009061) and the gynoecium (PO:0009062).
annotations.comment: The corolla (PO:0009059) is part of the perianth (PO:0009058). This  definition is based on the complete flower, which has all four whorls. In many species, and in mutants one or more of the whorls may be missing. The corolla (PO:0009059) often functions to attract pollinators.

annotations.label: calyx
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure (PO:0025023) that is composed of one or more sepals (PO:0009031), and comprises the outer whorl of non-reproductive floral organs (PO:0025395).
annotations.comment: The calyx is part of the perianth (PO:0009058). This definition is based on the perfect flower, which has all four whorls. In many species, and in mutants one or more of the whorls are missing.

annotations.label: androecium
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure (PO:0025023) composed all of the stamens (PO:0009029) in a flower (PO:0009046).
annotations.comment: The androecium also has as parts any staminodes (PO:0009077) that may be present in a flower. If annotating gene expression to an androecium with a single stamen (PO:0009029), the annotation should go on stamen. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), ear floret (PO:0006354), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006353), lower floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006351), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316). The development of an androecium in an ear floret of a normal maize plant is aborted and is present in a rudimentary state, while the development of an androecium in a tassel floret leads to a functional androecium.

synonyms.text: androecium of lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (narrow)
androecium of lower floret of sessile spikelet of ear (narrow)
androecium of lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (narrow)
androecium of upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (narrow)
androecium of upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (exact)
androecium of upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (narrow)
androecium of upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (narrow)

annotations.label: gynoecium
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure (PO:0025023) composed all of the carpels (PO:0009030) in a flower (PO:0009046).
annotations.comment: If annotating gene expression to a gynoecium with a single carpel (PO:0009030), the annotation should go on carpel. A gynoecium with two or more carpels may be either syncarpous (carpels fused) or apopcarpous (carpels free) or the carpels may be partially fused. The word pistil is used synonymously with gynoecium for a synocarpous gynoecium, and synonymously with carpel for an apocarpous gynoecium, whether it has one or more carpels. In Zea mays, gynoecia of tassel florets and of the lower florets of ear spikelets usually do not develop fully, and they are present in a rudimentary state. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), ear floret (PO:0006354), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006353), lower floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006351), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

synonyms.text: gynoecium of lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (narrow)
gynoecium of lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (narrow)
gynoecium of lower floret of sessile spikelet of ear (narrow)
gynoecium of lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (narrow)
gynoecium of upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (narrow)
gynoecium of upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (narrow)
gynoecium of upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (narrow)
gynoecium of upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (narrow)

annotations.label: epicalyx
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure (PO:0025023) composed of two or more phyllomes immediately outside the calyx (PO:0009060).

annotations.label: receptacle
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is the region at the distal end of either a peduncle or a pedicel where the floral appendages (e.g.: sepals, petals, stamens and pistils) are attached.
annotations.comment: The fleshy part of a strawberry develops from a receptacle.

annotations.label: hypanthium
annotations.definition: A cup- or tube-shaped cardinal organ part, bearing perianth and stamens on its rim, so these arise above the base, and often above the top, of the ovary.
annotations.comment: Only bears stamens or perianth segments if they are present in the flower (e.g., no stamens in a carpellate flower).

synonyms.text: 花床筒 、いちじく状花序 i.e.hypanthodium (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: anther
annotations.definition: A collective plant organ structure (PO:0025007) that is the pollen-bearing part of a stamen (PO:0009029).
annotations.comment: Generally consists of four pollen sacs (microsporangia) in two anther thecas. If you are annotating to anther or one of its parts for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

annotations.label: filament
annotations.definition: A stalk  (PO:0025066) that is part of a stamen (PO:0009029).
annotations.comment: Supports the anthers. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

annotations.label: connective
annotations.definition: The part of the stamen that connects the microsporangia/pollen sacs.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

annotations.label: anther theca
annotations.definition: A collective plant organ structure (PO:0025007) that is part of an anther (PO:0009066) and consists of a pair of sporangia (PO:0025094) that dehisce down a common slit.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

annotations.label: anther wall tapetum
annotations.definition: A microsporangium tapetum (PO:0025314) that is part of an anther wall (PO:0000002).
annotations.comment: Develops from a primary or secondary parietal cell layer. Innermost part of the anther wall, involved in the nutrition of the pollen. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

synonyms.text: portion of anther wall tapetal tissue (exact)
葯壁タペート組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: plant ovary
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0009011) that is the basal portion of a carpel (PO:0009030) or group of fused carpels (gynoecium; PO:0009062) and encloses the plant ovule(s) (PO:0020003).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), ear floret (PO:0006354), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352).

annotations.label: stigma
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001), usually apical, that is part of a carpel (PO:0009030) or group of fused carpels on which the pollen (PO:0025281) germinates.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), ear floret (PO:0006354), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of ear (PO:0006350), upper floret of sessile spikelet of ear (PO:0006352).

annotations.label: style
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) which is an elongated part of a carpel (PO:0009030) or gynoecium (PO:0009062) between the plant ovary (PO:0009072) and the stigma (PO:0009073), and through which the pollen tube (GO:0090406) grows.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this structure for grasses, please also add an annotation to spikelet floret (PO:0009082). For Zea spp. please use silk (PO:0006488).

annotations.label: staminode
annotations.definition: Sterile stamen.

annotations.label: pistillode
annotations.definition: A sterile pistil, often rudimentary.

synonyms.text: 不稔の雌蕊、退化雌蕊 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: spikelet rachilla
annotations.definition: The axis of the spikelet, above the glumes.

annotations.label: higher order inflorescence axis
annotations.definition: An inflorescence axis (PO:0020122) that develops from an inflorescence axillary meristem (PO:0009105) of an inflorescence axis (PO:0020122).
annotations.comment: A higher order inflorescence axis is any inflorescence axis (PO:0020122) that develops from another inflorescence axis (PO:0020122), and is named according to its position in an inflorescence (PO:0009049). Tenth order inflorescence axis (PO:0025111) is the greatest specific inflorescence axis order in the PO; when mapping annotations to inflorescence axes which are of an order greater than ten, or when order is ambiguous or unknown, annotate directly to higher order inflorescence axis.

annotations.label: spikelet floret
annotations.definition: A small flower (PO:0009046) that is part of a spikelet (PO:0009051).
annotations.comment: A spikelet floret (PO:0009082) is enclosed by one or both the flower bracts (PO:0009034);  lemma (PO:0009037) and palea (PO:0009038) and also includes a lodicule (PO:0009036).  To describe a floret of the Asteraceae, use ray flower (PO:0025331) or disk flower (PO:0025332).  Found in Poaceae, Cyperaceae and other Poales.

annotations.label: pericarp
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the outer layer (wall) of a fruit (PO:0009001), and develops from a plant ovary (PO:0009072) or a carpel (PO:0009030) wall.
annotations.comment: Pericarp comprises endocarp (PO:0009086), mesocarp (PO:0009087), and exocarp (PO:0009085) layers, each of which can be fleshy or dry. If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: exocarp
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the outer layer of a pericarp (PO:0009084).

annotations.label: endocarp
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the innermost differentiated layer of a pericarp (PO:0009084).

annotations.label: mesocarp
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the middle layer of a pericarp (PO:0009084).

annotations.label: seed coat
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue that is the covering of a seed derived from ovular - mainly integumentary - tissue.
annotations.comment: Used only when not specified more precisely; see testa, tegmen.

annotations.label: endosperm
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) which is a maximal portion of nutritive plant tissue in a seed (PO:0009010).
annotations.comment: In angiosperms the endosperm is usually triploid and formed after fertilization by the fusion of one gamete with the polar nucleus (GO:0043078); sometimes diploid or polyploid.

annotations.label: aril
annotations.definition: An arilloid that is an outgrowth of a seed from a seed funicle.
annotations.comment: Usually envelopes most of the seed and is fleshy. If the ovule is pressed against the funicle to form a raphe, it may appear that the aril is growing from the raphe.

annotations.label: involucre
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure composed of two or more involucral bracts at the base of a cluster of flowers, as found in Asteraceae or Compositae.
annotations.comment: The burs or bristles in Cenchrus and Pennisetum are not involucres because the fused structures are branches and not bracts.

annotations.label: long lateral tassel branch
