If you are describing a tepal (PO:0025137) that is part of a perianth (PO:0009058) where none of the tepals (PO:0009033) are fused, you should use tepal.

annotations.label: fused tepal
annotations.definition: A tepal (PO:0009033) that is part of a fused collective tepal structure (PO:0025138) in which it is fused to at least one other tepal.

annotations.label: fused collective tepal structure
annotations.definition: A collective tepal structure (PO:0025021) in which there are at least two fused tepals (PO:0025137).

synonyms.text: estrutura colectiva de t&#233palo fusionado (Spanish, exact)
複数の融合花被片構造 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome apex
annotations.definition: The apical portion of a phyllome.
annotations.comment: It is not precisely defined how far toward the base from the tip a phyllome apex extends. For the apical most portion of a phyllome, use the term phyllome tip.

annotations.label: phyllome base
annotations.definition: The basal part of a phyllome (PO:0006001), where it attaches to a shoot axis (PO:0025029).

annotations.label: phyllome tip
annotations.definition: The apical most portion of a phyllome apex.
annotations.comment: This term is to be used for only the apical most portion of a phyllome. For a larger area, use the term phyllome apex. Use this term when describing the shape of a phyllome tip.

synonyms.text: フィロム 先端 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf tip
annotations.definition: A phyllome tip (PO:0025141) that is part of a leaf apex (PO:0020137).
annotations.comment: This term is to be used for only the apical most portion of a leaf. For a larger area, use the term leaf apex. Use this term when describing the shape of a leaf tip. If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

annotations.label: tepal apex
annotations.definition: A phyllome apex PO:0025139) that is part of a tepal (PO:0009033).
annotations.comment: It is not precisely defined how far toward the base from the tip a tepal apex extends. For the apical most portion of a tepal, use the term tepal tip.

annotations.label: petal apex
annotations.definition: A phyllome apex (PO:0025139) that is part of a petal (PO:0009032).
annotations.comment: It is not precisely defined how far toward the base from the tip a petal apex extends. For the apical most portion of a petal, use the term petal tip.

annotations.label: sepal apex
annotations.definition: A phyllome apex that is part of a sepal.
annotations.comment: It is not precisely defined how far toward the base from the tip a sepal apex extends. For the apical most portion of a sepal, use the term sepal tip.

annotations.label: petal base
annotations.definition: A phyllome base (PO:0025140) that is part of a petal (PO:0009032).

annotations.label: sepal base
annotations.definition: A phyllome base that is part of a sepal.

annotations.label: tepal base
annotations.definition: A phyllome base (PO:0025140) that is part of a tepal (PO:0009033).

annotations.label: petal tip
annotations.definition: A phyllome tip that is part of a petal apex.
annotations.comment: This term is to be used for only the apical most portion of a petal. For a larger area, use the term petal apex. Use this term when describing the shape of a petal tip.

annotations.label: sepal tip
annotations.definition: A phyllome tip that is part of a sepal apex.
annotations.comment: This term is to be used for only the apical most portion of a sepal. For a larger area, use the term sepal apex. Use this term when describing the shape of a sepal tip.

annotations.label: tepal tip
annotations.definition: A phyllome tip that is part of a tepal apex.
annotations.comment: This term is to be used for only the apical most portion of a tepal. For a larger area, use the term tepal apex. Use this term when describing the shape of a tepal tip.

annotations.label: peduncle epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of branch epidermis (PO:0025179) that is part of a peduncle (PO:0009053).

annotations.label: peduncle trichome
annotations.definition: A shoot axis trichome that is part of a peduncle epidermis.

synonyms.text: 花梗毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: bract apex
annotations.definition: A phyllome apex (PO:0025139) that is part of a bract (PO:0009055).
annotations.comment: It is not precisely defined how far toward the base from the tip a bract apex extends. For the apical most portion of a bract, use the term bract tip.

annotations.label: bract base
annotations.definition: A phyllome base (PO:0025140) that is part of a bract (PO:0009055).

annotations.label: bract tip
annotations.definition: A phyllome tip that is part of a bract apex.
annotations.comment: This term is to be used for only the apical most portion of a bract. For a larger area, use the term bract apex. Use this term when describing the shape of a bract tip.

annotations.label: phyllome epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot system epidermis (PO:0006035) that is part of a phyllome (PO:0006001).

annotations.label: bract epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of phyllome epidermis (PO:0025157) that is part of a bract (PO:0009055).

annotations.label: bract abaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of bract epidermis (PO:0025158) that covers the abaxial/lower surface of a bract (PO:0009055).

synonyms.text: epidermis abaxial de la br&#225ctea (Spanish, exact)
苞背軸側 表皮 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: bract adaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of bract epidermis (PO:0025158) that covers the adaxial/upper surface of a bract (PO:0009055).

synonyms.text: epidermis adaxail de la br&#225ctea (Spanish, exact)
苞向軸側 表皮 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: portion of plant substance
annotations.definition: A portion of organism substance that is or was part of a plant.
annotations.comment: Refers to organism substance (CARO:0000004) which is defined as: Material anatomical entity in a gaseous, liquid, semisolid or solid state; produced by anatomical structures or derived from inhaled and ingested substances that have been modified by anatomical structures as they pass through the body and that does not have inherent 3D shape generated by coordinated expression of the organism's own genome. For plants, the word "body" refers to a whole plant, or part thereof.  Includes substances such as latex, xylem sap, phloem sap, resin, mucilage, oil, cuticle, cutin, and sporopollenin.

synonyms.text: 植物を構成する物質の一部 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: multicellular trichome
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that forms a non-sclerified outgrowth from the epidermis (PO:0005679).
annotations.comment: A multicellular trichome (PO:0000282) has as parts more than one trichome cell (PO:0008030), may vary in size and complexity, be branched and may contain a secretory gland.

synonyms.text: 多細胞毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: multicellular trichome branch cell
annotations.definition: A trichome cell (PO:0008030) that forms a branch of a multicellular trichome (PO:0025162).
annotations.comment: For branches of a unicellular trichome, see unicellular trichome branch (GO:0090405) in GO.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula ramificada de tricoma multicelular (Spanish, exact)
多細胞毛（毛茸、糸状体）分枝細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: root epidermal cell
annotations.definition: A plant epidermal cell (PO:0004013) that is part of a root epidermis (PO:0006036).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula epid&#233rmica de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: shoot epidermal cell
annotations.definition: A plant epidermal cell (PO:0004013) that is part of a shoot axis epidermis (PO:0000112).

synonyms.text: シュート表皮 細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: papilla cell
annotations.definition: An epidermal pavement cell with a short protuberance.
annotations.comment: See plant cell papilla (GO:0090395) for the subcellular component papilla.

annotations.label: leaf papilla cell
annotations.definition: A papilla cell that is part of a leaf epidermis.
annotations.comment: See leaf papilla (GO:0090396) for the subcellular component leaf papilla. If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula papilosa de la epidermis (Spanish, exact)
葉乳頭毛細胞（パピラ） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: stigma papilla cell
annotations.definition: A papilla cell that is part of a stigma epidermis.
annotations.comment: See stigma papilla (GO:0090397) for the subcellular component stigma papilla.

annotations.label: prickle
annotations.definition: A portion of epidermis (PO:0005679) that is a sclerified outgrowth.
annotations.comment: Often found on stems and leaves.

annotations.label: root prickle
annotations.definition: A prickle (PO:0025169) that is part of a root (PO:0009005)

annotations.label: shoot axis prickle
annotations.definition: A prickle (PO:0025169) that is part of a shoot axis (PO:0025029).

synonyms.text: espinilla del eje del epiblasto (epiblastema) (Spanish, exact)
シュート軸表皮のとげ (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: thorn
annotations.definition: A branch that is a sclerified, pointed outgrowth.
annotations.comment: A thorn is a short, pointed (PATO:0002258) woody structure, may be branched.

annotations.label: spine leaf
annotations.definition: A leaf that is a sclerified and pointed and lacks a lamina.
annotations.comment: See also stipule spine (PO:0025174).

annotations.label: spine stipule
annotations.definition: A stipule that is sclerified and pointed.
annotations.comment: See also leaf spine (PO:0025173).

annotations.label: leaf prickle
annotations.definition: A prickle (PO:0025169) that is part of a leaf (PO:0025034).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

annotations.label: stem trichome
annotations.definition: A shoot axis trichome that is part of a stem epidermis.

annotations.label: branch trichome
annotations.definition: A shoot axis trichome that is part of a branch epidermis.

annotations.label: stem epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot axis epidermis (PO:0000112) that is part of a stem (PO:0009047).

annotations.label: branch epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot axis epidermis (PO:0006035) that is part of a branch (PO:0025073).

annotations.label: plant axis elongation zone
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) which is part of a plant axis (PO:0025004), is located proximal to an apical meristem (PO:0020144), and in which plant cells (PO:0009002) are undergoing longitudinal expansion.

synonyms.text: zona de elongaci&#243n del eje de la planta (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: root elongation zone
annotations.definition: A plant axis elongation zone (PO:0025180) that is part of a root tip (PO:0000025) and located proximal to a root apical meristem (PO:0020147).
annotations.comment: The root elongation zone is only a few millimeters in length and is the only portion of the root in which cell elongation generally occurs. Root hairs are not yet produced in this region. Primary root tissues begin to differentiate in the root elongation zone. This term replaces elongation zone (PO:0020125).

synonyms.text: zona de elongaci&#243n de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: sepal trichome
annotations.definition: A phyllome trichome that is part of a sepal epidermis.

synonyms.text: tricoma de la epidermis del s&#233palo (Spanish, exact)
がく片毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A phyllome stomatal complex (PO:0025215) that is part of a leaf.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: complejo estom&#225tico de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: tepal epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of phyllome epidermis (PO:0025157) that is part of a tepal (PO:0009033).

annotations.label: leaf lamina stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A leaf stomatal complex that is part of a leaf lamina.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: complejo estom&#225tico de la l&#225mina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome trichome
annotations.definition: A trichome that is part of a phyllome epidermis.

synonyms.text: フィロム毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: petal trichome
annotations.definition: A phyllome trichome that is part of a petal epidermis.

synonyms.text: 花弁毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: tepal trichome
annotations.definition: A phyllome trichome that is part of a tepal epidermis.

synonyms.text: tricoma de la epidermis del t&#233palo (Spanish, exact)
花被片毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: petiole stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A leaf stomatal complex that is part of a petiole.

synonyms.text: 葉柄 気孔装置 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf abaxial stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A leaf stomatal complex (PO:0025183) that is part of a leaf abaxial epidermis (PO:0006019).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: complejo estom&#225tico de la superficie abaxial de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉背軸側気孔装置 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf adaxial stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A leaf stomatal complex that is part of a leaf adaxial epidermis.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: complejo estom&#225tico de la superficie adaxial de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉向軸側気孔装置 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: tepal abaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of tepal epidermis (PO:0025184) that covers the abaxial/lower surface of a tepal (PO:0009033).

synonyms.text: 花被片背軸側 表皮 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: tepal adaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of tepal epidermis (PO:0025184) that covers the adaxial/upper surface of a tepal (PO:0009033).

synonyms.text: 花被片向軸側 表皮 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: root nodule meristem
annotations.definition: A root meristem (PO:0006085) that gives rise to a root nodule (PO:0003023).

synonyms.text: meristema del n&#243dulo de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: pollen tube cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that develops from a microgametophyte vegetative cell (PO:0020099) and forms a tubular extension of the pollen (PO:0025281).
annotations.comment: Carries the male gametes (PO:0025006) into or near the plant ovule (PO:0020003). May be branched in gymnosperms. See GO for pollen tube (GO:0090406) or pollen tube tip (GO:0090404).

annotations.label: basal endosperm transfer layer
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is part of an endosperm (PO:0009089) and is composed of basal endosperm transfer cells (PO:0009018).
annotations.comment: Involved in transferring nutrients to a plant embryo (PO:0009009).

synonyms.text: capa de transferencia del endosperma basal (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: stele
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is the central column of a plant axis that consists of the primary vascular tissue and associated ground tissue.
annotations.comment: If a pith is present, it is part of the stele, otherwise the stele only includes the primary vascular tissue and the ground tissue that surrounds it.

annotations.label: shoot axis stele
annotations.definition: A stele that is part of a shoot axis.

synonyms.text: estela del eje del epiblasto (epiblastema) (Spanish, exact)
シュート軸中心柱 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: branch stele
annotations.definition: A shoot axis stele that is part of a branch.

annotations.label: stem stele
annotations.definition: A shoot axis stele that is part of a stem.

annotations.label: megasporangium
annotations.definition: A sporangium in which megaspores are produced.

annotations.label: microsporangium
annotations.definition: A sporangium (PO:0025094) in which microspores (PO:0020048) are produced.

annotations.label: pericarp vascular bundle
annotations.definition: A vascular bundle (PO:0005020) that is part of a pericarp (PO:0009084).

annotations.label: rhizome internode
annotations.definition: A shoot axis internode (PO:0005005) that is part of a rhizome (PO:0004542).

annotations.label: shoot system vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that is part of a shoot system (PO:0009006).

synonyms.text: sistema vascular del sistema del epiblasto (epiblastema) (Spanish, exact)
シュート系維管系 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome vascular system
annotations.definition: A vascular system (PO:0000034) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a phyllome (PO:0006001).

annotations.label: tepal vascular system
annotations.definition: A phyllome vascular system (PO:0025206) that includes the totality of the portions of vascular tissue in their specific arrangement in a tepal (PO:0009033).

annotations.label: carpel trichome
annotations.definition: A phyllome trichome (PO:0025186) that is part of a carpel epidermis (PO:0025096).

synonyms.text: 心皮毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: stamen trichome
annotations.definition: A phyllome trichome that is part of a stamen epidermis.

synonyms.text: 雄蕊毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf abaxial trichome
annotations.definition: A leaf trichome that is part of a leaf abaxial epidermis.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: tricoma de la superficie abaxial de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉背軸側毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf adaxial trichome
annotations.definition: A leaf trichome that is part of a leaf adaxial epidermis.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: tricoma de la superficie adaxial de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉向軸側毛（毛茸、糸状体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf pavement cell
annotations.definition: An epidermal pavement cell that is part of a leaf epidermis.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula de pavimento de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: leaf abaxial pavement cell
annotations.definition: A leaf pavement cell that is part of a leaf abaxial epidermis.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula de pavimento de la superficie abaxial de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉背軸側被蓋細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf adaxial pavement cell
annotations.definition: A leaf pavement cell that is part of a leaf adaxial epidermis.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula de pavimento de la superficie adaxial de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉向軸側被蓋細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A stomatal complex (PO:0002000) that is part of a phyllome (PO:0006001).

synonyms.text: フィロム 気孔装置  (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: bract stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A phyllome stomatal complex (PO:0025215) that is part of a bract (PO:0009055).

synonyms.text: complejo estom&#225tico de la br&#225ctea (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: carpel stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A phyllome stomatal complex that is part of a carpel.

annotations.label: petal stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A phyllome stomatal complex (PO:0025215) that is part of a petal (PO:0009032).

annotations.label: sepal stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A phyllome stomatal complex that is part of a sepal.

annotations.label: stamen stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A phyllome stomatal complex that is part of a stamen.

annotations.label: tepal stomatal complex
annotations.definition: A phyllome stomatal complex that is part of a tepal (PO:0009033).

annotations.label: reproductive shoot apex
annotations.definition: A shoot apex that has as part a reproductive shoot apical meristem.

synonyms.text: 生殖シュート頂、茎頂 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: vegetative shoot apex
annotations.definition: A shoot apex PO:0000037) that has as part a vegetative shoot apical meristem (PO:0008016).

synonyms.text: 栄養シュート頂、栄養枝頂 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: axil
annotations.definition: A plant anatomical space that is the space between a shoot axis and a leaf, bract, or branch that branches from the shoot axis.

annotations.label: bract axil
annotations.definition: An axil that is the space between a shoot axis and a bract that branches from the shoot axis.

annotations.label: branch axil
annotations.definition: An axil that is the space between a shoot axis and a branch that branches from the shoot axis.

annotations.label: tetrad of megaspores
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue consisting of four megaspores that remained joined together shortly after meiosis. One cell will give rise to a female gametophyte.
annotations.comment: Usually only one of the four megaspores develops and the other three die.

annotations.label: valve
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part that is a part of a plant structure that splits apart when the structure dehisces.

annotations.label: spore capsule valve
annotations.definition: A valve that is a part of a spore capsule that splits apart when the spore capsule dehisces.
annotations.comment: Found in bryophytes. A spore capsule may dehisce ongitudinally, forming evenely sized valves, or it may dehisce at the capsule annulus, forming the operculum, which is a specialized type of valve.

synonyms.text: valva de la c&#225psula de musgo (Spanish, exact)
コケ類　蒴（さく）弁 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: columella
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that forms the central axis of a plant structure such as a fruit (PO:0009001) or moss capsule (sporangium; PO:0025094).

annotations.label: spore capsule columella
annotations.definition: A columella (PO:0025230) that forms the central axis of a spore capsule.
annotations.comment: Found in bryophytes.

synonyms.text: columela de la c&#225psula de musgo (Spanish, exact)
コケ類　蒴（さく）軸柱 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: portion of embryo plant tissue
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is part of a plant embryo (PO:0009009).
annotations.comment: This class is for tissues that only occur as part of an embryo.

synonyms.text: 植物胚性組織 の一部   (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome stomatal pore
annotations.definition: A stomatal pore that is part of a phyllome stomatal complex.

synonyms.text: フィロム 気孔開口部 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf stomatal pore
annotations.definition: A phyllome stomatal pore that is part of a leaf stomatal complex.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: poro estom&#225tico de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome guard cell
annotations.definition: A guard cell that is part of a phyllome stomatal complex.

synonyms.text: フィロム孔辺細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf guard cell
annotations.definition: A phyllome guard cell that is part of a leaf stomatal complex.
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula guardiana de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: abaxial petiole canal
annotations.definition: A petiole canal on the abaxial surface of a petiole.

annotations.label: adaxial petiole canal
annotations.definition: A petiole canal on the adaxial surface of a petiole.

annotations.label: second order inflorescence
annotations.definition: A reproductive shoot system (PO:0025082) that is part of an inflorescence (PO:0009049) and has as parts a second order inflorescence axis (PO:0006321) and any shoot axes (PO:0025029) and flowers (PO:0009046) that arise from it.
annotations.comment: This a sub-inflorescence that arises from the main inflorescence axis.

annotations.label: second order infructescence
annotations.definition: A reproductive shoot system that is part of an infructescence and has as parts a second order infructescence axis and any infructescence axes and fruits that arise from it.

annotations.label: infructescence axis
annotations.definition: A shoot axis (PO:0025029) that is part of an infructescence (PO:0006342).

annotations.label: higher order infructescence axis
annotations.definition: An infructescence axis (PO:0025242), which is a branch (PO:0025073) in an infructescence (PO:0006342), that develops from a higher order inflorescence axis (PO:0009081).
annotations.comment: A higher order infructescence axis is named according to its position in an infructescence (PO:0006342). Tenth order infructescence axis (PO:0025112) is the greatest specific infructescence axis (PO:0025242) order in the PO; when mapping annotations to an infructescence axis which is of an order higher than ten, or when order is ambiguous or unknown, annotate directly to higher order infructescence axis (PO:0025243).

annotations.label: first order infructescence axis
annotations.definition: An infructescence axis (PO:0025242), which is the primary or main axis of an infructescence (PO:0006342), that develops from a first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104).

synonyms.text: eje de la infructescencia de primer orden (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: persistent sepal
annotations.definition: A phyllome that develops from a sepal and remains attached to a fruit at maturity.
annotations.comment: May be alive or dead at maturity.

annotations.label: persistent tepal
annotations.definition: A phyllome (PO:0006001) that develops from a tepal (PO:0009033) and remains attached to a fruit (PO:0009001) at maturity.
annotations.comment: May be alive or dead at maturity.

annotations.label: persistent stamen
annotations.definition: A phyllome that develops from a stamen and remains attached to a fruit at maturity.
annotations.comment: May be alive or dead at maturity.

annotations.label: pseudostem
annotations.definition: A collective organ part structure that forms a cylindrical false stem composed of concentric leaf sheaths.
annotations.comment: The peduncle later grows up through the pseudostem. Common in Musa species and some other monocots.

annotations.label: leaf sheath epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of leaf epidermis (PO:0006016) that is part of a leaf sheath (PO:0020104).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: epidermis de la vaina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: leaf sheath abaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of leaf sheath epidermis (PO:0025249) that covers the abaxial/lower surface of a leaf sheath (PO:0020104).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: epidermis abaxial de la vaina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: leaf sheath adaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of leaf sheath epidermis (PO:0025249) that covers the adaxial/upper surface of a leaf sheath (PO:0020104).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to vascular leaf (PO:0009025) or non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075), depending on the species. All annotations for angiosperms, gymnosperms, and pteridophytes should go to vascular leaf and all annotations for bryophytes should go to non-vascular leaf.

synonyms.text: epidermis adaxial de la vaina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: seventh order infructescence axis
annotations.definition: A higher order infructescence axis (PO:0025243) which arises from a sixth order infructescence axis (PO:0025116) and develops from a seventh order inflorescence axis (PO:0025108).

synonyms.text: eje de séptimo orden de la infructescencia (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: eighth order infructescence axis
annotations.definition: A higher order infructescence axis (PO:0025243) which arises from a seventh order infructescence axis (PO:0025252) and develops from an eighth order inflorescence axis (PO:0025109).

synonyms.text: eje de octavo orden de la infructescencia (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: ninth order infructescence axis
annotations.definition: A higher order infructescence axis (PO:0025243) which arises from an eighth order infructescence axis (PO:0025253) and develops from a ninth order inflorescence axis (PO:0025110).

synonyms.text: eje de noveno orden de la infructescencia (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: tenth order infructescence axis
annotations.definition: A higher order infructescence axis (PO:0025243) which arises from a ninth order infructescence axis (PO:0025254) and develops from a tenth order inflorescence axis (PO:0025111).
annotations.comment: Tenth order infructescence axis (PO:0025112) is the greatest specific infructescence axis (PO:0025242) order in the PO; when mapping annotations to an infructescence axis which is of an order higher than ten, or when order is ambiguous or unknown, annotate directly to higher order infructescence axis (PO:0025243).

synonyms.text: eje de decimo orden de la infructescencia (Spanish, exact)
ninth or higher order infructescence branch (exact)
第十以上の果序軸 (Japanese, related)

annotations.label: lateral root elongation zone
annotations.definition: A root elongation zone that is part of a lateral root tip.

synonyms.text: zona de elongaci&#243n de la ra&#237z lateral (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: primary root elongation zone
annotations.definition: A root elongation zone (PO:0025181) that is part of a primary root tip (PO:0000026).

synonyms.text: zona de elongaci&#243n de la ra&#237z primaria (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: procambial cell
annotations.definition: A meristematic cell (PO:0004010) that is part of a portion of procambium (PO:0025275).

annotations.label: leaf procambium
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot procambium (PO:0025276) tissue that is part of a vascular leaf (PO:0009025).

annotations.label: shoot axis procambium
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot procambium that is part of a shoot apical meristem and gives rise to the primary vascular tissue of a shoot axis.

synonyms.text: procambio del eje del epiblasto (Spanish, exact)
シュート軸前形成層 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: pericycle cell
annotations.definition: A ground tissue cell that is part of a portion of pericycle.

annotations.label: plant ovary septum
annotations.definition: A septum (PO:0000030) that divides a multilocular plant ovary (PO:0009072).
annotations.comment: Found in species with syncarpous gynoecia (fused carpels). A plant ovary septum is derived from the fused walls of two adjacent carpels, while a replum (PO:0005016) develops from early contact of placental tissue from opposite sides of the plant ovary. Annotations for Arabidopsis should go under replum, not under plant ovary septum.

annotations.label: locule
annotations.definition: A plant anatomical space (PO:0025117) that is a cavity a reproductive structure.
annotations.comment: May be a cavity in a sporophyll, within several fused sporophylls, or within a sporangium. In many instances, the locules are filled with seeds or other plant structures.

annotations.label: anther locule
annotations.definition: A sporangium locule that is part of an anther and is a cavity within a single pollen sac or two or more fused pollen sacs.

annotations.label: sporangium locule
annotations.definition: A locule (PO:0025263) that is a cavity in a sporangium (PO:0025094) or several fused sporangia.
annotations.comment: May be filled with spores.

annotations.label: plant ovary locule
annotations.definition: A locule (PO:0025263) that is a cavity in a plant ovary (PO:0009072).
annotations.comment: Generally contains at least one ovule. An ovary locule may be part of a single carpel, in the case of unicarpellate flowers. An ovary that is part of a syncarpous gynoecium may have multiple locules, or the anatomical spaces of multiple carpels may be fused to form a single locule.

annotations.label: fruit replum
annotations.definition: A portion of fruit placenta tissue  (PO:0004535) that divides a fruit (PO:0009001) into two or more chambers and develops from a plant ovary replum (PO:0025272).
annotations.comment: A fruit replum develops from the ovary replum and has no suture line in its center, while a fruit septum (PO:0025262) develops from an ovary septum via the fusion of two adjacent ovary walls. Common in Brassicaceae. Annotations for Arabidopsis fruit should go here, and not under fruit septum. If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: fruit septum
annotations.definition: A septum that divides a cavity or mass of tissue in a fruit and develops from an ovary septum.
annotations.comment: Annotations for Arabidopsis should go under fruit replum (PO:0025267), not under fruit septum. The partition between two fruit locules of an orange is a fruit septum. If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: collective organ part structure
annotations.definition: A collective plant structure (PO:0025497) composed of two or more cardinal organ parts (PO:0025001) that are part of adjacent plant organs (PO:0009008) and any associated portions of plant tissue (PO:0009007).
annotations.comment: A collective organ part structure is composed of parts of multiple organs, but no complete organs. The organ parts are often of the same type but may be of different types. Includes plant structures like a septum (PO:0000030) that consists of the walls of two fused carpels (PO:0009030) or a pseudostem (PO:0025248) that consist of multiple, overlapping leaf sheaths (PO:0020104). Unlike a collective plant organ structure (PO:0025007), a collective organ part structure does not contain any complete plant organs.

synonyms.text: &#243rgano colectivo de una parte de la estructura (Spanish, exact)
集合的器官の部分構造 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: fruit locule
annotations.definition: A locule (PO:0025263) that is a cavity in a fruit (PO:0009001) and develops from a plant ovary locule (PO:0025266).
annotations.comment: If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: plant ovary replum
annotations.definition: A portion of ovary placenta tissue (PO:0020001) which divides a plant ovary (PO:0009072) into two or more chambers, and develops from contact of the placental tissue from opposite sides of the plant ovary early in gynoecium development (GO:0048467).
annotations.comment: An ovary replum develops along with the ovary and has no suture line in its center, while a true ovary septum (PO:0025262) is derived from the fusion two adjacent ovary walls. Common in Brassicaceae. Annotations for Arabidopsis should go here, and not under ovary septum.

annotations.label: stem procambium
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot axis procambium gives rise to the primary vascular tissue of a stem.

annotations.label: branch procambium
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot axis procambium gives rise to the primary vascular tissue of a branch.

annotations.label: procambium
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) that undergoes differentiation to form the primary vascular tissue (PO:0025408).
annotations.comment: May occur in an apical meristem or in a developing phyllome. Gives rise to the primary xylem and primary phloem. This term replaces PO:0006074.

annotations.label: shoot procambium
annotations.definition: A portion of procambium (PO:0025275) tissue that is part of a shoot system (PO:0009006).

synonyms.text: シュート前形成層 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: pollen sac
annotations.definition: A microsporangium that is part of a sporophyll and where the pollen grains develop and are located after they develop.
annotations.comment: A pollen sac is a microsporangium in seed plants. In angiosperms, a single, unfused pollen sac may contain an anther locule or several pollen sacs may fuse so they contain a single anther locule. If you are annotating to this structure for Zea mays or other grasses, please also add an annotation to the corresponding floret type. Choose the most specific term possible from: spikelet floret (PO:0009082), tassel floret (PO:0006310), lower floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006313), lower floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006315), upper floret of pedicellate spikelet of tassel (PO:0006314), upper floret of sessile spikelet of tassel (PO:0006316).

annotations.label: rhizome scale leaf
annotations.definition: A scale leaf that grows from a rhizome.

annotations.label: megagametophyte
annotations.definition: A whole plant in the gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) that produces only plant egg cells (PO:0020094).
annotations.comment: Megagametophytes in bryophytes only produce egg cells, but do not develop from megaspores, because there is no heterospory in bryophytes. In some pteridophytes, megagametophytes develop from megaspores. In angiosperms, Gnetum, and Weltwischia, the megagametophyte is greatly reduced. The megagametophyte in angiosperms is an embryo sac.

annotations.label: microgametophyte
annotations.definition: A whole plant (PO:0000003) in the gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) that produces plant sperm cells (PO:0000084).
annotations.comment: The microgametophyte in bryophytes only produce sperm cells, but do not develop from microspores, because there is no heterospory in bryophytes. In seed plants the male gametophyte is reduced to a pollen grain.

annotations.label: pollen
annotations.definition: A microgametophyte (PO:0025280) that develops from a microspore (PO:0020048) is located in a pollen sac (PO:0025277).

annotations.label: archegonium megagametophyte
annotations.definition: A megagametophyte (PO:0025279) that has as parts one or more archegonia (PO:0025126).
annotations.comment: Megagametophytes in bryophytes only produce egg cells, but do not develop from megaspores, because there is no heterospory in bryophytes. In some pteridophytes, megagametophytes develop from megaspores.

annotations.label: antheridium microgametophyte
annotations.definition: A microgametophyte (PO:0025280) that has as parts one or more antheridia (PO:0025125).
annotations.comment: Microgametophytes in bryophytes only produce sperm cells, but do not develop from microspores, because there is no heterospory in bryophytes. In some pteridophytes, microgametophytes develop from microspores.

synonyms.text: 造精器小胞子体（小配偶体） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: embryo apical cell
annotations.definition: A sporophyte meristematic apical cell that is part of a plant embryo and is the uppermost cell formed after the first division of a zygote.
annotations.comment: The zygote divides to form an embryo apical cell and an embryo basal cell.

annotations.label: callus parenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A parenchyma cell that is part of a plant callus.

synonyms.text: カルス柔組織細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: plant embryo coleoptile
annotations.definition: A coleoptile (PO:0020033) that is part of a plant embryo (PO:0009009).

annotations.label: seedling coleoptile
annotations.definition: A coleoptile (PO:0020033) that is part of a seedling (PO:0008037).
annotations.comment: A seedling coleoptile develops from an embryo coleoptile (PO:0025286).

annotations.label: embryo coleorhiza
annotations.definition: A coleorhiza (PO:0020034) that is part of a plant embryo (PO:0009009).

annotations.label: seedling coleorhiza
annotations.definition: A coleorhiza (PO:0020034) that is part of a seedling (PO:0008037).
annotations.comment: Develops from an embryo coleorhiza (PO:0025288).

annotations.label: embryo hypocotyl
annotations.definition: A hypocotyl (PO:0020100) that is part of a plant embryo axis (PO:0019018).

annotations.label: seedling hypocotyl
annotations.definition: A hypocotyl (PO:0020100) that is part of a seedling (PO:0008037).
annotations.comment: Develops from an embryo hypocotyl (PO:0025290). This term should only be used for seed plants.

annotations.label: embryo epicotyl
annotations.definition: An epicotyl (PO:0020035) that is part of a plant embryo (PO:0009009).

annotations.label: seedling epicotyl
annotations.definition: An epicotyl (PO:0020035) that is part of a seedling (PO:0008037).
annotations.comment: Develops from an embryo epicotyl (PO:0025292).

annotations.label: embryo mesocotyl
annotations.definition: A mesocotyl (PO:0020037) that is part of a plant embryo (PO:0009009).

annotations.label: seedling mesocotyl
annotations.definition: A mesocotyl (PO:0020037) that develops from an embryo mesocotyl (PO:0025294) and is part of a seedling (PO:0008037).
annotations.comment: A seedling mesocotyl is an elongation of an embryo mesocotyl (PO:0025294) and is part of a seedling (PO:0008037)..

annotations.label: plant embryo radicle
annotations.definition: A radicle (PO:0020031) that is part of a plant embryo (PO:0009009).
annotations.comment: May eventually develop into the root system (PO:0025025)  of an adult plant. Sometimes is abortive.

annotations.label: seedling radicle
annotations.definition: A radicle (PO:0020031) that is part of a seedling (PO:0008037).
annotations.comment: Develops from an embryo radicle (PO:0025296). May develop into the root system (PO:0025025) of an adult plant.

annotations.label: fruit operculum
annotations.definition: A collective organ part structure that is the apical part of a circumsessile capsular fruit that separates from the rest of the capsule during dehiscence.
annotations.comment: Develops from the apical portion of a gynoecium, and is commonly found as part of the fruit of species of Myrtaceae, such as Eucalyptus spp. If you are annotating to this term, please add an additional annotation to the specific type of fruit (PO:0009001), such as achene fruit (PO:0030107), berry fruit (PO:0030108), capsule fruit (PO:0030091), caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104), drupe fruit (PO:0030103), follicle fruit (PO:0030105), legume fruit (PO:0030100), nut fruit (PO:0030102), samara fruit (PO:0030099), schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098), silique fruit (PO:0030106), or their subclasses, depending on the species.

annotations.label: embryo hypocotyl-root junction
annotations.definition: A hypocotyl-root junction (PO:0004724) that is part of an plant embryo axis (PO:0019018).

synonyms.text: conjunci&#243n del hipoc&#243tile-ra&#237z en el embri&#243n (Spanish, exact)
胚下胚軸-根合流点 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: seedling hypocotyl-root junction
annotations.definition: A hypocotyl-root junction (PO:0004724) that develops from an embryo hypocotyl-root junction (PO:0025300) and is part of a seedling (PO:0008037).
annotations.comment: Part of a seedling (PO:0008037).

synonyms.text: conjunci&#243n del hipoc&#243tile-ra&#237z en la pl&#225ntula (Spanish, exact)
実生胚軸-根合流点 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: somatic plant embryo
annotations.definition: A plant embryo (PO:0009009) that arises from previously differentiated somatic cells, rather than from fused gametes, i.e. the plant zygote (PO:0000423).
annotations.comment: Somatic plant embryos may arise where embryos normally would not arise as a product of apomixis. They may occur on the edges of leaves on some species of Kalanchoe or some ferns.

annotations.label: zygotic plant embryo
annotations.definition: A plant embryo (PO:0009009) that forms as a result of the fusion of gametes.

annotations.label: cultured zygote-derived plant embryo
annotations.definition: A zygotic plant embryo that is grown or maintained in vitro.

synonyms.text: embri&#243n cultivado derivado del cigoto vegetal (Spanish, exact)
培養接合子由来植物胚 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: microspore-derived cultured plant embryo
annotations.definition: A cultured somatic plant embryo that develops from isolated microspores.

synonyms.text: embri&#243n cultivado derivado de la microspora vegetal (Spanish, exact)
小胞子由来培養植物胚 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: sporangium wall
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is composed of the outer layers of a sporangium (PO:0025094) and bounds the archesporium (PO:0030074) or plant spores (PO:0025017).
annotations.comment: May have multiple layers such as exothecium, endothecium and tapetum. Early in development, the sporangium wall bounds the archesporium. Later, after spores develop from the archesporium, it bounds the spore or spores.

annotations.label: microsporangium wall
annotations.definition: A sporangium wall (PO:0025306) that is part of a microsporangium (PO:0025202).

annotations.label: megasporangium wall
annotations.definition: A sporangium wall that is part of a megasporangium.

annotations.label: microsporangium endothecium
annotations.definition: A sporangium wall endothecium (PO:0030049) that is part of a microsporangium wall (PO:0025307).

annotations.label: megasporangium endothecium
annotations.definition: A sporangium wall endothecium (PO:0030049) that is part of a megasporangium wall.

annotations.label: microsporangium exothecium
annotations.definition: A portion of exothecium (PO:0030073) that is part of a microsporangium wall (PO:0025307).

annotations.label: megasporangium exothecium
annotations.definition: A portion of exothecium (PO:0030073) that is part of a megasporangium wall (PO:0025308).

annotations.label: tapetum
annotations.definition: A portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059) that is the inner-most layer of a sporangium wall (PO:0025306).
annotations.comment: May function in providing nutrition to developing sporocytes or pollen. May have one or more layers of cells. May break down as spores mature. A tapetum does not develop in some plants, such as Psilotum. In angiosperms, the tapetum develops from one of the parietal layers. In non-seed plants, the tapetum may develop from the outer cells of archesporium or from the inner-most layer of the sporangium wall. The tapetum may be one of two types ameboid or secretory/glandular.

synonyms.text: 絨毯組織、タペート組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: microsporangium tapetum
annotations.definition: An tapetum (PO:0025313) that is part of a microsporangium wall (PO:0025307).

synonyms.text: 小胞子嚢絨毯組織（タペート組織） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: megasporangium tapetum
annotations.definition: A tapetum (PO:0025313) that is part of a megasporangium wall (PO:0025308).

synonyms.text: 大胞子嚢嚢絨毯組織（タペート組織） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: axillary hair
annotations.definition: A multicellular trichome (PO:0025162) that has as parts a long terminal cell atop a basal stalk and grows in a leaf axil (PO:0009023).
annotations.comment: Found in monocots and mosses. The basal stalk may contain one or more cells.

annotations.label: axillary hair terminal cell
annotations.definition: A trichome cell (PO:0008030) that is the long terminal cell of an axillary hair (PO:0008030).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula terminal del pelo axilar (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: axillary hair base
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the basal part of an axillary hair (PO:0025316), below the axillary hair terminal cell (PO:0025317).
annotations.comment: Consists of two or more plant cells.

annotations.label: axillary hair basal cell
annotations.definition: A trichome cell (PO:0008030) that is part of a base of an axillary hair (PO:0025316).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula basal del pelo axilar (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: cotyledonary node rhizoid
annotations.definition: An epidermal rhizoid that grows form a cotyledonary node.
annotations.comment: Found in some monocots.

synonyms.text: rizoide del nodo del cotiled&#243n (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: cotyledonary node
annotations.definition: A stem node (PO:0020141) from which cotyledons (PO:0020030) may develop.

annotations.label: hydathode pore
annotations.definition: A plant anatomical space that is an opening in a hydathode.
annotations.comment: Similar to a stomatal pore, but lacks the ability to open and close. Involved in the release water or xylem sap through guttation or secretion.

annotations.label: flower fascicle
annotations.definition: A reproductive shoot system (PO:0025082) that is a second order inflorescence (PO:0025240), or higher, in which the inflorescence branch (PO:0009081) bears two or more flowers (PO:0009046), but is not elongated.
annotations.comment: A fascicle appears to be a cluster of flowers in an axil of a single bract of the main inflorescence. Fascicles are common in some sections of the Fabaceae, where they are second order inflorescences.

annotations.label: banner petal
annotations.definition: A petal (PO:0009032) that is the top-most petal of a papilionaceous corolla (PO:0009059).
annotations.comment: Usually larger than the adjacent wing petals. Found in the flowers of some Fabaceae.

annotations.label: wing petal
annotations.definition: One of two lateral petals (PO:0009032) that is adjacent to the banner petal of a papilionaceous corolla (PO:0009059).
annotations.comment: The wing petals are usually much smaller than the banner petal and the corolla keel. Found in the flowers of some Fabaceae.

annotations.label: keel petal
annotations.definition: A petal (PO:0009032) that is one of the two lowest petals of a papilionaceous corolla (PO:0025324).
annotations.comment: The keel petals are usually larger than the adjacent wing petals. They are often partially fused to each other. Each petal is shaped like half a boat and projects toward the front of the flower.

annotations.label: keel
annotations.definition: A boat-shaped collective phyllome structure (PO:0025023) that consists of the two lowest petals (PO:0009032) of a papilionaceous corolla (PO:0009059).
annotations.comment: The two petals of the keel are often fused at the apex and free at the base. The keel projects toward the front of the flower. Found in the flowers of some Fabaceae.

synonyms.text: 竜骨（イネ科）、舟弁（マメ科） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: unifacial vascular leaf
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf that has increased activity of either the adaxial or abaxial meristem early in development, leading to absence of the opposite surface on the mature leaf.
annotations.comment: A unifacial leaf may be round in cross-section (terete) or it may be laminar (ensiform), in which case lamina development is in a median plane (perpendicular to the axis), rather than a transverse plane (tangent to the axis). Unifacial leaves may be bifacial at the leaf base. Many unifacial leaves develop by reduced (or absent) activity of the marginal meristems and increased activity of the adaxial meristem early in development, leading to mature leaves with only an adaxial surface (e.g., Acacia phyllodes). However, some develop by increased activity of the abaxial meristem early in development, leading to mature leaves with only an abaxial surface. Note that unifacial leaves may have two sides, as in ensiform leaves, but both sides arise from the same surface of the leaf meristem (abaxial or adaxial).

annotations.label: calyptra calyx
annotations.definition: A fused calyx (PO:0025582) that is composed of fused sepals (PO:0025584).
annotations.comment: Sometimes found in Eucalyptus and other Myrtaceae, covering an inner operculum or calyptra corolla. The sepals are generally completely fused. Species of the subgenus Monocalyptrus have only a calyptra corolla (inner operculum) while in most other species, a calyptra calyx is also present, at least early in flower development. Not the same structure as a spore capsule calyptra or fruit operculum. Sometimes erroneously referred to as part of a fruit.

annotations.label: calyptra corolla
annotations.definition: A fused corolla (PO:0025581) that is composed of fused petals (PO:0025583).
annotations.comment: Often found in Eucalyptus and other Myrtaceae. The petals are generally completely fused. If an outer operculum (calyptra calyx) is present, it covers the inner operculum (calyptra corolla). Species of the subgenus Monocalyptrus have only a calyptra corolla (inner operculum) while in most other species, a calyptra calyx is also present, at least early in flower development. Not the same structure as a spore capsule calyptra or fruit operculum. Sometimes erroneously referred to as part of a fruit.

annotations.label: ray flower
annotations.definition: A small pistillate flower (PO:0025599) on the margin of a head-type (capitulum) inflorescence  (PO:0009049).
annotations.comment: Found in the Asteraceae, surrounding the disk flowers (PO:0025332). Usually zygomorphic, and different in form from disk flowers. Usually pistillate but sterile.

annotations.label: disk flower
annotations.definition: A small flower (PO:0009046) in the center of a head-type (capitulum) inflorescence (PO:0009049).
annotations.comment: Found in the Asteraceae. May be actinomorphic or bilabiate, often bisexual. Usually surrounded by and different in form from ray flowers (PO:0025331).

annotations.label: ensiform vascular leaf
annotations.definition: A unifacial leaf that is flat in cross section due to a lamina that develops in a median plane (perpendicular to the axis), rather a transverse plane (tangent to the axis) throughout all of part of the length of the leaf.
annotations.comment: Common in many monocots and some dicots. Note that ensiform leaves have two sides, but both sides arise from the same surface of the leaf meristem (abaxial or adaxial). In some leaves, the petiole may twist, giving the appearance that the lamina is ensiform, but it is not.

annotations.label: terete vascular leaf
annotations.definition: A unifacial leaf that is round in cross section throughout all of part of the length of the leaf.
annotations.comment: The surface of a terete leaf corresponds to either the adaxial or abaxial surface of a normal leaf.

synonyms.text: 維管束系円柱形葉 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllode leaf
annotations.definition: An adult ensiform leaf with a lamina that develops in a median plane, rather a transverse plane, throughout the length of the leaf and is a result of increased activity of the adaxial meristem early in development.
annotations.comment: Common in legumes of the genus Acacia. Similar development occurs in other ensiform leaves in some monocots, but they are not called phyllodes. Transitional leaves also occur, in which the basal portion of the leaf develops similar to a phyllode, but the apical portion of the leaf develops normal leaflets (see PO:0025336, transition phyllode leaf). Phyllodes are generally xeromorphic.

annotations.label: transition phyllode leaf
annotations.definition: A transition vascular leaf in which the basal portion of the leaf has unifacial lamina development in a median plane, similar to a phyllode, and the apical portion of the leaf develops leaflets similar to a juvenile leaf.
annotations.comment: Common in seedlings of legumes of the genus Acacia. May also occur later, after the plant has begun to produce phyllodes.

annotations.label: life of whole plant stage
annotations.definition: A maximal whole plant development stage.
annotations.comment: There are only two types of life of whole plant. Every life of a whole plant is either a maximal gametophyte stage or a maximal sporophyte stage.

synonyms.text: vida de la planta entera (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: collective plant organ structure development stage
annotations.definition: A plant structure development stage (PO:0009012) that has as primary participant a collective plant structure (PO:0025497).
annotations.comment: Includes flower development stage (PO:0007615), corolla development stage (PO:0007604), and inflorescence development stage (PO:0001083).

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo de una estructura colectiva de la planta (Spanish, exact)
集合的植物構造の発生過程 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: plant organ development stage
annotations.definition: A multi-tissue plant structure development stage (PO:0025571) that has as primary participant a plant organ (PO:0009008) .
annotations.comment: Includes development stages for leaves and roots.

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo de un &#243rgano (Spanish, exact)
植物器官発生過程 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: gametophyte vegetative stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) that occurs during the interval between spore germination and the formation of a gametangium.
annotations.comment: A gametophyte may begin to senesce or die from other causes before producing gametangia. Some plants, such as the fern Vittaria appalachiana, may never produce gametangia, and remain in the vegetative stage indefinitely. Developmental vegetative growth (GO:0080186) can also occur during the reproductive stage.

annotations.label: gametophyte reproductive stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) that occurs during the interval between the initiation of either a multicellular plant gametangium (PO:0025124) or a unicellular plant gametangium (PO:0025521) and the onset of senescence.
annotations.comment: A whole plant (PO:0000003) may continue developmental vegetative growth (GO:0080186) after it has entered the reproductive stage, and it may revert to a stage in which it is no longer producing gametantia, but once it has initiated a single gametangium, it remains in the reproductive stage until senescence or death by some other cause.

annotations.label: gametophyte dormant stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) during which a gametophyte participates in a dormancy process.
annotations.comment: A dormancy process (GO:0022611) is a developmental process in which dormancy (sometimes called a dormant state) is induced, maintained or broken. Dormancy is a suspension of most physiological activity and growth that can be reactivated. This term only applies to a whole plant (PO:0000003) in the gametophyte stage, not to the dormant stage of other plant structures. The dormant stage may be a response to environmental conditions such as seasonality or extreme heat, drought, or cold. Examples of a gametophyte dormant stage are the moss Polytrichum commune after it has undergone dessication (a type of resurrection plant), the boreal moss Polytrichum alpestre, whose shoot systems have annual growth segments due to a period of dormancy during the winter, the female gametophyte of Ginkgo biloba, which is dormant before fertilization, and dormancy of plant spores.

annotations.label: gametophyte senescent stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) during which a gametophyte participates in multicellular organism senescence.
annotations.comment: The gametophyte senescent stage is often preceded by the gametophyte reproductive stage, and it ends with death of the gametophyte, either as a result of senescence or some other cause. This stage is distinct from gametophyte dormant stage, in which organs may senesce, but some parts of the plant remain alive. Multicellular organism senescence/aging (GO:0010259) includes loss of functions such as resistance to disease, homeostasis, and fertility, as well processes like cellular senescence, organ senescence, and wear and tear. The POC curators have requested to the GOC that they revise the definition of GO:0010259, to make it more appropriate for plants.

synonyms.text: etapa de senescencia del gametofito (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: gametophore reproductive whole plant development stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte reproductive stage (PO:0025341) in which a gametophore (PO:0030018) and gametangium is being produced.

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del gamet&#243foro reproductivo de la planta entera (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: gametophore vegetative whole plant development stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte vegetative whole plant development stage during which a gametophore is being produced but no gametangia are being produced.
annotations.comment: Succeeds a protonema stage.

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del gamet&#243foro vegetativo de la planta entera (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: thallus reproductive whole plant development stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte reproductive development stage in which a thallus and gametangium is being produced.

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del talo reproductivo de la planta entera (Spanish, exact)
葉状体生殖生長期 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: thallus vegetative whole plant development stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte vegetative whole plant development stage during which a thallus is being produced but no gametangia are being produced.
annotations.comment: May succeed a protonema whole plant development stage or may directly succeed spore germination.

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del talo vegetativo de la plant entera (Spanish, exact)
葉状体栄養生長期 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome lamina vein
annotations.definition: A vascular bundle (PO:0005020) that is part of a phyllome lamina (PO:0025396).

annotations.label: awn
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part that is a slender, more or less straight and stiff, fine-pointed, terminal or subterminal appendage of a glume, lemma, or palea.
annotations.comment: Found in a spikelet of Poaceae. Sometimes the awn is a continuation of the bearing structure's central primary vein.

annotations.label: palea awn
annotations.definition: An awn that is part of a palea.

annotations.label: glume awn
annotations.definition: An awn that is part of a glume.

annotations.label: tubercle
annotations.definition: An enlarged leaf base (PO:0020040) that is fused with adjacent shoot axis (PO:0025029) tissue.
annotations.comment: Found in cacti and other succulents. The upper part of a leaf from which a tubercle develops is usually shed early in development. Tubercles are often conical, but they may merge with those directly above and below to form ribs, as in saguaro (Carnegiea), or they may elongate, as in species of pincushion cacti (Mammillaria). In species with tubercles, the areole bud (PO:0025353) is often found at the distal end of the tubercle. The areole bud originates in the leaf axil (PO:0009023) of the leaf that becomes the tubercle, but is displaced to the tip through elongation of the tubercle.

annotations.label: areole bud
annotations.definition: An axillary vegetative bud (PO:0004712) that is not elongated, in which the vascular leaves (PO:0009025) develop as spine leaves (PO:0025173).
annotations.comment: Found in Cactaceae. Appears as a cluster of spine leaves (PO:0025173). May be covered in trichomes (PO:0000282). In species with tubercles (PO:0025352), the areole bud is often found at the distal end of the tubercle. The areole bud originates in a leaf axil (PO:0009023) of a leaf that becomes a tubercle but is displaced to the tip of a tubercle through elongation of the tubercle.

annotations.label: cladode
annotations.definition: A shoot axis that is flattened and expanded.
annotations.comment: Has an increased surface area for photosynthesis and may function similar to leaves in plants that have no or small leaves, such as cacti. Consists of one to several internodes and nodes. Cladodes bear scale leaves (or leaf scars where temporary leaves have fallen off) that subtend axillary buds.

synonyms.text: 扁茎、仮葉枝、葉状茎 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: corm
annotations.definition: A shoot system (PO:0009006) that has as parts a short, enlarged stem (PO:0009047) in which the shoot internodes (PO:0005005) do not elongate, and one or more buds (PO:0000055).
annotations.comment: May serve as a storage or perennating organ. Usually underground. A corm differs from a bulb (PO:0025356) in that it does not have fleshy leaves or leaf bases but usually has scale leaves or remnants of dry leaf sheaths that form a protective covering around it. May bear remnants or scars of detached leaves. Adventitious roots may develop from the base of a corm, and these may be contractile, pulling the corm into the soil. Examples include Gladiolus and Crocus. The pseudobulb of an orchid is equivalent to a corm. A corm is very similar to a rhizome (PO:0004542) with very short sympodial units, although not all corms have sympodial growth.

annotations.label: bulb
annotations.definition: A short, enlarged shoot system (PO:0009006) that has as parts a short stem (PO:0009047) in which the shoot internodes (PO:0005005) do not elongate, and one or more buds (PO:0000055) enclosed by fleshy leaves (PO:0009025) or leaf bases (PO:0020040).
annotations.comment: May serve as a storage and/or perennating organ. Usually underground. A bulb differs from a corm (PO:0025355) by having fleshy leaves or leaf bases. The outer leaves of a bulb are often dry and membranous, rather than fleshy. May bear adventitious roots. Examples include Alium and Tulipa. Not the same structure as a bulbil.

annotations.label: pneumatophore
annotations.definition: A lateral root (PO:0020121) that is erect and protrudes above the surface of the soil or water.
annotations.comment: Pneumatophores are found in trees that live in flooded habitats such as mangroves, and often have many lenticels (PO:0000031) which may provide oxygen to below-ground roots growing in flooded soils. Knee roots are a type of pneumatophore in which the lateral root first grows upward, then turns and grows back down into the substrate, forming a knee-like bend. Note that a pneumatophore differs from a prop root (PO:0000044), which is formed at a stem node above the ground, and grows downward into the soil.

annotations.label: pneumatorhiza
annotations.definition: A small lateral root that grows from a prop root and has a mealy surface texture.
annotations.comment: Found in some palms and other species growing in flooded habitats. Pneumatorhiza appear as small outgrowths on the surface of a prop root. Similar, but distinct from, pneumatophores.

synonyms.text: 支持根から出る側根 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: sorus
annotations.definition: A collective plant organ structure (PO:0025007) on the surface of a vascular leaf (PO:0009025) that has as part a cluster of two or more adjacent sporangia (PO:0025094).

synonyms.text: 生殖器群、造精器群、胞子嚢群 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: ceonosorus
annotations.definition: A collective plant organ structure (PO:0025007) on the surface of a vascular leaf that has as parts two or more fused sori (PO:0025359).

synonyms.text: 融合胞子嚢を有する維管束葉表面の集合構造 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf tendril
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf that is slender and coiling and lacks a lamina.
annotations.comment: Aids plant in climbing. A leaf tendril will usually have a bud or shoot axis in its axil. See also leaflet tendril (PO:0025362), leaf apex tendril (PO:0025363), branch tendril (PO:0025364), stem apex tendril (PO:0025365), leaf rachis tendril (PO:0025366), and prophyll tendril (PO:0025367). Careful observation is required to correctly classify some tendrils.

annotations.label: leaflet tendril
annotations.definition: A leaflet that is slender and coiling and lacks a lamina.
annotations.comment: Aids plant in climbing. If the tendril is derived from the terminal leaflet, use this term instead of leaf apex tendril (PO:xxxx). See also leaf tendril (PO:0025361), leaf apex tendril (PO:0025363), branch tendril (PO:0025364), stem apex tendril (PO:0025365), leaf rachis tendril (PO:0025366), and prophyll tendril (PO:0025367).  Careful observation is required to correctly classify some tendrils.

synonyms.text: 小葉巻きひげ、若葉巻きひげ (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf apex tendril
annotations.definition: A leaf apex of a laminar vascular leaf that is slender and coiling.
annotations.comment: Aids plant in climbing. Found at the apex of a leaf lamina, but the leaf apex tendril is not laminar or it may be a narrow lamina. If the tendril is derived from the terminal leaflet, use the term leaflet tendril (PO:0025362). See also leaf tendril (PO:0025361), leaflet tendril (PO:0025362), branch tendril (PO:0025364), stem apex tendril (PO:0025365), leaf rachis tendril (PO:0025366), and prophyll tendril (PO:0025367). Careful observation is required to correctly classify some tendrils.

synonyms.text: &#225pice en zarzillo de la hoja   (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: branch tendril
annotations.definition: A branch that is slender and coiling.
annotations.comment: Aids plant in climbing. A branch tendril will usually be subtended by a leaf or leaf scar. May be branched, may have adhesive disks at its distal end, may be woody, and may bear small leaves. See also leaf tendril (PO:0025361), leaflet tendril (PO:0025362), leaf apex tendril (PO:0025363), stem apex tendril (PO:0025365), leaf rachis tendril (PO:0025366), and prophyll tendril (PO:0025367). Careful observation is required to correctly classify some\ntendrils.

annotations.label: stem apex tendril
annotations.definition: A shoot axis that arises from the apical part of a stem and is slender and coiling.
annotations.comment: Aids plant in climbing. May be branched, may have adhesive disks at its distal end, may be woody, and may bear small leaves. A stem apex tendril differs from a branch tendril in that it arises from the shoot apical meristem, rather than in an axil. However, a shoot apex tendril may arise either from the apex itself, in which case it is terminal, or from the side of an apical meristem, in which case it is a lateral structure. In the latter case, the main stem of the plant undergoes sympodial growth. A stem apex tendril is often located on the opposite side of a stem from a leaf at the same node, as in Vitaceae. See also leaf tendril (PO:0025361), leaflet tendril (PO:0025362), leaf apex tendril (PO:0025363), branch tendril (PO:0025364),  leaf rachis tendril (PO:0025366), and prophyll tendril (PO:0025365). Careful observation is required to correctly classify some tendrils.

annotations.label: leaf rachis tendril
annotations.definition: A leaf rachis (PO:0020055) that is slender and coiling and lacks a lamina.
annotations.comment: Aids plant in climbing. Differs from a leaf tendril, in that a leaf rachis tendril bear leaflets. See also leaf tendril (PO:0025361), leaflet tendril (PO:0025362), leaf apex tendril (PO:0025363), branch tendril (PO:0025364), stem apex tendril (PO:0025365), and prophyll tendril (PO:0025366). Careful observation is required to correctly classify some tendrils.

synonyms.text: raquis en zarzillo de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: prophyll tendril
annotations.definition: A prophyll that is slender and coiling and lacks a lamina.
annotations.comment: Aids plant in climbing. A prophyll may be difficult to distinguish from a branch tendril, because it may be found in the axil of a leaf, but it develops from a prophyll of the axillary bud, rather than a branch. Found in Curburitaceae. See also leaf tendril (PO:0025361), leaflet tendril (PO:0025362), leaf apex tendril (PO:0025363), branch tendril (PO:0025364), stem apex tendril (PO:0025365), and leaf rachis tendril (PO:0025366). Careful observation is required to correctly classify some tendrils.

synonyms.text: 前葉巻きひげ、前出葉巻きひげ (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: trichome development stage
annotations.definition: A plant structure development stage (PO:0009012) that has as primary participant a trichome (PO:0000282).

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del tricoma (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: seed trichome development stage
annotations.definition: A trichome development stage that has as primary participant a seed trichome.
annotations.comment: Seed trichome development stage consists of the following sub stages: seed trichome initiation stage (PO:0025370), seed trichome elongation stage (PO:0025371), seed trichome secondary wall biosynthesis stage(PO:0025372) and seed trichome maturation stage (PO:0025373).

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del tricoma de la semilla (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: seed trichome initiation stage
annotations.definition: A seed trichome development stage which occurs during the interval between the onset of seed trichome initiation and the onset of seed trichome elongation.
annotations.comment: Refers to the GO Biological Process terms seed trichome initiation (GO:0090377) and seed trichome elongation (GO:0090378).

synonyms.text: etapa de iniciaci&#243n del tricoma de la semilla (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: seed trichome elongation stage
annotations.definition: A seed trichome development stage which occurs during the interval between the onset of seed trichome elongation and the onset of seed trichome secondary wall biosynthesis.
annotations.comment: Refers to the GO Biological Process terms: seed trichome elongation (GO:0090378) and secondary cell wall biogenesis involved in seed trichome differentiation (GO:0090379).

synonyms.text: etapa de elongaci&#243n del tricoma de la semilla (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: seed trichome secondary wall biosynthesis stage
annotations.definition: A seed trichome development stage which occurs during the interval between the onset of seed trichome secondary wall biosynthesis and the onset of seed trichome maturation.
annotations.comment: Refers to the GO Biological Process terms: secondary cell wall biogenesis involved in seed trichome differentiation (GO:0090379) and seed trichome maturation (GO:0090380).

synonyms.text: etapa de bios&#237ntesis de la pared secundaria del tricoma de la semilla (Spanish, exact)
seed hair secondary wall biosynthesis stage (exact)
種子毛二次壁生合成段階 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: seed trichome maturation stage
annotations.definition: A seed trichome development stage which encompasses the process of seed trichome maturation.
annotations.comment: Refers to the GO Biological Process term: seed trichome maturation (GO:0090380).

synonyms.text: etapa de maduraci&#n del tricoma de la semilla (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: seed dormant stage
annotations.definition: A seed development stage (PO:0001170) during which a seed (PO:0009010) participates in a seed dormancy process (GO:0010162).
