annotations.comment: A seed dormancy process (GO:0010162) is a dormancy process in which dormancy (sometimes called a dormant state) is induced, maintained or broken in a seed. Seed dormancy is a suspension of most physiological activity and growth in a seed, including the embryo contained therein, that can be reactivated. It often requires special conditions for reactivation, such as specific temperature, scarification, or leaching of inhibitors. Seed dormancy includes dormancy of the embryo and the seed coat, although\nthese may not begin or end simultaneously. Not all seeds go through a seed dormant stage. Most seeds that go through a dormant stage do so only one time.

synonyms.text: etapa latente de la semilla (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: plant spore stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) that occurs during the interval between meiosis and the germination of a spore.
annotations.comment: Includes the stage when a megaspore is present before it develops into a megagametophyte or embryo sac and the stage when a microspore is present before it develops into a microgametophyte or pollen. There may be cell division in a spore before it germinates.

synonyms.text: etapa esporof&#237tica de la planta (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: plant spore dormant stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte dormant stage during which a spore participates in a dormancy process.
annotations.comment: A dormancy process (GO:0022611) is a developmental process in which dormancy (sometimes called a dormant state) is induced, maintained or broken. Dormancy is a suspension of most physiological activity and growth that can be reactivated.

synonyms.text: etapa latente esporof&#237tica de la planta (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: plant embryo dormant stage
annotations.definition: A mature plant embryo stage (PO:0001081) during the interval between when a plant embryo (PO:0009009) begins a dormancy process (GO:0022611) and one of the following: the beginning of the seed germination stage (PO:0007057) in seed plants, formation of the first vascular leaf (PO:0009025) in pteridophytes, the beginning of development of a sporangium (PO:0025094) in bryophytes, or the beginning of the formation of a plant organ (PO:0009008) such as a root (PO:0009005), shoot axis (PO:0025029), or vascular leaf (PO:0009025) in a cultured plant embryo (PO:0000010).
annotations.comment: During a plant embryo dormant stage, a plant embryo participates in a dormancy process, but not all plant embryos have a dormant stage. A dormancy process (GO:0022611) is a developmental process in which dormancy (sometimes called a dormant state) is induced, maintained or broken. Dormancy is a suspension of most physiological activity and growth that can be reactivated. In seed plants, a plant embryo dormant stage may be part of a seed dormant stage.

annotations.label: arillode
annotations.definition: An arilloid that is an elaborate outgrowth of a seed at the micropylar end.
annotations.comment: Generally larger than a caruncle and not hard.

synonyms.text: 偽種衣　偽仮種皮　アリロード (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: intercellular space
annotations.definition: A plant anatomical space that is part of a portion of plant tissue between two or more cells.
annotations.comment: Often part of a mesophyll (PO:0006070). This term does not include spaces on the surface of a plant structure, like a stomatal pore (PO:0008032), stomium (PO:0020101), or micropyle (PO:0020025), nor larger cavities or canals that are surrounded by tissue, such as an archegonium neck canal (PO:0030066). May be filled with air or fluid.

annotations.label: leaf mesophyll intercellular space
annotations.definition: An intercellular space that is part of a leaf mesophyll.
annotations.comment: Includes the air spaces in the spongy mesophyll (PO:0005647), the palisade mesophyll (PO:0005648) and substomatal cavity (PO:0025382).

synonyms.text: espacio intercelular en el mes&#243filo de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉肉細胞内空間（間隙） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: spongy mesophyll intercellular space
annotations.definition: A leaf mesophyll intercellular space that is part of a spongy mesophyll.

synonyms.text: espacio intercelular en el mes&#243filo esponjoso de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
海綿状葉肉細胞間間隙 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: substomatal cavity
annotations.definition: An intercellular space that is located directly below and contiguous with a stomatal pore.

annotations.label: phyllome sinus
annotations.definition: A plant anatomical space that is a phyllome marginal embayment, incision or indentation between marginal projections of any sort, typically lobes, teeth, or the base of cordate phyllomes.
annotations.comment: Can be angular or rounded.

annotations.label: leaf sinus
annotations.definition: A phyllome sinus that is a marginal embayment, incision or indentation between marginal projections of any sort, typically lobes, teeth, or the base of cordate leaves.
annotations.comment: Can be angular or rounded.

annotations.label: cutin
annotations.definition: A portion of plant substance (PO:0025161) that is the inner layer of a plant cuticle (PO:0000022), and is composed of a polyester matrix of hydroxy- and hydroxy epoxy fatty acid C16 and C18 chains.
annotations.comment: Cutin is the insoluble, covalently cross-linked polymer that forms an electron dense layer over the epidermal cells (PO:0004013) of an aerial plant structure.

annotations.label: cuticular wax
annotations.definition: A portion of plant substance (PO:0025161) comprised predominantly of very long chain aliphatic lipids and is part of a plant cuticle (PO:0000022).
annotations.comment: Also includes triterpenoids and minor secondary metabolites, such as sterols and flavonoids. May be embedded within or external to the layer of cutin of the cuticle proper.  Phenotypically, wax is distinguished as the glaucous or whitish bloom on a part of a plant shoot system that corresponds to crystalloid epicuticular wax with light scattering capacity.

synonyms.text: クチクラワックス (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: plant cuticle proper
annotations.definition: A portion of plant substance (PO:0025161) that is the inner layer of a plant cuticle (PO:0000022), composed primarily of cutin (PO:0025385), embedded and overlayed with cuticular wax (PO:0025386).

synonyms.text: cut&#237cula propia de la planta (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: epicuticular wax
annotations.definition: A portion of cuticular wax (PO:0025386) that forms crystalloids or a smooth film on the exterior of a plant cuticle (PO:0000022).
annotations.comment: Epicuticular waxes may take a variety of forms, such as plates, ribbons, tubes, and rods.

synonyms.text: エピクチクラワックス (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: intercuticular wax
annotations.definition: A portion of cuticular wax (PO:0025386) that is an amorphous mixture of lipids embedded in the cutin (PO:0025385) of a plant cuticle proper (PO:0025387).
annotations.comment: The intercuticular wax is located adjacent to the cell wall.

synonyms.text: クチクラ層間ワックス (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: free ending veinlet
annotations.definition: A leaf lamina vein (PO:0020138) above the first order that is connected to another vein only at one end.
annotations.comment: May be branched or simple. May be found inside a lamina areole. Usually used with third order or higher veins. If vein order is known, an additional annotation should be place on secondary vein (PO:0020140), tertiary vein (PO:0008021), quaternary vein (PO:0008022), or higher order vein (PO:0008023).

annotations.label: phyllome lamina areole
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is part of a phyllome lamina (PO:0025396) and is completely surrounded by phyllome lamina veins (PO:0025348) and cannot be divided into any smaller parts that are completely surrounded by phyllome lamina veins.
annotations.comment: Any order of phyllome lamina vein can form the sides of a phyllome lamina areole. Taken together, the phyllome lamina areoles form a contiguous field of polygons over the area of a phyllome lamina.

synonyms.text: フィロム葉身網目隙 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf lamina areole
annotations.definition: A phyllome lamina areole (PO:0025391) that is part of a leaf lamina (PO:0020039) and is completely surrounded by leaf lamina veins (PO:0020138) and cannot be divided into any smaller parts that are completely surrounded by leaf lamina veins.
annotations.comment: Any order of leaf lamina vein can form the sides of a leaf lamina areole. Taken together, the leaf lamina areoles form a contiguous field of polygons over the area of a leaf lamina. Free ending veinlets (PO:0025390) may be found within a leaf lamina areole.

synonyms.text: areola de la l&#225mina foliar (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: pappus
annotations.definition: A collective organ part structure that has a parts one or more pappus elements (scales, awns, or bristles) at the apex of an inferior ovary just beneath or outside the corolla.
annotations.comment: Found in flowers and fruits (cypsela) of the Asteraceae. May aid in dispersal by wind or animals. Considered by some to be an evolutionarily modified upper calyx portion, the lower portion having been incorporated into the floral tube casing adnate to the ovary wall; considered by others to be an outgrowth from the ovary wall.

annotations.label: pappus element
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part that is a segment of a pappus.
annotations.comment: May be awn-, scale-, or bristle-shaped.

annotations.label: floral organ
annotations.definition: A plant organ (PO:0009008) that is part of a flower (PO:0009046).
annotations.comment: Includes phyllomes such as anthers and petals, as well as shoot axes such as androphores and gynophores.

annotations.label: phyllome lamina
annotations.definition: A plant organ lamina (PO:0025513) that is part of a phyllome (PO:0006001).

annotations.label: palisade mesophyll intercellular space
annotations.definition: A leaf mesophyll intercellular space that is part of a palisade mesophyll.

synonyms.text: espacio intercelular del mes&#243filo en palisada (Spanish, exact)
柵状葉肉細胞間間隙 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome substomatal cavity
annotations.definition: A substomatal cavity that is part of a phyllome.

synonyms.text: フィロム気孔下腔、フィロム呼吸腔 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf substomatal cavity
annotations.definition: A phyllome substomatal cavity that is part of a leaf mesophyll.

synonyms.text: cavidad subestom&#225tica de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉気孔下腔, 呼吸腔 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome adaxial meristem
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) that is found on the adaxial side of a developing phyllome (PO:0006001).
annotations.comment: Contributes to the thickness of a phyllome (PO:0006001). There are two types of adaxial meristems. In phyllomes with a petiole (PO:0020038) and lamina (PO:0025060), an adaxial rounding meristem occurs later in development, is separated from the apical meristem (PO:0020144), and contributes to the thickness of the petiole and midrib. In unifacial phyllomes, the an adaxial meristem occurs earlier in development, is close to but separate from the apical meristem, and contributes to the thickness and cross-sectional shape of the entire phyllome. If annotating to a leaf, use the more specific term leaf adaxial meristem (PO:0025401).

synonyms.text: フィロム向軸側分裂組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: vascular leaf adaxial meristem
annotations.definition: A portion of phyllome adaxial meristem (PO:0025400) tissue that is found on the adaxial side of a developing vascular leaf (PO:0009025).
annotations.comment: Contributes to the thickness of a vascular leaf (PO:0009025). There are two types of adaxial meristems. In leaves with a petiole (PO:0020038) and lamina (PO:0025060), such as Arabidopsis thaliana, an adaxial rounding meristem occurs later in development, is separated from the apical meristem (PO:0020144), and contributes to the thickness of the petiole and midrib. In unifacial leaves, the an adaxial unifacial meristem occurs earlier in development, is close to but separate from the apical meristem, and contributes to the thickness and cross-sectional shape of the entire leaf.

synonyms.text: 葉向軸側分裂組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome abaxial meristem
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) that is found on the abaxial side of a developing phyllome (PO:0006001).
annotations.comment: Contributes to the thickness of a phyllome (PO:0006001). There are two types of adaxial meristems. In phyllomes with a petiole (PO:0020038) and lamina (PO:0025060), an adaxial rounding meristem occurs later in development, is separated from the apical meristem (PO:0020144), and contributes to the thickness of the petiole and midrib. In unifacial phyllomes, the an adaxial meristem occurs earlier in development, is close to but separate from the apical meristem, and contributes to the thickness and cross-sectional shape of the entire phyllome. If annotating to a leaf, use the more specific term leaf abaxial meristem (PO:0025403).

synonyms.text: フィロム背軸側分裂組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: vascular leaf abaxial meristem
annotations.definition: A portion of phyllome abaxial meristem (PO:0025402) tissue that is found on the abaxial side of a developing vascular leaf (PO:0009025).
annotations.comment: Contributes to the thickness of a vascular leaf (PO:0009025). There are two types of abaxial meristems. In leaves with a petiole (PO:0020038) and lamina (PO:0025060), such as Arabidopsis thaliana, an abaxial rounding meristem occurs later in development, is separated from the apical meristem (PO:0020144), and contributes to the thickness of the petiole and midrib. In unifacial leaves, the abaxial unifacial meristem occurs earlier in development, is close to but separate from the apical meristem, and contributes to the thickness and cross-sectional shape of the entire leaf.

synonyms.text: 葉背軸側分裂組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome marginal meristem
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem (PO:0009013) tissue that is part of a phyllome margin (PO:0025018) of an expanding phyllome (PO:0006001) and has as parts marginal and submarginal initials.
annotations.comment: Phyllome marginal meristems may develop on the lateral margins of a phyllome after the transition from a phyllome primordium (PO:0025128) and may contribute to expansion of the phyllome lamina (PO:0025396). Marginal growth can occur in a phyllome primordium, but it is not considered part of a phyllome marginal meristem. Phyllome marginal initials may give rise to phyllome epidermis (PO:0025157) while phyllome submarginal initials may give rise to the mesophyll (PO:0006070). May be of limited duration.

synonyms.text: フィロム縁分裂細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf marginal meristem
annotations.definition: A portion of phyllome marginal meristem (PO:0025404) tissue that is part of a leaf margin (PO:0025009) of a vascular leaf (PO:0009025) during a leaf expansion stage (PO:0001052) and has parts marginal and submarginal initials.
annotations.comment: Leaf marginal meristems develop on the lateral margins of a vascular leaf after the transition from a vascular leaf primordium (PO:0000017) and may contribute to expansion of the leaf lamina (PO:0020039). Marginal growth can occur in a vascular leaf primordium, but it is not considered part of a leaf marginal meristem. Leaf marginal initials (add) may give rise to leaf epidermis (PO:0006016) while leaf submarginal initials (add) may give rise to the leaf mesophyll (PO:0005645). May be of limited duration.

annotations.label: sieve element
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that is part of a portion of phloem tissue (PO:0005417), has sieve areas in its walls, and lacks a nucleus in its mature protoplast.
annotations.comment: Sieve elements function mainly in longitudinal conduction of organic solutes. Classified into sieve cells (PO:0025415) and sieve tube elements (PO:0000289) based on the presence of sieve plates and specialization of sieve areas in different parts of the cell. Develops from either a cambial initial (PO:0000295), a phloem mother cell (PO:0000400), or a cell of the procambium (PO:0025275).

annotations.label: plant fiber cell
annotations.definition: A sclerenchyma cell (PO:0000077) that is elongated and tapering.
annotations.comment: May or may not have lignin in the secondary cell wall (GO:0009531) or a living protoplast at maturity. It may be difficult to distinguish fiber cells from tracheary elements (PO:0000290). Compare with sclerid cells (PO:0025418), which are usually not elongated.

annotations.label: primary vascular tissue
annotations.definition: A portion of vascular tissue (PO:0009015) that develops from a procambium (PO:0025275).
annotations.comment: Forms as part of primary growth (GO term for primary growth has been requested).

annotations.label: secondary vascular tissue
annotations.definition: A portion of vascular tissue (PO:0009015) that develops from a vascular cambium (PO:0005598).
annotations.comment: Forms as part of secondary growth (GO:0080117) in vascular plants. Often differentiated into the axial system (PO:0025410) and ray system (PO:0025411).

annotations.label: axial system
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary vascular tissue (PO:0025409) that has as parts cells derived from fusiform initials (PO:0000079) and oriented with their longest diameter parallel to a plant axis (PO:0025004).
annotations.comment: Includes portions of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) and axial wood parenchyma (PO:0004533).

annotations.label: ray system
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary vascular tissue (PO:0025409) that has as part ray wood parenchyma (PO:0004534) and extends radially in the secondary xylem (PO:0005848) and secondary phloem (PO:0005848) of a plant axis (PO:0025004).
annotations.comment: May include ray tracheids, as in Pinaceae and some Cupressaceae.

annotations.label: albuminous cell
annotations.definition: A parenchyma cell (PO:0000074) that is morphologically and physiologically associated with a sieve cell (PO:0025415) but does is not derived from the same initial cell (PO:0004011) as the sieve cell.
annotations.comment: May be found in the ray system (PO:0025411) and sometimes the axial system (PO:0025410) of gymnosperms. Compare to companion cells (PO:0000071), which are found in angiosperms.

annotations.label: primary leaf vein
annotations.definition: A leaf lamina vein (PO:0020138) that connects directly to a petiole vascular system (PO:0000052) or a shoot axis vascular system (PO:0000039).
annotations.comment: Generally the largest and most prominent of the leaf veins. A leaf may have more than one primary vein. The central primary vein is the midvein (PO:0020139). Vein orders only apply to hierarchically branching vein patterns, not to dichotomously branching vein patterns, as found in some ferns and gymnosperms.

synonyms.text: vena primaria de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: secondary thickening meristem
annotations.definition: A lateral meristem (PO:0020145) that is not contiguous with an apical meristem (PO:0020144) and has a parts multiple layers of meristematic cells (PO:0004010).
annotations.comment: Contributes mainly to formation of the body of a stem (PO:0009047), but may also produce adventitious (shoot-borne) roots (PO:0000042) and may also be found in the roots (PO:0009005) of some species. May be continuous or discontinuous with the primary thickening meristem (PO:0005039). Found in some monocot species of the Liliales and Asperigales.

synonyms.text: 二次 肥厚分裂組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: sieve cell
annotations.definition: A sieve element (PO:0025406) in which the sieve areas are not aggregated into sieve plates (GO:0097218).
annotations.comment: Sieve areas in sieve cells generally have narrow pores and thin connecting strands, and they are not highly specialized, but instead are fairly uniform throughout the cell wall. Typical of gymnosperms and non-seed vascular plants such as ferns. Differ from sieve tube elements, which have sieve plates and more pronounced specialization of sieve areas.

annotations.label: sieve tube
annotations.definition: A portion of phloem tissue (PO:0005417) that has as parts a series of sieve tube elements (PO:0000289) arranged end to end and interconnected through sieve plates.
annotations.comment: A sieve plate is part of a sieve tube member.

annotations.label: xylem vessel
annotations.definition: A portion of xylem (PO:0005352) tissue that has as parts a tube-like series of vessel members (PO:0002003), the common walls of which have perforations.

annotations.label: sclerid cell
annotations.definition: A sclerenchyma cell (PO:0000077) with many pits in the secondary cell wall.
annotations.comment: Usually not very elongated. The lumen of a sclerid cell is occluded by the secondary cell wall.

annotations.label: septate fiber tracheid
annotations.definition: A fiber tracheid (PO:0000355) that has as parts one or more thin internal partitions or septa.
annotations.comment: A septate fiber tracheid septum is composed of the adjacent primary cell walls of two internal cells that arise from mitosis within the septate fiber tracheid after it has formed a secondary cell wall (GO:0009531).

annotations.label: septate libriform fiber cell
annotations.definition: A libriform fiber cell (PO:0004520) that has as parts one or more thin internal partitions or septa.
annotations.comment: A septate libriform fiber cell septum is composed of the adjacent primary cell walls of two internal cells that arise from mitosis within the septate libriform fiber cell after it has formed a secondary cell wall (GO:0009531).

synonyms.text: 隔壁靭皮繊維細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: septate phloem fiber cell
annotations.definition: A phloem fiber cell (PO:0004519) that has as parts one or more thin internal partitions or septa.
annotations.comment: A septatephloem fiber cell septum is composed of the adjacent primary cell walls of two internal cells that arise from mitosis within the septate phloem fiber cell after it has formed a secondary cell wall (GO:0009531).

synonyms.text: 隔壁師部 fiber cell (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: gelatinous fiber cell
annotations.definition: A xylem fiber cell (PO:0000274) in which the inner-most layer of the secondary cell wall (GO:0009531) contains abundant cellulose and is poor in lignin.
annotations.comment: Often found in reaction wood in dicots and in some phloem fiber cells (PO:0004519), as in Linum usitatissimum. In gelatinous fiber cells, the inner layer of the cell wall is a cellulosic gelatinous-layer or G-layer, which may be relatively porous and less compact than the outer layers of the cell wall and may absorb water and swell.

annotations.label: plant tissue development stage
annotations.definition: A plant structure development stage (PO:0009012) that has as primary participant a portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007).

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo de tejido vegetal (Spanish, exact)
植物細胞発生過程 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: vascular tissue development stage
annotations.definition: A plant tissue development stage (PO:0025423) that has as primary participant a portion of vascular tissue (PO:0009015).

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del tejido vascular (Spanish, exact)
維管束細胞発生過程 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: xylem development stage
annotations.definition: A vascular tissue development stage (PO:0025424) that has as primary participant a portion of xylem (PO:0005352) tissue.

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del xilema (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: phloem development stage
annotations.definition: A vascular tissue development stage (PO:0025424) that has as primary participant a portion of phloem (PO:0005417) tissue.

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del floema (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: secondary xylem development stage
annotations.definition: A xylem development stage (PO:0025425) that has as primary participant a portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) tissue during the interval between the beginning of a cell division stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025463) and the death of the last living cell in the secondary xylem.
annotations.comment: During a secondary xylem development stage (also called a wood development stage), the cells of secondary xylem (PO:0005848), including tracheary elements (PO:0000290), xylem fiber cells (PO:0000274), and wood parenchyma cells (PO:0004525) undergo cellular developmental processes (GO:0048869) such as cell division (GO:0051301), cell growth (GO:0016049), cell differentiation (GO:0030154), or cell death (GO:0008219). The transition from sapwood to heartwood marks the end of the secondary xylem development stage.

synonyms.text: etapa de desarrollo del xilema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次木質部発生過程 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome plate meristem
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem (PO:0009013) tissue in which the planes of cell division in each layer are anticlinal to the surface of the phyllome (PO:0006001).
annotations.comment: The activity of a phyllome plate meristem results in increase in the surface area, but not thickness of a phyllome.

synonyms.text: meristema en placa del filoma (Spanish, exact)
フィロムplate分裂細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf plate meristem
annotations.definition: A phyllome plate meristem (PO:0025428) in the vascular leaf (PO:0009025).
annotations.comment: The activity of a leaf plate meristem results in increase in the surface area, but not thickness of a vascular leaf (PO:0009025).

synonyms.text: meristema en placa de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉の板状分裂組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome anlagen
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) that is part of a peripheral zone (PO:0000225) of a shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) and will give rise to a phyllome primordium (PO:0025128).
annotations.comment: The phyllome anlagen is only detectable by gene expression, not morphology.

annotations.label: vascular leaf anlagen
annotations.definition: A phyllome anlagen (PO:0025430) that will give rise to a vascular leaf primordium (PO:0000017) and is part of a peripheral zone (PO:0000225) of a shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148).
annotations.comment: This is the region where you have the maximum concentration of auxin in the SAM (at least in a few species that have been studied).

annotations.label: cotyledon anlagen
annotations.definition: A phyllome anlagen (PO:0025430) that will give rise to a cotyledon primordium (PO:0000015) and is part of a peripheral zone (PO:000225) of an embryo shoot apical meristem (PO:0006362).

annotations.label: root anlagen
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) that is committed to the development of a root primordium (PO:0005029).
annotations.comment: Only detectable by gene expression, not morphology. May arise in a pericycle (PO:0006203), as for lateral roots in most seed plants, an endodermis (PO:0000252), as for lateral roots in ferns, or from parenchyma cells (PO:0000074) that are part a shoot axis (PO:0025029), in the case of a basal root (PO:0025002) or shoot-borne root (PO:0000042).

annotations.label: petiole vascular bundle
annotations.definition: A vascular bundle (PO:0005020) that is part of a petiole vascular system (PO:0000052).

annotations.label: shoot axis cork cambium
annotations.definition: A cork cambium (PO:0005599) that is part of a shoot axis (PO:0025029).

synonyms.text: cambium suber&#237geno del &#225pice del epiblasto (Spanish, exact)
シュート軸コルク形成層 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: root cork cambium
annotations.definition: A cork cambium (PO:0005599) that is part of a root (PO:0009005).

synonyms.text: cambium suber&#237geno de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: shoot axis vascular cambium
annotations.definition: A vascular cambium (PO:0005598) that is part of a shoot axis (PO:0025029).

synonyms.text: cambium vascular del eje del epiblasto (Spanish, exact)
シュート軸維管束形成層 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: root vascular cambium
annotations.definition: A vascular cambium (PO:0005598) that is part of a root (PO:0009005).

synonyms.text: cambium vascular de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: cambial zone
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is part of a plant axis (PO:0025004) and has as parts a cambium (PO:0005597) and adjacent cells.
annotations.comment: The cambial zone generally consists of the initial cells of the cambium and their immediate derivatives, arranged in radial files. This term is used for the cambium (PO:0005597) and surrounding area, especially when the borders of the cambium are not clear.

annotations.label: shoot axis cambial zone
annotations.definition: A cambial zone that is part of a shoot axis (PO:0025029).
annotations.comment: Includes a cambium (PO:0005597) and adjacent cells. This term is used when the borders of the cambium are not clear.

synonyms.text: zona cambial del eje del epiblasto (Spanish, exact)
シュート軸形成層 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: root cambial zone
annotations.definition: A cambial zone that is part of a root (PO:0009005).
annotations.comment: Includes a cambium (PO:0005597) and adjacent cells. This term is used when the borders of the cambium are not clear.

synonyms.text: zona cambial de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: stem secondary thickening meristem
annotations.definition: A secondary thickening meristem (PO:0025414) that is part of a stem (PO:0009047).

synonyms.text: meristema de engrosamiento secundario del tallo (Spanish, exact)
茎二次肥厚分裂細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: root secondary thickening meristem
annotations.definition: A secondary thickening meristem (PO:0025414) that is part of a root (PO:0009005).

synonyms.text: meristema de engrosamiento secundario de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)
根二次肥厚分裂組織 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: primary xylem tracheary element
annotations.definition: A tracheary element (PO:0000290) that is part of a primary xylem tissue (PO:0005849).

synonyms.text: elemento de tr&#225quea del xilema primario (Spanish, exact)
一次木質部導管要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: secondary xylem tracheary element
annotations.definition: A tracheary element (PO:0000290) that is part of a portion of secondary xylem tissue (PO:0005848).

synonyms.text: elemento de tr&#225quea del xilema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次木質部仮導管要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: primary xylem tracheid
annotations.definition: A tracheid (PO:0000301) that is part of a xylem tissue (PO:0005849).

synonyms.text: 一次木質部仮導管 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: secondary xylem tracheid
annotations.definition: A tracheid PO:0000301 that is part of a portion of secondary xylem tissue (PO:0005848).

synonyms.text: 二次木質部仮導管 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: primary xylem vessel member
annotations.definition: A xylem vessel member (PO:0002003) that is part of a primary xylem tissue (PO:0005849).

synonyms.text: elemento vaso del xilema primario (Spanish, exact)
一次木質部導管状要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: secondary xylem vessel member
annotations.definition: A xylem vessel member (PO:0002003) that is part of a portion of secondary xylem tissue (PO:0005848).

synonyms.text: elemento vaso del xilema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次木質部導管要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: primary xylem vessel
annotations.definition: A xylem vessel (PO:0025417) that is part of a primary xylem tissue (PO:0005849).

annotations.label: secondary xylem vessel
annotations.definition: A xylem vessel (PO:0025417) that is part of a portion of secondary xylem tissue (PO:0005848).

annotations.label: primary phloem sieve element
annotations.definition: A sieve element (PO:0025406) that is part of a portion of primary phloem (PO:0006075).

synonyms.text: elemento criboso del floema primario (Spanish, exact)
一次師部 師（篩）要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: secondary phloem sieve element
annotations.definition: A sieve element (PO:0025406) that is part of a portion of secondary phloem (PO:0005043).

synonyms.text: elemento criboso del floema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次師部（師管部） 篩要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: primary phloem sieve cell
annotations.definition: A sieve cell (PO:0025415) that is part of a portion of primary phloem (PO:0006075).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula cribosa del floema primario (Spanish, exact)
一次師部 師（篩）細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: primary phloem sieve tube element
annotations.definition: A sieve tube element (PO:0000289) that is part of a portion of primary phloem (PO:0006075).

synonyms.text: elemento de tubo criboso del floema primario (Spanish, exact)
一次師部 師（篩）管 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: secondary phloem sieve cell
annotations.definition: A sieve cell (PO:0025415) that is part of a portion of secondary phloem (PO:0005043).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula cribosa del floema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次師部（師管部）篩細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: secondary phloem sieve tube element
annotations.definition: A sieve tube element (PO:0000289) that is part of a portion of secondary phloem (PO:0005043).

synonyms.text: elemento de tubo criboso del floema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次師部（師管部） 篩管要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: transfer companion cell
annotations.definition: A companion cell (PO:0000400) that is a transfer cell (PO:0000078) with many cell wall invaginations.
annotations.comment: Plasmodesmata between a transfer companion cell and adjacent mesophyll cells may be few in number. Transfer companion cells are found in plants that use apoplastic phloem loading. Unlike sieve tube elements (PO:0000289), transfer companion cells retain their nuclei and other organelles at maturity.

annotations.label: ordinary companion cell
annotations.definition: A companion cell (PO:0000400) that lacks cell wall invaginations and has few or no plasmodesmata connecting it to adjacent mesophyll cells.
annotations.comment: Found in plants that use apoplastic phloem loading. Unlike sieve tube elements (PO:0000289), ordinary companion cells retain their nuclei and other organelles at maturity.

annotations.label: intermediary companion cell
annotations.definition: A companion cell (PO:0000400) that has dense cytoplasm, a small vacuole, proplastids rather than chloroplasts, and abundant and highly branched plasmodesmata linking it to adjacent mesophyll cells.
annotations.comment: Intermediary companion cells translocate high concentrations of raffinose-family oligosaccharides and are found in plants that use symplastic phloem loading. Unlike sieve tube elements (PO:0000289), intermediary companion cells retain their nuclei and other organelles at maturity.

annotations.label: early wood tracheary element
annotations.definition: A secondary xylem tracheary element (PO:0025445) that is part of a portion of early wood (PO:0004515).
annotations.comment: Early wood tracheary elements generally have larger cell lumens than late wood tracheary elements (PO:0025462) of the same growth ring (PO:0004514).

annotations.label: late wood tracheary element
annotations.definition: A secondary xylem tracheary element (PO:0025445) that is part of a portion of late wood (PO:0004516).
annotations.comment: Late wood tracheary elements generally have smaller cell lumens than early wood tracheary elements (PO:0025461) of the same growth ring (PO:0004514).

synonyms.text: elemento de tr&#225quea de madera temprana (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: cell division stage of secondary xylem
annotations.definition: A secondary xylem development stage (PO:0025427) that occurs during the interval between the first and last cell division in a portion of secondary xylem tissue (PO:0005848), during which cells are produced by divisions of cambial initials (PO:0000295) or xylem mother cells.
annotations.comment: This stage includes production of xylem mother cells by cambial initials and production of tracheary elements (PO:0000290) or other xylem cells by xylem mother cells. Precedes a cell differentiation stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025469), although cell differentiation may begin before a cell division stage of secondary xylem ends.

synonyms.text: elemento de tr&#225quea de madera tard&#237a (Spanish, exact)
二次木部の細胞分裂期 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: cell expansion stage of secondary xylem
annotations.definition: A cell differentiation stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025469) that occurs during the interval between the end of a cell division stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025463) and the onset of a secondary cell wall formation stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025465), during which the cells undergo cell growth (GO:0016049).
annotations.comment: This stage succeeds a cell division stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025463) and precedes secondary cell wall formation stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025465). At least part of this stage can be easily observed in woody species containing xylem vessels (PO:0025417).

synonyms.text: etapa de expansi&#243n celular del xilema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次木部の細胞伸長期 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: secondary cell wall formation stage of secondary xylem
annotations.definition: A cell differentiation stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025469) that occurs during the interval between the onset of secondary wall biogenesis (GO:0009834) of the cells in a portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) and the beginning of a cell death stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025466).
annotations.comment: During this stage, secondary cell walls (GO:0009531) are being formed in a portion of secondary xylem tissue, preventing further expansion of the cells. This stage succeeds a cell expansion stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025464) and precedes a cell death stage of secondary xylem.

synonyms.text: estapa de formaci&#243n de la pared secundaria celular del xilema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次木部の細胞壁二次壁形成段階 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: cell death stage of secondary xylem
annotations.definition: A cell differentiation stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025469) that occurs during the interval between the first cell death of a tracheary element (PO:0000290) or non-septate xylem fiber cell (PO:0000274) and the death of the last secondary xylem parenchyma cell (PO:0004525) or septate fiber cell (PO:0004521) in a portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848).
annotations.comment: During this stage, the cells of a portion of secondary xylem are undergoing cell death (GO:0008219), sometimes called apoptosis. Death of the tracheary elements and non-septate xylem fiber cells generally precedes the death of secondary parenchyma cells and septate fiber cells. Once all cells in portion of secondary xylem are dead, it transitions from sapwood (PO:0004513) to heartwood (PO:0004512), and secondary xylem development ends.

synonyms.text: etapa de muerte celular del xilema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次木部の細胞死段階 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: tracheary element death stage of secondary xylem
annotations.definition: A cell death stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025466) that occurs during the interval between the first and last cell death of a tracheary element (PO:0000290) or non-septate xylem fiber cell (PO:0000274).
annotations.comment: During this stage, the tracheary elements and non-septate xylem fiber cells of a portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) undergo cell death (GO:0008219), but most of the secondary xylem parenchyma cells (PO:0004525) and septate xylem fiber cells (septate fiber tracheid, PO:0025419, and septate libriform fiber cell, PO:0025420) remain alive. Precedes a secondary xylem parenchyma cell death stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025468).

synonyms.text: etapa de muerte del elemento de tr&#225a del xilema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次木部管状要素枯死期 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: secondary xylem parenchyma cell death stage of secondary xylem
annotations.definition: A cell death stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025466) that occurs during the interval between the last cell death of a tracheary element (PO:0000290) or non-septate xylem fiber cell (PO:0000274) and the last cell death in a portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848).
annotations.comment: During this stage, the secondary xylem parenchyma cells (PO:0004525) and septate xylem fiber cells (septate fiber tracheid, PO:0025419, and septate libriform fiber cell, PO:0025420) of a portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) undergo cell death (GO:0008219). Succeeds a tracheary element death stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025467). Once all cells in portion of secondary xylem are dead, it transitions from sapwood (PO:0004513) to heartwood (PO:0004512), and secondary xylem development ends.

synonyms.text: etapa de muerte de las c&#233lulas parenquim&#225ticas del xilema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次木部柔組織細胞死期 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: cell differentiation stage of secondary xylem
annotations.definition: A secondary xylem development stage (PO:0025427) that occurs during the interval between the end of a cell division stage of secondary xylem (PO:0025463) and the death of the last living cell in the secondary xylem.
annotations.comment: During a this stage, cells of secondary xylem undergo cell differentiation (GO:0030154), including cell growth (GO:0016049), cell morphogenisis (GO:0000902), or cell death (GO:0008219). Cell death is considered part of differentiation in secondary xylem, because it is an essential part of wood development. This stage succeeds the last cell division in a cell division stage of secondary xylem, even though cell differentiation may begin before all cell division finishes.

synonyms.text: etapa de diferenciaci&#243 celular del xilema secundario (Spanish, exact)
二次木部の細胞分化期 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: plant embryo cotyledon
annotations.definition: A cotyledon (PO:0020030) that is part of a plant embryo (PO:0009009).

annotations.label: seedling cotyledon
annotations.definition: A cotyledon (PO:0020030) that is part of a seedling (PO:0008037).
annotations.comment: Develops from an embryo cotyledon (PO:0025470).

annotations.label: reaction wood
annotations.definition: A portion of secondary xylem (PO:0005848) found on either the upper or lower side of a shoot axis (PO:0025029) that is or was angled away from vertical, with anatomical characteristics that depend on whether it is compression wood (PO:0025473) or tension wood (PO:0025474).
annotations.comment: Develops as a response to gravity (either tension or compression) and may function in restoring a stem (PO:0009047) or branch (PO:0025073) that is leaning to its original position. May be either compression wood, found on the lower side of a gymnosperm shoot axis, or tension wood, found on the upper side of an angiosperm shoot axis.

annotations.label: compression wood
annotations.definition: A portion of reaction wood (PO:0025472) found on the lower side of a shoot axis (PO:0025029) that is or was angled away from vertical and that has as parts heavily lignified tracheids (PO:0000301) with a specialized S2 layer that contains more lignin and has a larger (more horizontal) microfibril angle.
annotations.comment: Develops as a response to gravity (compression) and may function in restoring a stem (PO:0009047) or branch (PO:0025073) that is leaning to its original position. Found in gymnosperms.

annotations.label: tension wood
annotations.definition: A portion of reaction wood (PO:0025472) found on the upper side of a shoot axis (PO:0025029) that is or was angled away from vertical and that has as parts gelatinous fiber cells (PO:0025422).
annotations.comment: Develops as a response to gravity (tension) and may function in restoring a stem (PO:0009047) or branch (PO:0025073) that is leaning to its original position. Found in angiosperms. Secondary xylem tracheary elements (PO:0025445) in tension wood are often longer than the tracheary elements in non-tension wood of the same plant. The gelatinous fiber cells found in reaction wood have a cell wall inner layer (G layer) that contains abundant cellulose and is poor in lignin.

annotations.label: coleorhiza emergence stage
annotations.definition: A root development stage (PO:0007520) during which a coleorhiza (PO:0020034) emerges from the seed coat (PO:0009088).
annotations.comment: Occurs in Poaceae and some other monocotyledons. May occur simultaneously with coleoptile emergence from the growth medium.  The actual point of emergence from the seed coat (PO:0009088) may not be observed if the seed (PO:0009010) is underneath a growth medium. In a fruit (PO:0009001) with a persistent pericarp (PO:0009084), emergence from the seed coat may not be observed. If the fruit is a caryopsis, the dry pericarp is fused with the seed coat, and emergence from the pericarp is simultaneous with emergence from the seed coat. This terms is used only for seed plants.

annotations.label: tuberous root tuber
annotations.definition: A root (PO:0009005) that develops from a tuberous root (PO:0025523) and is further radially enlarged for storage.
annotations.comment: Functions in storage. This term should be used for tubers derived from roots (PO:0009005), as found in Cassava or sweet potato (Ipomoea batatas), not for tubers derived from branches (PO:0025073), as found in potatoes and yams. Use the term shoot axis tuber (PO:0004543), aerial tuber (PO:0004548), or subterranean tuber (PO:0004547) for tubers derived from branches. A tuberous root tuber may be distinguished from a tuber that is derived from a branch by the presence of a root cap (PO:0020123) early in development, or the presence of lateral roots (PO:0020121) or their remnants. Sometimes tuber roots bear vegetative buds (PO:0000058) like a tuber derived from a branch. A tuberous root tuber usually develops from a shoot-borne root (PO:0000042) or a lateral root (PO:0020121).

annotations.label: floral organ primordium
annotations.definition: A primordium (PO:0025127) that is committed to the development of a floral organ (PO:0025395) and is part of a reproductive shoot apex (PO:0025222).
annotations.comment: If annotating to a primordium of a floral structure, it is better to use the more specific term, such as petal primordium (PO:0000021) or anther primordium (PO:0006089).

synonyms.text: portion of floral structure primordium tissue (exact)

annotations.label: androecium primordium
annotations.definition: A floral structure primordium (PO:0025477) that is committed to the development of an androecium (PO:0009061).
annotations.comment: Use stamen primordium (PO:0004705) for the primordium (PO:0025127) of an individual stamen (PO:0009029).

synonyms.text: 雄蕊群原基(可視的） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: basal root primordium
annotations.definition: A root primordium (PO:0005029) that is committed to the development of a basal root (PO:0025002).
annotations.comment: Transition from basal root primordium to basal root occurs with the formation of a root cap (PO:0020123), shortly after the development of a root apical meristem (PO:0020147).

synonyms.text: portion of basal root primordium tissue (exact)
primordio basal de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)
下胚軸由来の根の原基(可視的）、不定根原基(可視的）  (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: shoot-borne root primordium
annotations.definition: A root primordium (PO:0005029) that is committed to the development of a shoot-borne root (PO:0000042).
annotations.comment: Transition from shoot-borne root primordium to shoot-borne root occurs with the formation of a root cap (PO:0020123), shortly after the development of a root apical meristem (PO:0020147).

synonyms.text: portion of shoot-borne root primordium tissue (exact)
primordio del eje  del epiblasto de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)
シュート細胞由来の根原基（可視的） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaflet primordium
annotations.definition: A primordium (PO:0025127) that develops as part of a leaf primordium (PO:0000017) of a compound leaf (PO:0020043) and is committed to the development of a leaflet (PO:0020049).
annotations.comment: Leaflets of palms does not develop from leaflet primordia but by separation of segments of a leaf lamina (PO:0020039).

annotations.label: bract anlagen
annotations.definition: A phyllome anlagen (PO:0025430) that will give rise to a bract primordium (PO:0025487) and is part of a peripheral zone (PO:000225) of a reproductive shoot apical meristem (PO:0008028).

annotations.label: lodicule anlagen
annotations.definition: A phyllome anlagen (PO:0025430) that will give rise to a lodicule primordium (PO:0008040) and is part of a peripheral zone (PO:000225) of a flower meristem (PO:0000229).

annotations.label: petal anlagen
annotations.definition: A phyllome anlagen (PO:0025430) that will give rise to a petal primordium (PO:0000021) and is part of a peripheral zone (PO:000225) of a flower meristem (PO:0000229).

annotations.label: sepal anlagen
annotations.definition: A phyllome anlagen (PO:0025430) that will give rise to a sepal primordium (PO:0004704) and is part of a peripheral zone (PO:000225) of a flower meristem (PO:0000229).

annotations.label: stamen anlagen
annotations.definition: A phyllome anlagen (PO:0025430) that will give rise to a stamen primordium (PO:0004705) and is part of a peripheral zone (PO:000225) of a flower meristem (PO:0000229).

annotations.label: bract primordium
annotations.definition: A phyllome primordium (PO:0025487) that is committed to the development of a flower bract (PO:0009055).

annotations.label: flower bract primordium
annotations.definition: A bract primordium (PO:0025128) is committed to the development of a flower bract (PO:0009034).

synonyms.text: portion of flower bract primordium tissue (exact)
primordio de la br&#225ctea floral (Spanish, exact)
花苞（葉）原基（可視的） (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: inflorescence bract primordium
annotations.definition: A bract primordium (PO:0025128) is committed to the development of an inflorescence bract (PO:0009054).

synonyms.text: portion of inflorescence bract primordium tissue (related)
primordio de la br&#225ctea de la inflorescencia (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: plant ovary ovule
annotations.definition: A plant ovule (PO:0020006) that is part of a plant ovary (PO:0009072).
annotations.comment: Found in angiosperms.

annotations.label: ovuliferous scale ovule
annotations.definition: A plant ovule (PO:0020006) that is part of an ovuliferous scale (add).
annotations.comment: Found in conifers.

annotations.label: pericyclic lateral root primordium
annotations.definition: A lateral root primordium (PO:0000016) that develops from a portion of pericycle tissue (PO:0006203) and is committed to the development of a pericyclic lateral root (PO:0025494).

synonyms.text: primordio de ra&#237z lateral peric�clica (Spanish, exact)
内鞘由来分枝根始原体 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: non-pericyclic lateral root primordium
annotations.definition: A lateral root primordium (PO:0000016) that develops from a portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) other than a pericycle (PO:0006203) and is committed to the development of a non-pericyclic lateral root (PO:0025495).
annotations.comment: A non-pericyclic lateral root primordium may develop from cells of an endodermis (PO:0000252) in ferns and some seed plants.

synonyms.text: primordio de ra&#237z lateral no peric�clica (Spanish, exact)
内鞘以外の組織から発生する分枝根始原体 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: pericyclic lateral root
annotations.definition: A lateral root (PO:0025495) that develops from a pericyclic lateral root primordium (PO:0025492).
annotations.comment: A pericyclic lateral root primordium develops from cells that are  part of a pericycle (PO:0006203). This is the most common type of lateral root in seed plants.

annotations.label: non-pericyclic lateral root
annotations.definition: A lateral root (PO:0025495) that develops from a non-pericyclic lateral root primordium (PO:0025493).
annotations.comment: A non-pericyclic lateral root primordium develops from cells that are not part of a pericycle (PO:0006203). Develops from cells in an endodermis (PO:0000252) in ferns and some seed plants.

synonyms.text: 内鞘以外の組織から発生する分枝根 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: multi-tissue plant structure
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0009011) that has as parts two or more portions of plant tissue (PO:0009007) of at least two different types and which through specific morphogenetic processes forms a single structural unit demarcated by primarily bona-fide boundaries from other structural units of different types.
annotations.comment: Most multi-tissue plant structures have at least a small connection to other plant structures via a fiat boundary, such as where a leaf (PO:0025034) connects to a shoot axis (PO:0025029), a petal (PO:0009032) connects to a receptacle (PO:0009064), or a branch (PO:0025073) connects to a stem (PO:0009047).  Refers to CARO:0000055 multi-tissue structure, def'n: Anatomical structure that has as parts two or more portions of tissue of at least two different types, and which through specific morphogenetic processes, form a single distinct structural unit demarcated by bona-fide boundries from other structural units of differnt types.

synonyms.text: estructura vegetal con m&#250ltiples tejidos (Spanish, exact)
複数の組織からなる植物構造 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: collective plant structure
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0009011) that is a proper part of a whole plant (PO:0000003) and includes two or more adjacent plant organs (PO:0009008) or adjacent cardinal organ parts (PO:0025001), along with any associated portions of plant tissue (PO:0009007).
annotations.comment: This is a parent term to describe both collective organ part structure (PO:0025269)  (e.g. septum), as well as collective plant organ structure (PO:0025007) (which was formerly named collective plant structure), for example shoot system (PO:0009006).

annotations.label: cardinal part of multi-tissue plant structure
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0009011) that is a proper part of a multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025496) and includes portions of plant tissues (PO:0009007) of at least two different types.
annotations.comment: Includes cardinal parts of plant organs (PO:0025001) as well as parts of other multi-tissue plant structures. Cardinal refers to the fact that these are biologically meaningful and not arbitrary parts. Most cardinal parts of multi-tissue plant structures have both fiat and bona-fide boundaries, including parts with large fiat boundaries, such as leaf base (PO:0020040) or fruit distal end (PO:0008001), and parts with primarily bona-fide boundaries, such as leaflet (PO:0020049).

synonyms.text: parte cardinal de estructura vegetal con m&#250ltiples tejidos (Spanish, exact)
複数の組織からなる植物構造の基本（主要）部分 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: sepal epidermis giant cell
annotations.definition: An shoot epidermal pavement cell (PO:0000332) that is part of sepal epidermis (PO:0006040) and is approximately one fifth the length of a sepal (PO:0009031).
annotations.comment: Compare to other epidermal pavement cells that are approximately one hundredth of the length of a sepal (PO:0009031). In Arabidopsis, sepal giant epidermal cells arise by DNA endoreplication (GO:0042023) and have high DNA content (up to 16C).

synonyms.text: がく片表皮巨大細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: whole plant fruit development stage
annotations.definition: A sporophyte reproductive stage that begins when a fruit (PO:0009001) on a whole plant (PO:0000003) that is not currently participating in a whole plant fruit development stage begins a fruit initiation stage (PO:0025503), and ends with the earliest of the following: (1) the whole plant begins a sporophyte senescent stage (PO:0007017), (2) the whole plant begins a sporophyte dormant stage (PO:0007132), or (3) all fruits on the whole plant are finished ripening.
annotations.comment: A whole plant fruit development stage begins with the beginning of whole plant fruit formation stage (PO:0007042) and ends with the end of a whole plant fruit ripening stage (PO:0007010). A whole plant fruit development stage can be thought of as a fruiting cycle for a whole plant that begins when the plant starts to produce fruit and ends when all of the fruit on the plant are ripe, unless the plant enters a sporophyte senescent stage or sporophyte dormant stage before all the fruit is ripe. An iteroparous plant will have a single whole plant fruit development stage during its life of whole plant (PO:0025337), while a semelparous plant may have multiple whole plant fruit development stages during its life, each of which will be part of the same instance of a sporophyte reproductive stage. Some plants retain their fruit after ripening and through dormancy, and thus may begin a new whole fruit development stage while fruits from the previous whole plant fruit development stage are still present.

annotations.label: fruit formation stage
annotations.definition: A fruit development stage (PO:0001002) that begins with the formation of a plant zygote (PO:0000423) that is contained in the fruit (PO:0009001) being studied and ends when that fruit's fruit ripening stage (PO:0025502) begins.
annotations.comment: A fruit formation stage may begin without fertilization or formation of a plant zygote (PO:0000423) in cases of parthenocarpy, apomixis, or other hormone-induced conditions. This stage applies to an individual fruit. For the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003), use whole plant fruit formation stage (PO:0007042).

annotations.label: fruit ripening stage
annotations.definition: A fruit development stage (PO:0001002) during which a single fruit (PO:0009001) participates in the process of fruit ripening (GO:0009835).
annotations.comment: Succeeds a fruit formation stage (PO:0025501). This stage is to be used for an individual fruit.  Fruit ripening (GO:0009835) is defined as: an aging process that has as participant a fruit. Ripening causes changes in one or more characteristics of a fruit (color, aroma, flavor, texture, hardness, cell wall structure) and may make it more attractive to animals and aid in seed dispersal. For the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003), use whole plant fruit ripening stage (PO:0007010).

annotations.label: fruit initiation stage
annotations.definition: A fruit formation stage (PO:0025501) that begins with the formation of a plant zygote (PO:0000423) that is contained in the fruit (PO:0009001) being studied and ends when the plant cells (PO:0009002) in the fruit begin to divide or expand.
annotations.comment: The fruit initiation stage may begin without fertilization or formation of a plant zygote (PO:0000423) in cases of parthenocarpy, apomixis, or other hormone-induced conditions. This stage ends as soon as plant cells (PO:0009002) in a developing fruit begin to divide or expand and may be very brief. This stage is to be used for an individual fruit. For the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003), use whole plant fruit formation stage up to 10% (PO:0007032).

annotations.label: fruit size up to 10% stage
annotations.definition: A fruit formation stage (PO:0025501) that begins when the plant cells (PO:0009002) in a fruit (PO:0009001) begin to divide or expand, and ends when the fruit has reached 10% of its final size.
annotations.comment: Succeeds a fruit initiation stage (PO:0025503) and proceeds a fruit size 10 to 30% stage (PO:0025505). This term can only be used if the final size of the fruit is known. This stage is to be used for an individual fruit. For the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003), use whole plant fruit formation stage up to 10% (PO:0007032).

annotations.label: fruit size 10 to 30% stage
annotations.definition: A fruit formation stage (PO:0025501) that spans the interval when a fruit (PO:0009001) has reached 10% to 30% of its final size.
annotations.comment: Succeeds a fruit size up to 10% stage (PO:0025504) and proceeds a fruit size 30 to 50% stage (PO:0025506). This term can only be used if the final size of the fruit is known. This stage is to be used for an individual fruit. For the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003), use whole plant fruit formation stage 10 to 30% (PO:0007009).

annotations.label: fruit size 30 to 50% stage
annotations.definition: A fruit formation stage (PO:0025501) that spans the interval when a fruit (PO:0009001) has reached 30 to 50% of its final size.
annotations.comment: Succeeds a fruit size 10 to 30% stage (PO:0025505) and proceeds a fruit size 50 to 70% stage (PO:0025507). This term can only be used if the final size of the fruit is known. This stage is to be used for an individual fruit. For the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003), use whole plant fruit formation stage 30 to 50% (PO:0007029).

annotations.label: fruit size 50 to 70% stage
annotations.definition: A fruit formation stage (PO:0025501) that spans the interval when a fruit (PO:0009001) has reached 50 to 70% of its final size.
annotations.comment: Succeeds a fruit size 30 to 50% stage (PO:0025505) and proceeds a fruit size 70% to final size stage (PO:0025508). This term can only be used if the final size of the fruit is known. This stage is to be used for an individual fruit. For the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003), use whole plant fruit formation stage 50 to 70% (PO:0007007).

annotations.label: fruit size 70% to final size stage
annotations.definition: A fruit formation stage (PO:0025501) that spans begins when a fruit (PO:0009001) has reached 70% of its final size and ends when a fruit has reached its final size and a fruit ripening stage (PO:0025502) begins.
annotations.comment: Succeeds a fruit size 50 to 70% stage (PO:0025507) and proceeds a fruit ripening stage. This term can only be used if the final size of the fruit is known. This stage is to be used for an individual fruit. For the development stage of a whole plant (PO:0000003), use whole plant fruit formation stage 70% to final size (PO:0007027).

annotations.label: archegonium central cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that is the larger, distal cell arising from the first division of an archegonium initial cell (PO:0025510).
annotations.comment: The archegonium central cell divides asymmetrically to form a smaller ventral canal cell (PO:0025524) and a larger archegonium egg cell (PO:0025122). It is larger than the archegonium neck canal cell (PO:0030065), which also develops from the archegonium initial cell (PO:0025510). The archegonium central cell only occurs in gymnosperms.

annotations.label: archegonium initial cell
annotations.definition: An initial cell (PO:0004011) that divides asymmetrically to form an archegonium central cell (PO:0025509) and a smaller archegonium neck canal cell (PO:0030035).

annotations.label: petal spur
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is a slender, hollow extension of a petal (PO:0009032).
annotations.comment: Often contains nectar. Use this term if the spur is part of a single petal. If the spur is part two or more fused petals, use corolla spur (PO:0025512).

annotations.label: corolla spur
annotations.definition: A collective organ part structure (PO:0025269) that is a slender, hollow extension of a corolla and has as parts segments of two more fused petals.
annotations.comment: Often contains nectar. Use this term if the spur is part of two or more fused petals (PO:0009032). If the spur is part of a single petal, use petal spur (PO:0025511).

annotations.label: plant organ lamina
annotations.definition: A lamina (PO:0025060) that is part of a plant organ (PO:0009008).

annotations.label: phyllome lamina lobe
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is a projection on a phyllome margin (PO:0025018) for which the corresponding phyllome sinus (PO:0025383) extends one quarter or more of the distance to the center of the long axis of the phyllome lamina (PO:0025396).
annotations.comment: A phyllome lobe may be either rounded or angular at the tip, and may have phyllome teeth (PO:0025515) or phyllome crenae (PO:0025516) on its margin. The corresponding phyllome sinus may be rounded or angular.

synonyms.text: フィロム葉身裂片 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome lamina tooth
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is an angular projection on a phyllome margin (PO:0025018) for which the corresponding phyllome sinus (PO:0025383) extends less than one quarter of the distance to the center of the long axis of the phyllome lamina (PO:0025396).
annotations.comment: See phyllome lamina crena (PO:0025516) for a small rounded projection. The corresponding phyllome sinus may be rounded or angular.

annotations.label: phyllome lamina crena
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is a rounded projection on a phyllome margin (PO:0025018) for which the corresponding phyllome sinus (PO:0025383) extends less than one quarter of the distance to the center of the long axis of the phyllome lamina (PO:0025396).
annotations.comment: See phyllome lamina tooth (PO:0025515) for a small angular projection. The corresponding phyllome sinus may be rounded or angular.

synonyms.text: フィロム葉身鈍鋸歯状 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf lamina lobe
annotations.definition: A phyllome lobe (PO:0025514) that is a projection on a leaf lamina margin (PO:0025009) for which the corresponding leaf sinus (PO:0025384) extends one quarter or more of the distance to the center of the long axis of the leaf lamina (PO:0020039).
annotations.comment: A leaf lobe may be either rounded or angular at the tip, and may have leaf teeth (PO:0025518) or leaf crenae (PO:0025519) on its margin. The corresponding leaf sinus may be rounded or angular.

synonyms.text: lóbulo de la lámina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: leaf lamina tooth
annotations.definition: A phyllome lamina tooth (PO:0025515) that is an angular projection on a leaf margin (PO:0020128) for which the corresponding leaf sinus (PO:0025384) extends less than one quarter of the distance to the center of the long axis of the leaf lamina (PO:0020039).
annotations.comment: See leaf lamina crena (PO:0025519) for a small rounded projection. The corresponding leaf sinus may be rounded or angular.

synonyms.text: diente de la lámina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: leaf lamina crena
annotations.definition: A phyllome lamina crena (PO:0025516) that is a rounded projection on a leaf margin (PO:0020128) for which the corresponding leaf sinus (PO:0025384) extends less than one quarter of the distance to the center of the long axis of the leaf lamina (PO:0020039).
annotations.comment: See leaf lamina tooth (PO:0025518) for a small angular projection. The corresponding leaf sinus may be rounded or angular.

synonyms.text: crena de la lámina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: leaf lamina intramarginal vein
annotations.definition: A leaf lamina vein (PO:0020138) that is just inside the leaf lamina margin (PO:0025009) and extends from the leaf lamina base (PO:0008019) to the leaf apex (PO:0020137).
annotations.comment: The leaf lamina intramarginal vein is much thinner than the leaf midvein (PO:0020139; syn: midrib), and is of constant thickness.

synonyms.text: vena intramarginal de la lámina de la hoja (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: unicellular plant gametangium
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that produces one or more gametes (PO:0025006) and participates in the gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003).
annotations.comment: Occurs in some green alga, such as Ulva.  Participates in the gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003).

synonyms.text: 単細胞植物配偶子嚢 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: tuber
annotations.definition: A plant axis (PO:0025004) that is radially enlarged.
annotations.comment: Functions in storage of photosynthate metabolites. May develop from a branch (PO:0025073) or a root (PO:0009005).  If you are annotating to tuber, you should pick the more specific child term: shoot axis tuber (PO:0004543) or tuberous root tuber (PO:0025476).

annotations.label: tuberous root
annotations.definition: A root (PO:0009005) that is radially enlarged relative to other lateral roots (PO:0020121) or shoot-borne roots (PO:0000042) on the same plant, but not as enlarged as a tuberous root tuber (PO:0025476).

annotations.label: ventral canal cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that develops from the archegonium central cell (PO:0025509), and lies at the base of the archegonium neck (PO:0030039), above the archegonium egg cell (PO:0025122) in the venter (PO:0030038).
annotations.comment: The ventral canal cell is smaller than the archegonium egg cell (PO:0025122), which also develops from the asymmetric division of the archegonium central cell (PO:0025509). It lack a plant-type cell wall (GO:0009505) (ref? ). When the archegonium (PO:0025126) is mature, the ventral canal cell (along with the archegonium neck canal cells; PO:0030065), contributes to the formation of the archegonium neck canal (PO:0030066) by distintegrating and becoming mucilaginous. The canal cells and may produce chemicals to attract the male gamete, the antheridium sperm cells (PO:0025120).

annotations.label: spermatogenous cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that gives rise to one or more plant sperm cells (PO:0000084).
annotations.comment: In liverworts, hornworts and mosses the spermatogenous cell gives rise to a single, biflagellated, antheridium sperm cell (PO:0025120). In angiosperms please use the generative cell (PO:0020097) and in gymnosperms use the body cell (PO:0025526).

annotations.label: body cell
annotations.definition: A spermatogenous cell (PO:0025525) of the microgametophyte (PO:0025280) that divides to produce a vegetative cell (PO:0020099) and the pollen sperm cell (PO:0025121).
annotations.comment: Occurs in gymnosperms.

annotations.label: shoot system development stage
annotations.definition: A collective plant organ structure development stage (PO:0025338) that begins with the onset of the plant organ development stage (PO:0025339) and ends with either gametophyte senescent stage (PO:0025343) or sporophyte senescent stage (PO:0007017) or death.
annotations.comment: The shoot system (PO:0009006) develops fron the shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) in the plant embryo (PO:0009009) in vascular plants.  In lower plants the gametophore (PO:0030018) arises from a gametophore meristematic apical cell (PO:0030019) in the protonema (PO:0030003).

annotations.label: bud development stage
annotations.definition: A shoot system development stage (PO:0025527) that has as primary participant a bud (PO:0009006).

annotations.label: bud dormancy stage
annotations.definition: A bud development stage (PO:0025528) during which a bud (PO:0009006) participates in a bud dormancy process (GO:0097207).

annotations.label: reproductive shoot system development stage
annotations.definition: A shoot system development stage (PO:0025527) that has as primary participant a reproductive shoot system (PO:0025082).

annotations.label: bud swell stage
annotations.definition: A bud development stage (PO:0025528) during which a bud (PO:0009006) enlarges due to growth of the shoot system (PO:0009006).

annotations.label: bud burst stage
annotations.definition: A shoot system development stage (PO:0025527) during which a shoot system (PO:0009006) emerges from the bud (PO:0009006).

annotations.label: prothallial cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) which is vegetative and develops from the division of the microspore (PO:0020048) of vascular plants other than angiosperms.
annotations.comment: The prothallial cell (PO:0025533) is part of the microgametophyte (PO:0025280) and is believed to be the remnant of the vegetative tissue of the male gametophyte.

annotations.label: adventitious shoot axis
annotations.definition: A shoot axis (PO:00025029) that develops in an unusual place.

annotations.label: multicellular trichome tip
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the apical most portion of a multicellular trichome apex (PO:0025551).
annotations.comment: This term describes the apex region of a tip of a multicellular trichome (PO:0000282). The tip may be various shapes.  A tip of a unicellular trichome is a GO cellular component term; unicellular trichome tip (GO:0090553).

synonyms.text: 多細胞トリコーム（毛茸、糸状体）先端 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: plant cuticle ridge
annotations.definition: A portion of plant cuticle (PO:000022) that forms a ridge, may be overlayed with epicuticular wax (PO:0025388).

synonyms.text: cresta de la cuticula vegetal (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: unicellular trichome
annotations.definition: A trichome (PO:0000282) that is a single plant cell (PO:0009002).
annotations.comment: This should not be confused with a trichome cell (PO:0008030), which is a plant cell (PO:0009002) that is part of a multicellular trichome (PO:0000282). For parts of the unicellular trichome (such as branch, tip, apex), please see the GO cellular component terms; unicellular trichome apex (GO:0090552), unicellular trichome tip (GO:0090553), and unicellular trichome branch (GO:0090405).

synonyms.text: 単細胞トリコーム（毛茸、糸状体) (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: plant sap
annotations.definition: A portion of plant substance (PO:0025161) that is an aqueous solution that can be transported through the apoplast (GO:0048046) or symplast (GO:0055044).
annotations.comment: Plant sap should not be confused with latex, resins or plant cell cytoplasm.

annotations.label: xylem sap
annotations.definition: A plant sap (PO:0025538) that is an aqueous solution which may contain mineral elements, nutrients, and plant hormones, and is transported from the root system (PO:0025025) toward the leaves through the tracheary elements (PO:0000290) of the xylem.

annotations.label: phloem sap
annotations.definition: A plant sap (PO:0025538) that is an aqueous solution which may contain sugars, mineral elements, and plant hormones, and is transported through the phloem sieve elements (PO:0025406).

annotations.label: bundle sheath cell
annotations.definition: A ground tissue cell (PO:0000076) that is part of a bundle sheath (PO:0006023).

synonyms.text: celula de la vaina del haz vascular (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: bundle sheath chlorenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A chlorenchyma cell (PO:0000076) that is part of a bundle sheath (PO:0006023).

synonyms.text: celula clorenquimatica de la vaina del haz vascular (Spanish, exact)  (exact)
維管束鞘同化組織、葉緑組織細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: tapetum cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that is part of the tapetum (PO:0025313).
annotations.comment: The tapetum is the inner-most layer of a sporangium wall (PO:0025306).

annotations.label: microsporangium tapetum cell
annotations.definition: A tapetum cell (PO:0025543) that is part of a microsporangium tapetum (PO:0025314).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula del tapete del microsporangio (Spanish, exact)
小胞子嚢タペート細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: anther wall tapetum cell
annotations.definition: A microsporangium tapetum cell (PO:0025544) that is part of the anther wall tapetum (PO:0009071).

synonyms.text: célula de la pared del tapete de la antera (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: accessory paraclade
annotations.definition: A second order inflorescence axis (PO:0006321) borne in the same axil (PO:0025224) as an existing second order inflorescence axis.

annotations.label: cauline paraclade
annotations.definition: A second order inflorescence axis (PO:0006321) borne on an elongated first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104).

annotations.label: rosette paraclade
annotations.definition: A second order inflorescence axis (PO:0006321) borne on a compacted first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104), forming the rosette.

synonyms.text: ロゼット側生分枝 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: trichome tip
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0009011) that is the apical most portion of a trichome apex (PO:0025550).
annotations.comment: This term is the parent to the tip of a multicellular trichome (PO:0025162) or the tip of a unicellular trichome (PO:0025537), specifically, a multicellular trichome tip (PO:0025535) and a unicellular trichome tip (GO:0090553), which is a GO cellular component term.

synonyms.text: トリコーム（毛茸、糸状体）先端 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: trichome apex
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0009011) that is the apical most portion of a trichome (PO:0000282).
annotations.comment: This term is the parent to the apex of a multicellular trichome (PO:0025162) or a unicellular trichome (PO:0025537), specifically, a multicellular trichome apex (PO:0025551) and an unicellular trichome apex (GO:0090552) which is a GO cellular component term.

synonyms.text: トリコーム（毛茸、糸状体）頂端 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: multicellular trichome apex
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the apical most portion of a multicellular trichome (PO:0025162).
annotations.comment: This term describes the apex region of a multicellular trichome (PO:0000282).  The apex of a unicellular trichome is a GO cellular component: unicellular trichome apex (GO:0090552).

annotations.label: centarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of protoxylem (PO:0000272) tissue in which the maturation of primary xylem of the shoot system progresses centrifugally with the oldest elements (protoxylem) in the center of the axis.
annotations.comment: This pattern was common in early land plants.

annotations.label: mesarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of protoxylem (PO:0000272) tissue in which the maturation of primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the shoot system (PO:0009006) progresses both centripetally and centrifugally from the center.
annotations.comment: Mesarch protoxylem is found in ferns and cycads stems.

annotations.label: exarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of protoxylem (PO:0000272) tissue in which the maturation of primary xylem (PO:0005849) progresses centripetally, the oldest elements (protoxylem) are farthest from the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).

annotations.label: root system exarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of exarch protoxylem (PO:002554) tissue in which the maturation of primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the root system (PO:0025025) progresses centripedally, with the oldest elements (protoxylem) farthest from the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).

synonyms.text: protoxilema exarco de la raiz (Spanish, exact)  (exact)
根外原型原生木部 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: shoot system exarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of exarch protoxylem (PO:002554) tissue in which the maturation of primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the shoot system (PO:0009006) progresses centripetally, with the oldest elements (protoxylem) farthest from the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).

synonyms.text: protoxilema exarco del sistema del epiblasto (Spanish, exact)
シュート組織外原型原生木部 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: endarch protoxylem
