annotations.definition: A portion of protoxylem (PO:0000272) tissue in which the maturation of primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the shoot system (PO:0009006) progresses centrifugally, with the oldest elements (protoxylem) towards the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).
annotations.comment: Endarch protoxylem is typically found in stems of seed plants, and also in leaves in which the protoxylem is on the adaxial side.

annotations.label: monarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of root exarch protoxylem (PO:0025555) tissue in which the primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the root system (PO:0025025) has only one protoxylem strand or pole, and differentiation progresses centripetally, with the oldest element (protoxylem) farthest from the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).

annotations.label: diarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of root exarch protoxylem (PO:0025555) tissue in which the primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the root system (PO:0025025) has two protoxylem strands or poles, and differentiation progresses centripetally, with the oldest elements (protoxylem) farthest from the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).

annotations.label: triarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of root exarch protoxylem (PO:0025555) tissue in which the primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the root system (PO:0025025) has three protoxylem strands or poles, and differentiation progresses centripetally, with the oldest elements (protoxylem) farthest from the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).

annotations.label: tetrarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of root exarch protoxylem (PO:0025555) tissue in which the primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the root system (PO:0025025) has four protoxylem strands or poles, and differentiation progresses centripetally, with the oldest elements (protoxylem) farthest from the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).

annotations.label: pentarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of root exarch protoxylem (PO:0025555) tissue in which the primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the root system (PO:0025025) has five protoxylem strands or poles, and differentiation progresses centripetally, with the oldest elements (protoxylem) farthest from the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).

annotations.label: polyarch protoxylem
annotations.definition: A portion of root exarch protoxylem (PO:0025555) tissue in which the primary xylem (PO:0005849) of the root system (PO:0025025) has more than five protoxylem strands or poles, and differentiation progresses centripetally, with the oldest elements (protoxylem) farthest from the center of the plant axis (PO:0025004).

annotations.label: megasporangium tapetum cell
annotations.definition: A tapetum cell (PO:0025543) that is part of a megasporangium tapetum (PO:0025315).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula del tapete de megasporangio (Spanish, exact)
大胞子嚢絨毯組織（タペート組織）細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: shoot system xylem sap
annotations.definition: A xylem sap (PO:0025539) that is located in the shoot system (PO:0009006).

synonyms.text: savia del floema del epiblasto (Spanish, exact)  (exact)
シュート組織木部樹液 (Japanese, exact)  (exact)

annotations.label: shoot system phloem sap
annotations.definition: A phloem sap (PO:0025540) that is located in the shoot system (PO:0009006).

synonyms.text: savia del floema del sistema del epiblasto (Spanish, exact)
シュート組織師管液 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: root system phloem sap
annotations.definition: A phloem sap (PO:0025540) that is located in the root system (PO:0025025).

synonyms.text: savia del floema del sistema de la raiz (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: root system xylem sap
annotations.definition: A xylem sap (PO:0025539) that is located in the root system (PO:0025025).

synonyms.text: savia del xilema del sistema de la raiz (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: free petal
annotations.definition: A petal (PO:0009032) that is part of a perianth (PO:0009058) that contains fused sepals (PO:0025584) and/or fused petals (PO:0025583), but is separate from the other sepals (PO:0009031) and petals (PO:0009032).
annotations.comment: A free petal (PO:0025569) only occurs in a perianth (PO:0009058) that contains fused sepals (PO:0025584) and/or fused petals (PO:0025583). If you are describing a petal (PO:0009032) that is part of a perianth (PO:0009058) where none of the sepals (PO:0009031) and/or petals (PO:0009032) are fused, you should use petal.

annotations.label: vascular leaf development stage
annotations.definition: A leaf development stage (PO:0001050) that occurs during the interval between the onset of the vascular leaf initiation stage (PO:0001051) and the termination of the vascular leaf senescent stage (PO:0001054).
annotations.comment: In some plants, the leaf developmental stages may occur in the same leaf simultaneously.  For example, leaves maize (or other grasses) have various zones where stages are occurring along the length of the lamina.

annotations.label: multi-tissue plant structure development stage
annotations.definition: A plant structure development stage (PO:0009012) that has as primary participant a multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025496).

annotations.label: vascular leaf primordium polarity determination stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf initiation stage (PO:0001051) during which the vascular leaf primordium (PO:0004725) develops an adaxial side (PO:0004726) and an abaxial side (PO:0004725).
annotations.comment: The vascular leaf primordium adaxial side (PO:0004726) is the side proximal to the axis of the vegetative shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148), while abaxial side (PO:0004725) is the distal side.

annotations.label: non-vascular leaf development stage
annotations.definition: A leaf development stage (PO:0001050) that has as a primary participant a non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075).

annotations.label: vascular leaf differentiation stage
annotations.definition: A vascular leaf development stage (PO:0025570) that begins with the formation of procambium (PO:0025275) and protoderm (PO:0006210) and ends with the formation and differentiation of the leaf epidermis (PO:0006016) and leaf vascular system (PO:0000036) and all the specialized cells of the vascular leaf.

annotations.label: protoxylem tracheary element
annotations.definition: A primary xylem tracheary element (PO:0025444) with predominately annular or helical secondary wall thickening that are the first formed tracheary elements (PO:0000290) at a particular location.
annotations.comment: In a given portion of primary xylem (PO:0005849), protoxylem tracheary elements are generally smaller in size, fewer in number, un-pitted and have less secondary wall material than metaxylem tracheary elements (PO:0025576).

synonyms.text: elemento de traqueida del protoxilema (Spanish, exact)
原生木部仮導管要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: metaxylem tracheary element
annotations.definition: A primary xylem tracheary element (PO:0025444) that may have helical, scalariform, reticulate and/or pitted secondary wall thickening.
annotations.comment: In a given portion of primary xylem (PO:0005849), metaxylem tracheary elements are generally larger in size, greater in number, are pitted and have more secondary wall material than protoxylem tracheary elements (PO:0025575).

synonyms.text: elemento de traqueida del metaxilema (Spanish, exact)
後生木部仮導管要素 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: epicalyx bract
annotations.definition: A flower bract (PO:0009034) that is part of the epicalyx (PO:0009063).

synonyms.text: がく状総苞 苞葉 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: collective phyllome structure development stage
annotations.definition: A collective plant organ stucture development stage (PO:0025338) that has as primary participant collective phyllome structure (PO:0025023).

synonyms.text: estado de desarrollo de la estructura colectiva del filoma (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: phyllome development stage
annotations.definition: A plant organ development stage (PO:0025339) that has a primary participant a phyllome (PO:0025023).

synonyms.text: estado de desarrollo del filoma (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: fused perianth
annotations.definition: A perianth (PO:0009058) in which one or more of the petals (PO:0009032), sepals (PO:0009031), and/or tepals (PO:0009033) are fused (PATO:0000642).
annotations.comment: The petals (PO:0009032), sepals (PO:0009031) and tepals (PO:0009033) may be fused anywhere along the phyllome margin (PO:0025018). In some cases where the calyx (PO:0009060) and corolla (PO:0009060) are fused at the base it is referred to as a perianth tube.

annotations.label: fused corolla
annotations.definition: A corolla (PO:0009059) that is composed of fused petals (PO:0025583).

annotations.label: fused calyx
annotations.definition: A calyx (PO:0009060) that is composed of fused sepals (PO:0025584).

annotations.label: fused petal
annotations.definition: A petal (PO:0009032) that is part of a fused corolla (PO:0025581) or a fused perianth (PO:0025580).
annotations.comment: In fused perianth (PO:0025580), the fused petal (PO:0025583) may be joined to a fused sepal (PO:002569) or another fused petal.

annotations.label: fused sepal
annotations.definition: A sepal (PO:0009031) that is part of the fused calyx (PO:0025582) or a fused perianth (PO:0025580).

annotations.label: floral organ formation stage
annotations.definition: A flower development stage (PO:0007615) that occurs in an individual flower (PO:000904) during the interval between the onset of a floral organ meristem development stage (PO:0007601) and the onset of a flowering stage (PO:0007616).
annotations.comment: During this stage, the process of floral organ development (GO:0048437) occurs. Floral organ development is defined as the process whose specific outcome is the progression of the floral organ over time, from its formation to the mature structure.

annotations.label: phyllome founder cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that is part of the phyllome anlagen (PO:0025430).
annotations.comment: As a group, the phyllome founder cells encompass several layers of the shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) and form a phyllome anlagen (PO:0025430). Founder cells do not function as stem cells but rather have a determinate fate and can be defined by histological, clonal and molecular criteria.

annotations.label: free sepal
annotations.definition: A sepal (PO:0009031) that is part of a perianth (PO:0009058) that contains fused sepals (PO:0025584) and/or fused petals (PO:0025583), but is separate from the other sepals (PO:0009031) and petals (PO:0009032).
annotations.comment: A free sepal (PO:0025587) only occurs in a perianth (PO:0009058) that contains fused sepals (PO:0025584) and/or fused petals (PO:0025583). If you are describing a sepal (PO:0009031) that is part of a perianth (PO:0009058) where none of the sepals (PO:0009031) and/or petals (PO:0009032) are fused, you should use sepal.

annotations.label: flower meristem transition stage
annotations.definition: A flower development stage (PO:0007615) which begins with the transition of a portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) to a flower meristem (PO:0000229) and ends with the onset of the floral organ formation stage (PO:0025585).
annotations.comment: During this stage, a process occurs involving a vegetative to reproductive phase transition of meristem (GO:0010228), defined as the process involved in transforming a meristem that produces vegetative structures, such as leaves, into a meristem that produces reproductive structures, such as a flower or an inflorescence.

annotations.label: leaf lamina tip
annotations.definition: A leaf tip (PO:0025142) that is the distal portion of the leaf lamina (PO:0020039).
annotations.comment: This is the portion of the leaf lamina (PO:0020039) that is furthest away from the stem (PO:0009047).

annotations.label: receptacle pith
annotations.definition: A portion of pith (PO:0006109) that is part of a receptacle (PO:0009064).

annotations.label: receptacle cortex
annotations.definition: A portion of cortex (PO:0005708) that is part of a receptacle (PO:0009064).

annotations.label: vascular leaf founder cell
annotations.definition: A phyllome founder cell (PO:0025586) that is part of a vascular leaf anlagen (PO:0025431).
annotations.comment: As a group, the vascular leaf founder cells encompass several layers of the shoot apical meristem (PO:0020148) and form a vascular leaf anlagen (PO:0025431). Founder cells do not function as stem cells but rather have a determinate fate and can be defined by histological, clonal and molecular criteria. The analagen may consist of one or more founder cells.

annotations.label: shoot-borne internode root
annotations.definition: A shoot-borne root (PO:0000042) that arises from a shoot axis internode (PO:0005005).

annotations.label: ground meristem
annotations.definition: A portion of meristem tissue (PO:0009013) that gives rise to a portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059).

annotations.label: shoot system ground meristem
annotations.definition: A ground meristem (PO:0025594) that gives rise to a portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059) in the shoot system (PO:0009006).
annotations.comment: The meristem L2 layer (PO:0009021) and meristem L3 layer (PO:0009021) of the are part of the shoot system ground meristem (PO:0025595).

annotations.label: root system ground meristem
annotations.definition: A ground meristem (PO:0025594) that gives rise to a portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059) in the root system (PO:0025025).

annotations.label: ear infructescence
annotations.definition: An infructescence (PO:0006342) in Zea species that bears kernels (i.e. caryopsis fruit; PO:0030104) on a compact ear infructescence axis (PO:0025623) and develops from the ear inflorescence (PO:0020136).

annotations.label: pistillate inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) that includes as part only pistillate flowers (PO:0025599).

annotations.label: pistillate flower
annotations.definition: A flower (PO:0009046) that includes as part a single carpel (PO:0009030) or a gynoecium (PO:0009062), but the androecium (PO:0009061) is absent or has aborted during development.

annotations.label: staminate flower
annotations.definition: A flower (PO:0009046) that includes as part an androecium (PO:0009061), and the gynoecium (PO:0009062) is absent or has aborted during development.

annotations.label: staminate inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) that includes as part only staminate flowers (PO:0025600).

annotations.label: microgametophyte development stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) during which a microgametophyte (PO:0025280) develops.

annotations.label: plant resin
annotations.definition: A portion of plant substance (PO:0025161) that consists of a complex mixture of organic aromatic compounds such as mono-, sesqui-, and diterpenoids.
annotations.comment: Plant resins are often produced by Gymnosperms but not restricted to those plants. In the conifers, plant resins are constitutively and inducibly produced through the secretory activity of resin cells (PO:0025604), and can be exuded through a resin canal (PO:0005665) to a site of injury or predator attack.

annotations.label: resin cell
annotations.definition: A plant epidermal cell (PO:0004013) that secretes plant resin (PO:0025603), and may line resin canals (PO:0005665) and/or be associated with resin blisters (or cysts).

annotations.label: fruit pedicel abscission zone
annotations.definition: An abscission zone (PO:0000146) that forms as a swelling or joint on a fruit pedicel (PO:0030114).

annotations.label: native plant cell
annotations.definition: A plant cell (PO:0009002) that is either part of a multicellular whole plant (PO:0000003) 'in vivo' or a unicellular organism 'in natura' (i.e. part of a natural environment).
annotations.comment: A native plant cell is one that is not grown or maintained in vitro, nor part of an in vitro plant structure (PO:0000004).

annotations.label: vegetative shoot system
annotations.definition: A shoot system (PO:0009006) that has as parts only vegetative tissues and organs.

annotations.label: flower pedicel abscission zone
annotations.definition: An abscission zone (PO:0000146) that forms as a swelling or joint on a flower pedicel (PO:0030113).

annotations.label: shoot axis tegument layer
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that forms a tough, protective outer layer on a shoot axis and includes as parts a shoot axis hypodermis (PO:0005057) and a shoot axis epidermis (PO:0000112).

annotations.label: stem hypodermis
annotations.definition: A portion of hypodermis (PO:0005051) that is part of a stem cortex (PO:0025612).

annotations.label: stem tegument layer
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot axis tegument layer (PO:0025609) that a tough, protective outer layer on a stem (PO:0009047) and includes as parts a stem hypodermis (PO:0025610) and a stem epidermis (PO:0025178).

annotations.label: stem cortex
annotations.definition: A portion of shoot axis cortex (PO:0000223) that is part of a stem (PO:0009047).

annotations.label: spine petiole
annotations.definition: A petiole (PO:0020038) that is sclerified and persistent.
annotations.comment: The petiole (PO:0020038) abcisses longitudinally and the leaf lamina (PO:0020039) withers.

annotations.label: gemma
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) which is a small ball of cells born on a thallus (PO:0030027) and is involved in asexual reproduction.

annotations.label: rosette
annotations.definition: A collective leaf structure (PO:0025022) consisting of a group of rosette leaves (PO:0000014), generally borne at the base of the plant, that are separated by very short stem internodes (PO:0020142).

annotations.label: lemma apiculus
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) which is a short, abrupt, flexible point at the apex of the lemma (PO:0009037).

annotations.label: carpel abaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of carpel epidermis (PO:0025096) that covers the abaxial surface of a carpel (PO:0009030).
annotations.comment: The abaxial surface is the one which is furthest from the plant axis.

annotations.label: carpel adaxial epidermis
annotations.definition: A portion of carpel epidermis (PO:0025096) that covers the adaxial surface of a carpel (PO:0009030).
annotations.comment: The adaxial surface is the one which is closest to the plant axis.

annotations.label: leaf sheath margin
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is the edge of a laminar portion of a leaf sheath (PO:0020104) and the area immediately internally adjacent to it.

annotations.label: metaxylem vessel
annotations.definition: A primary xylem vessel (PO:0025450) which is part of the metaxylem (PO:0000372).
annotations.comment: Metaxylem vessels are composed of metaxylem vessel members (elements; PO:0025622).

annotations.label: metaxylem tracheid
annotations.definition: A primary xylem tracheid (PO:0025446) that is part of the metaxylem (PO:0000372).

annotations.label: metaxylem vessel member
annotations.definition: A primary xylem vessel member (PO:0025448) that is part of the metaxylem (PO:0000372).

annotations.label: ear infructescence axis
annotations.definition: A first order infructescence axis (PO:0025244) in an ear infructescence (PO:0025597) that bears kernels (i.e. caryopsis fruit; PO:0030104).

annotations.label: endodermis cell
annotations.definition: A ground tissue cell (PO:0025030) that is part of a portion of endodermis (PO:0000252).

annotations.label: cortex-endodermis initial cell
annotations.definition: An initial cell (PO:0004011) that produces cortex (PO:0005708) and endodermis (PO:0000252).

annotations.label: plant gall
annotations.definition: A multi-tissue plant structure (PO:0025496) that develops in response to a biotic stress (PSO:0000011).

annotations.label: phloem parenchyma cell
annotations.definition: A parenchyma cell (PO:0000074) that is associated with a sieve tube element (PO:0000289) and stores photosynthate.

annotations.label: aggregate fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) that develops from the merger of several plant ovaries (PO:0009072) that were separated in a single flower (PO:0009046).
annotations.comment: Examples of aggregate fruits include the raspberry (Rubus spp.), which is a collection drupelets.

annotations.label: sporophyte development stage
annotations.definition: A whole plant development stage (PO:0007033) that has as primary participant a whole plant (PO:0000003) during the interval between fertilization (or apogamy) and death.
annotations.comment: A whole plant (PO:0000003) in the sporophyte development stage usually has twice the chromosome complement of a plant in the gametophytic phase, but may not in the case of apogamy. Examples of apogamy include both naturally occurring instances (such as sporophytes arising form haploid cells as in bryophytes or ferns) as well as in vitro instances (such as haploid embryo culture or in vitro fusion of non-gamete cells or protoplasts). During the sporophyte development stage, a plant may produce meiospores by meiosis.

annotations.label: gametophyte development stage
annotations.definition: A whole plant development stage (PO:0007033) that has as primary participant a whole plant (PO:0000003) during the interval between meiosis or apospory and the time that the whole plant ceases to exist.
annotations.comment: In bryophytes and pteridophytes, a gametophytic phase may begin without meiosis by apospory. This usually occurs when damage to a plant in the sporophytic phase leads directly to the growth of a plant that is in the gametophyte development stage but bears the chromosome complement that would normally be found in the sporophyte development stage (PO:0028002). A whole plant in the gametophyte development stage usually has half the chromosome complement of a plant in the sporophyte development stage, but not in the case of apospory. A whole plant in the gametophyte development stage may cease to exist by death at any point in its development. In seed plants, the whole plant in the gametophyte development stage may cease to exists through transformation, such as when pollen (PO:0025281) releases its pollen sperm cells (PO:0025121) or when a plant embryo (PO:0009009) begins to develop within an embryo sac (PO:0025074) of an angiosperm. During the gametophytic phase, a plant may produce gametes (PO:0025006) by mitosis.

annotations.label: branch apex
annotations.definition: A shoot axis apex (PO:0000037) that is part of a branch (PO:0025073).

annotations.label: gametophyte coma
annotations.definition: A collective plant organ structure (PO:0025007) which is a cluster of gametophore branches (PO:0030021) or non-vascular leaves (PO:0025075) at the top of the gametophore axis (PO:0030020), forming a tuft.
annotations.comment: In the moss genus Sphagnum, the gametophore coma is always composed of gametophore branches, but in all other mosses, it is composed of non-vascular leaves.

annotations.label: chloronema cell
annotations.definition: A chlorenchyma cell that is part of a chloronema and has cross walls of adjacent cells that are perpendicular to the protonema axis.
annotations.comment: A chloronema cell contains many well-developed chloroplasts.

annotations.label: caulonema cell
annotations.definition: A chlorenchyma cell that is part of a caulonema and has cross walls of adjacent cells that are oblique to the protonema axis.
annotations.comment: A caulonema cell contains fewer, less well-developed chloroplasts than a chloronema cell.

annotations.label: protonema
annotations.definition: A portion of chlorenchyma tissue that develops directly from a spore and grows by division of an apical cell to form filaments that are one cell wide and dichotomously branching.
annotations.comment: The protonema is the first structure that develops in the gametophytic phase of mosses, some other bryophytes, and leptosporangiate ferns. The term protonema is also used to refer to the phase of development in which protonemal tissue is produced (see PO:0030006 protonema phase). In some species of moss, the protonema is perennial. A protonema may develop from sporophytic tissue during apospory.

annotations.label: chloronema
annotations.definition: A portion of protonema tissue that consists of only chloronema cells.
annotations.comment: Generally, the development of chloronema precedes the development of caulonema. Only the caulonema, and not the chloronema, initiates gametophore buds.

annotations.label: caulonema
annotations.definition: A portion of protonema tissue that consists of only caulonema cells.
annotations.comment: Generally, the development of caulonema follows the development of chloronema. Only the caulonema, and not the chloronema, initiates gametophore buds.

annotations.label: protonema whole plant development stage
annotations.definition: A gametophyte development stage during which a gametophyte undergoes filamentous growth and that succeeds spore germination and precedes a thalloid development stage (planar growth) or gametophore development stage.
annotations.comment: The protonema phase ends with the development of gametophores in mosses, some other bryophytes, and leptosporangiate ferns and with the development of a thallus in other bryophytes, although protonema tissue may persist of continue to develop after this.

synonyms.text: 原糸体を形成する植物発生段階 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A single meristematic cell (PO:0004010) at the tip of a plant structure where apical growth occurs.
annotations.comment: Apical cells occur only at the tip of a shoot axis apex, leaf apex, root apex, thallus apex, or protonema in bryophytes and some pteridophytes. Apical growth results from division of a single meristematic cell located at the tip of an apical meristem or plant organ, rather than from a population of meristematic cells located at the tip of an apical meristem. The apical cell may be tetrahedral shaped, with three (in shoots) or four (in roots) cutting faces, or wedge-shaped (lenticular) with two cutting faces (in non-vascular leaves or thalli). An apical cell may be established upon germination of a spore or upon the first cell division of an embryo, or later.

synonyms.text: 分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: root meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A sporophyte meristematic apical cell that is part of a root tip.
annotations.comment: Occurs in the sporophytic phase of pteridophytes.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical de la ra&#237z (Spanish, exact)
根分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: shoot meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A meristematic apical cell that is part of a shoot system.
annotations.comment: May occur in shoot axes or leaves of bryophytes or ferns.

synonyms.text: シュート分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: shoot axis meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A shoot meristematic apical cell that is part of a shoot apex.
annotations.comment: Divides to produces leaf initial cells (if leaves are present) and other stem or branch tissues.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical del eje del epiblasto (Spanish, exact)
シュート軸分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: leaf meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A shoot meristematic apical cell that is part of a leaf apex.
annotations.comment: Occurs in the non-vascular leaves of bryophytes and the vascular leaves of some ferns. Only in plants where leaf growth is apical.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical de la hoja (Spanish, exact)
葉分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: vascular leaf meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A leaf meristematic apical cell that is part of the leaf apex of a vascular leaf.
annotations.comment: Occurs in vascular leaves of some ferns in their sporophytic phase.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical de la hoja vascular (Spanish, exact)
維管束を持つ葉の分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: non-vascular leaf meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A leaf meristematic apical cell (PO:0030011) that is part of a leaf apex of a non-vascular leaf.
annotations.comment: Occurs in the non-vascular leaves of bryophytes, which grow by division of a single, wedge-shaped apical cell with two cutting faces.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical de la hoja no vascular (Spanish, exact)
維管束を持たない葉の分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: gametophyte meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: An apical cell that is part of a whole plant in the gametophytic phase.
annotations.comment: Occurs bryophytes and pteridophytes.

synonyms.text: 配偶体分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: sporophyte meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A meristematic apical cell that is part of a whole plant in the sporophytic phase.
annotations.comment: Occurs in pteridophytes and the sporophyte of bryophytes.

synonyms.text: 胞子体分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: seta meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A shoot axis meristematic apical cell at the tip of a seta.
annotations.comment: Ceases being an apical cell when the sporangium begins to develop.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical de la seta (Spanish, exact)
柄分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: spore capsule mouth
annotations.definition: A plant anatomical space that is the opening on the apical end of a spore capsule.
annotations.comment: May be covered by a peristome.

synonyms.text: boca de la c&#225psula productora de esporas (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: gametophore
annotations.definition: A shoot system (PO:0009006) that consists of the shoot axes (PO:0025029) and non-vascular leaves (PO:0025075) of a plant in the gametophyte development stage (PO:0028003) .
annotations.comment: A gametophore is the leafy part of the gametophyte of mosses and leafy liverworts, excluding the protonema. Develop from buds that form on the protonema. Antheridia and archegonia arise on the gametophore.

synonyms.text: 茎葉体, 蘚苔類の配偶体 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: gametophore meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A shoot meristematic apical cell that is part of a gametophore.
annotations.comment: Occurs in the non-vascular shoot system of the gametophyte of mosses and leafy liverworts.

synonyms.text: 茎葉体分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: gametophore axis
annotations.definition: A shoot axis (PO:0025029) that is part of a gametophore (PO:0030018).
annotations.comment: Found in mosses and leafy liverworts. A stem or branch of the leafy part of a bryophyte.

annotations.label: gametophore branch
annotations.definition: A branch that is part of a gametophore.
annotations.comment: A branch of the leafy part of a bryophyte shoot system.

annotations.label: gametophore stem
annotations.definition: A stem that is part of a gametophore.
annotations.comment: A stem (the primary axis) of the leafy part of a bryophyte shoot system.

annotations.label: gametophore axis meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A shoot axis meristematic apical cell at the tip of a gametophore axis.
annotations.comment: Occurs at the tips of the stems and branches of bryophytes.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical del eje del gamet&#243foro (Spanish, exact)
茎葉体軸分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: vascular shoot axis meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A shoot axis meristematic apical cell at the tip of a shoot apex in a shoot system that has as part vascular tissue.
annotations.comment: Occurs in some ferns in their sporophytic phase.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical del eje del epiblasto vascualr (Spanish, exact)
維管束を持つシュート軸分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: thallus meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A gametophyte apical cell that is part of a thallus.

synonyms.text: 葉状体分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: gametophore bud
annotations.definition: A vegetative bud that develops into a gametophore.
annotations.comment: Occurs in mosses and leafy liverworts. Develops from a caulonema cell in mosses.

annotations.label: thallus
annotations.definition: A whole plant in the gametophytic phase that has a flat growth form and no distinct organs.
annotations.comment: A thallus is a gametophyte of liverworts and pteridophytes and develops from a short-lived protonema. Roughly two dimensional growth results from division of a single apical cell. Thalli may be ribbon or heart shaped or almost filamentous. Although there are no distinct organs, there may be tissue differentiation and dichotomous branching.

annotations.label: perigonial bract
annotations.definition: A bract (PO:0009055) that subtends an antheridium (PO:0025125).
annotations.comment: When an antheridium occurs on a specialized lateral branch of the gametophore, all of the phyllomes on that branch are usually perigonial bracts. If an antheridium occurs on a main axis of the gametophore, usually only the terminal phyllomes are perigonial bracts.

annotations.label: sporophyte foot
annotations.definition: A plant organ that is the base of a whole plant in the sporophytic phase and attaches it to the gametophore.
annotations.comment: Found in bryophytes and some pteridophytes. In mosses, the foot is located below the seta. In ferns, the foot is formed from the upper hypobasal quandrant of the embryo. The sporophyte foot serves for both attachment and absorption. The outer portion of the foot is composed of absorptive transfer cells.

annotations.label: perichaetal bract
annotations.definition: A bract (PO:0009055) that subtends an archegonium (PO:0025126).
annotations.comment: When an archegonium occurs on a specialized lateral branch of the gametophore, all of the phyllomes on that branch are usually perichaetal bracts. If an archegonium occurs on a main axis of the gametophore, only the terminal phyllomes are usually perichaetal bracts. The two or three terminal-most perichaetal bracts may fuse to form a gametophytic perianth.

annotations.label: gametophyte perianth
annotations.definition: A collective phyllome structure that consists of two or more of the most distal perichaetal bracts on a gametophore axis and surround a capsule.
annotations.comment: The perichaetal bracts may fuse laterally in the gametophytic perianth. The gametophytic perianth is not the same structure as a perianth (PO:0009058) in angiosperms.

annotations.label: seta
annotations.definition: A stalk (PO:0025066) that supports a spore capsule, the sporangium (PO:0025094).
annotations.comment: Found in some bryophytes, especially mosses and liverworts.

synonyms.text: とげ、剛毛、コケの柄 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: antheridiophore
annotations.definition: A stalk (PO:0025066) that supports an antheridium head (PO:0030075).
annotations.comment: Found in mosses and other bryophytes such as the liverwort Marchantiales.

annotations.label: archegoniophore
annotations.definition: A stalk (PO:0025066) that supports an archegonium head (PO:0030034).
annotations.comment: Found in Marchantiales.

annotations.label: antheridium stalk
annotations.definition: A stalk (PO:0025066) that is the basal part of an antheridium (PO:0025125).
annotations.comment: The antheridium stalk raises the antheridium (PO:0025125) from the antheridium head (PO:0030075).

annotations.label: archegonium stalk
annotations.definition: A stalk (PO:0025066) that the basal part of an archegonium (PO:0025126).
annotations.comment: Raises the venter above the rest of the gametophyte.

annotations.label: spore capsule calyptra
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that develops from an archegonium (PO:0025126) and is a cap or hood enclosing the sporangium (syn: spore capsule; PO:0025094).
annotations.comment: Found in bryophytes. The spore capsule calyptra is a membranous or hairy cap or hood, composed of gametophytic tissue (venter; PO:0030038) that protects the embryonic sporophyte within the archegonium (PO:0025126). In some species, it may persist after the sporophyte develops and gets carried upward as the seta (PO:0030032) elongates. The spore capsule calyptra is usually lost before the spores are released, and its shape can be used for identification purposes.

synonyms.text: caliptra de la c&#225psula productora de esporas (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: venter
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is the enlarged basal part of an archegonium (PO:0025126) and has an archegonium egg cell (PO:0025122) located in it.
annotations.comment: After fertilization, the venter develops into the spore capsule calyptra (PO:0030037), which protects the sporangium (syn: spore capsule; PO:0025094) containing the developing sporophyte.

annotations.label: archegonium neck
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is the elongated, apical part of an archegonium (PO:0025126).
annotations.comment: Early in development, the archegonium neck is occluded by a single row of neck canal cells (PO:0030065). At maturity, the neck canal cells (PO:0030065) and the ventral canal cell (PO:0025524) disintegrate, creating a archegonium neck canal (PO:0030066) for the plant sperm cells (PO:0000084) to enter the archegonium (PO:0025126).

annotations.label: sporangium base
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part that is the basal (distal) part of a sporangium.
annotations.comment: Term used for bryophytes and pteridophytes. The sporangium base is the sterile part of the sporangium below the sporangium theca. If swollen and distinct from the rest of the sporangium, it is called an apophysis or hypophysis.

annotations.label: sporangium theca
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part that is the main body of a sporangium and has spores located in it.
annotations.comment: Term used for bryophytes and pteridophytes.

annotations.label: peristome
annotations.definition: A single or double circle of peristome teeth inside a sporangium mouth.
annotations.comment: The peristome is located under the operculum, if an operculum is present. Upon maturity of a capsule, the teeth of the peristome open to release the spores.

annotations.label: peristome tooth
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part that is a single lobe of a peristome.
annotations.comment: Generally triangular. Peristome teeth may be simple or compound, and may be attached by their tips to the epiphragm.

synonyms.text: 蒴歯歯片、縁歯歯片,　歯環を構成する個々の歯(Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: spore capsule operculum
annotations.definition: A cardinal organ part that is the apical part of a spore capsule that separates from the rest of the capsule during dehiscence.
annotations.comment: Found in mosses. May remain partly attached to the rest of the capsule.

synonyms.text: op&#233rculo de la c&#225psula productora de esporas (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: stereid
annotations.definition: A ground tissue cell with thickened cell walls.
annotations.comment: Found only in bryophytes in gametophore axes, setae, or leaves. Functions in support rather than conduction. May or may not retain a living protoplast at maturity.

annotations.label: protonema meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A gametophyte meristematic apical cell that is part of a protonema.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula meristem&#225tica apical del protonema (Spanish, exact)
原糸体、糸状体の分裂組織の頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: hydrome
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that has as parts hydroid (PO:0025032) cells and cells of no other types.
annotations.comment: A hydrome is composed of only hydroids. Found in bryophytes, in the center of a non-vascular shoot axis (PO:0025029) such as a gametophore (PO:0030018) or seta (PO:0030032). Often surrounded by the leptome (PO:0030048). May function in water conduction and may provide support.

annotations.label: leptome
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that has as parts leptoid (PO:0025033) cells and cells of no other types.
annotations.comment: A leptome is composed of only leptoids (PO:0025033). Found in bryophytes, in non-vascular shoot axes such as a gametophore axis or seta (PO:0030032) or in the midrib of a non-vascular leaf. Often surrounding a central hydrome. May function in the conduction of organic substances, similar to phloem in vascular plants.

annotations.label: sporangium wall endothecium
annotations.definition: A portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059) that is the sub-epidermal layer of a sporangium wall (PO:0025306).
annotations.comment: Term used for vascular plants to describe the outermost layer of a sporangium wall that is internal to the exothecium.

synonyms.text: endotecio de la pared del esporangio (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: stereome
annotations.definition: A portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059)  that has as parts stereid cells (PO:0030045) and no other cell types.
annotations.comment: A stereome is composed of only stereids. Found only in bryophytes in gametophore axes or non-vascular leaves. May function in support.

annotations.label: central strand
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is an axial strand in the center of a gametophore axis (PO:0030020) or seta (PO:0030032) and has as part a hydrome (PO:0030047) or a leptome (PO:0030048).
annotations.comment: Often contains both hydrome and leptome. Found in bryophytes. The term central strand is sometimes used to describe a protostele in vascular plants, but this ontology class should only be used for non-vascular plants. Analogous to a vein or vascular bundle in vascular plants.

annotations.label: antheridium jacket layer cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that is part of an antheridium jacket layer (PO:0030053).

synonyms.text: capa c&#233lular de la cubierta del anteridio (Spanish, exact)
造精器被覆層細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: antheridium jacket layer
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is a single layer of cells on the outside of an antheridium (PO:0025125).
annotations.comment: Cells in the antheridium jacket layer surround the spermatogenous cells (PO:0025525) and do not give rise to antheridium sperm cells (PO:0025120).

synonyms.text: capa de la cubierta del anteridio (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: epiphragm
annotations.definition: A portion of parenchyma tissue that forms a circular membrane extending from a spore capsule columella and attached to the ends of the peristome teeth that covers a spore capsule mouth.
annotations.comment: Present in some moss species of the family Polytrichaceae.

annotations.label: pseudopodium
annotations.definition: A gametophore axis that is a leafless extension of a gametophore stem or branch on which a sporophyte is borne.
annotations.comment: Raises the sporophyte above the perichaetial bracts. Found in Sphagnum and Andreaea.

annotations.label: archesporial cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that divides to gives rise to a sporocyte (PO:0006204) and is part of a sporangium (PO:0025094).
annotations.comment: May also give rise to sterile cells such as elators (in liverworts) or tapetal cells. In most species outside seed plants, multiple archesporial cells are part of an archesporium or sporogenous tissue.

annotations.label: alar cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) located at the base of a non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075) adjacent to where the leaf attaches to the stem (PO:0009047).
annotations.comment: Found in mosses. Alar cells occur in an area at the base of a leaf that may be triangular, ovate or rectangular and is sometimes called the alar region. They are different in size, shape, color, thickness, or ornamentation from other cells in the leaf.

annotations.label: brachycyte
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that develops from a cell in a protonema (PO:0030003) and has a thick cell wall.
annotations.comment: Found in mosses. A brachycyte is a drought tolerant cell that can act as a gemma (PO:0025614) and may give rise to a new plant. Cells of a protonema (PO:0030003) may develop into brachycytes under stress. May form a chain or be separated by tmema cells (PO:0030059).

synonyms.text: 原糸体細胞の細胞壁を厚くした細胞、耐性細胞(brood cell?) (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: tmema cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) at the base of a gemma (PO:0025614) or adjacent to a brachycyte (PO:0030058) that breaks down to allow abscission of the gemma or brachycyte.
annotations.comment: May be a single cell adjacent to a brachycyte (PO:0030058) or part of a portion of tmema tissue.

annotations.label: gametophore axillary hair
annotations.definition: An axillary hair that grows in a leaf axil of a non-vascular leaf.
annotations.comment: Found in pleurocarpous mosses. Form in the axils of leaf primordia and may disappear when the gametophore is mature.

annotations.label: tmema
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that has as parts tmema cells (PO:0030059).
annotations.comment: Found in bryophytes at the base of a gemma (PO:0025614). Participates in abscission of the gemma.

annotations.label: gametophore axillary hair terminal cell
annotations.definition: An axillary hair terminal cell that is the long terminal cell of a gametophore axillary hair.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula terminal de pelo axilar del gamet&#243foro (Spanish, exact)
茎葉体腋毛終端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: gametophore axillary hair base
annotations.definition: A axillary hair base that is the basal part of a gametophore axillary hair, below the gametophore axillary hair terminal cell.
annotations.comment: Consists of two to a dozen cells.

synonyms.text: base del pelo axilar del gamet&#243foro (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: gametophore axillary hair basal cell
annotations.definition: An axillary hair basal cell that is part of a gametophore axillary hair base.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula basal del pelo axilar del gamet&#243foro (Spanish, exact)
茎葉体腋毛基底細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: archegonium neck canal cell
annotations.definition: A native plant cell (PO:0025606) that is one of the axial row of cells in an immature archegonium neck (PO:0030039).
annotations.comment: During archegonium development, the archegonium neck canal cells (PO:0030065) and the ventral canal cell (PO:0025524) disintegrate, creating a archegonium neck canal for the plant sperm cells (PO:0000084) to enter the archegonium (PO:0025126).

synonyms.text: c&#233lula del canal del cuello del arquegonio (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: archegonium neck canal
annotations.definition: A canal (PO:0025132) in the center of an archegonium neck (PO:0030039).
annotations.comment: During archegonium development, the archegonium neck canal cells (PO:0030065) and the ventral canal cell (PO:0025524) disintegrate, creating a archegonium neck canal for the plant sperm cells (PO:0000084) to enter the archegonium (PO:0025126).

synonyms.text: canal del cuello del arquegonio (Spanish, exact)

annotations.label: protonema side branch initial cell
annotations.definition: An protonema sub-apical initial that gives rise to a branch of a protonema.
annotations.comment: May give rise to a chloronema, caulonema, protonema rhizoid, tmema cell, or brachycyte.

synonyms.text: rama lateral inicial del protonema (Spanish, exact)
原糸体側枝始原細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: epidermal rhizoid initial cell
annotations.definition: A plant epidermal initial cell (PO:0000349) that gives rise to an epidermal rhizoid (PO:0030071).
annotations.comment: Part of the epidermis of a thallus or gametophore of a bryophyte or pteridophyte in the gametophytic phase or the stem of a monocot in the sporophytic phase.

synonyms.text: 表皮仮根始原細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: paraphyllium
annotations.definition: A portion of plant epidermis (PO:0005679) tissue that is an outgrowth from the epidermis between the leaves of a gametophore axis (PO:0030020).
annotations.comment: Paraphyllia are much smaller than leaves and may be filamentous, scale-like, or leaf-like. Found in pleurocarpous mosses and a few leafy liverworts.

annotations.label: paraphysis
annotations.definition: A slender, usually uniseriate multicellular trichome (PO:0025162) that grows intermixed with the gametangia.
annotations.comment: Found in bryophytes and pteridophytes, especially in mosses.

annotations.label: epidermal rhizoid
annotations.definition: A rhizoid (PO:0030078) that develops from an epidermal rhizoid initial cell (PO:0030068).
annotations.comment: Found in bryophytes and pteridophytes growing from the epidermis of a gametophore or the lower surface of a thallus and in some monocots growing from the stem at the cotyledonary node. May be considered a type of trichome.

annotations.label: costa
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is a single or double strand in the center of a non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075) and has as part hydrome (PO:0030047) or leptome (PO:0030048).
annotations.comment: Found in bryophytes. Often contains both hydrome and leptome. Functions in support and conduction in a phyllid. The term costa is sometimes used for the midrib of monocot leaves, but this ontology class should only be used for non-vascular leaves.

annotations.label: exothecium
annotations.definition: A portion of plant epidermis (PO:0005679) that is the outermost layer of a sporangium wall (PO:0025306).
annotations.comment: Develops from the outermost layer of an amphithecium in bryophytes.

annotations.label: archesporium
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the internal part of a sporangium (PO:0025094), bounded by the sporangium wall (PO:0025306), and has as parts archesporial cells (PO:0030056).
annotations.comment: Gives rise to sporocytes and may give rise to other sterile cells such as elators.

annotations.label: antheridium head
annotations.definition: A disk-shaped cardinal organ part (PO:0025001) that is the apical portion of an antheridiophore (PO:0030033) and bears antheridia (PO:0025125).
annotations.comment: Found in mosses and other bryophytes such as the liverwort Marchantiales.

annotations.label: archegonium head
annotations.definition: An umbrella-shaped cardinal organ part that is the apical portion of an archegoniophore and bears archegonia.
annotations.comment: Found in Marchantiales.

annotations.label: protonemal side branch rhizoid initial cell
annotations.definition: A protonema side branch initial that gives rise to a protonemal rhizoid.

synonyms.text: rama lateral inicial del rizoide proton&#233mico (Spanish, exact)
原糸体側枝仮根始原細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: rhizoid
annotations.definition: A plant structure (PO:0009011) that is a uniseriate filament with positive geotropism that grows from an epidermis (PO:0005679) or protonema (PO:0030003).
annotations.comment: May be unicellular or multicellular. Serves to anchor the plant to the substrate, and may function in nutrient and water uptake. Usually lacks chlorophyll. Includes epidermal rhizoids that grow from the epidermis of bryophytes and pteridophytes in the gametophytic phase or from the stems of some monocots, protonemal rhizoids that branch from a protonema, and rhizoids that develop from a basal cell of a gametophore bud.

annotations.label: protonemal rhizoid
annotations.definition: A rhizoid that is part of a protonema and develops from a protonemal side branch rhizoid initial.

annotations.label: rhizoid meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A gametophyte meristematic apical cell that is part of a rhizoid.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical meristem&#225tica del rizoide (Spanish, exact)
仮根分裂頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: caulonema meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A protonema meristematic apical cell that is part of a caulonema.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical meristem&#225tica del caulonema (Spanish, exact)
カウロネマ分裂頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: chloronema meristematic apical cell
annotations.definition: A protonema meristematic apical cell that is part of a chloronema.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula apical meristem&#225tica del cloronema (Spanish, exact)
クロロネマ分裂頂端細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: gametophore bud initial cell
annotations.definition: A protonema sub-apical initial hat gives rise to a gametophore bud.
annotations.comment: In Physcomitrella patens, a gametophore bud initial typically is more bulbous than a protonema side branch initial, and its plane of first cell division is oblique, rather than perpendicular, to the plane of growth.

synonyms.text: 茎葉体芽始原細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: amphithecium
annotations.definition: A portion of plant tissue (PO:0009007) that is the outer layer or layers of a sporangium (PO:0025094) early in sporangium development (GO:0043582).
annotations.comment: Term used for bryophytes. May have one or more layers. An amphithecium may give rise to an exothecium and other layers of a sporangium wall (jacket layer or layers). In true mosses, the amphithecium gives rise to the sporangium wall and the peristome. In hornworts and peat mosses, it gives rise to the sporangium wall and sporogenous tissue.

synonyms.text: アンフィテシウム細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: protosporangium endothecium
annotations.definition: A portion of ground tissue (PO:0025059) that is the tissue internal to an amphithecium early in sporangium development.
annotations.comment: Term used for bryophytes. In liverworts an early sporangium endothecium produces sporogenous tissue. In Sphagnum, Andreaea, and hornworts, it produces columella and sporogenous tissue.

annotations.label: protonema sub-apical initial cell
annotations.definition: An initial cell that is produced by longitudinal division of a sub-apical cell of a protonema.
annotations.comment: May give rise to a branch of the protonema (including a protonema rhizoid, tmema cell or brachycyte), a gametophore bud, or may not divide further.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula subapical inicial del protonema (Spanish, exact)
原糸体次頂端始原細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: non-vascular leaf initial cell
annotations.definition: An initial cell (PO:0004011) that is part of a gametophore (PO:0030018) and gives rise to a non-vascular leaf (PO:0025075).
annotations.comment: In Physcomitrella patens, non-vascular leaf initials arise from the daughter cells of a gametophore axis apical cell. Subsequent division of a non-vascular leaf initial establishes a non-vascular leaf apical cell.

synonyms.text: c&#233lula inicial de la hoja no vascular (Spanish, exact)
非維管束葉の始原細胞 (Japanese, exact)

annotations.label: lateral root spine
annotations.definition: A lateral root (PO:0020121) that is sclerified and pointed.
annotations.comment: Lateral root spines cannot grow indeterminately or supply the plant with water and solutes. See JSTOR:43235457 for documentation of lateral root spines in Dioscorea prehensilis (Dioscoreaceae) and Moraea sp. (Iridaceae).

annotations.label: shoot-borne root spine
annotations.definition: A shoot-borne root (PO:0000042) that is sclerified and pointed.
annotations.comment: Shoot-borne root spines cannot grow indeterminately or supply the plant with water and solutes. See PO_REF:00015 for documentation of shoot-borne root spines in Acanthorhiza aculeata (Arecaceae).

annotations.label: root periderm scar
annotations.definition: A portion of root periderm (PO:0005047) formed in response to a disruption of the root epidermis (PO:0006036).
annotations.comment: Root periderm scars are lenticel-like structures on the tuberous roots (PO:0025523), primary roots (PO:0020127), and/or tuberous root tubers (PO:0025476) of plants such as sweet potato, cassava, parsnips, and carrots. Disruption of the epidermis by the emergence of a lateral root initiates a wound response which results in the production of a root periderm scar. Gas exchange does not occur through a root periderm scar.

annotations.label: capsule fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a gynoecium (PO:0009062) and at maturity comprises a dry exocarp (PO:0009085), dry mesocarp (PO:0009087), and dry endocarp (PO:0009086) that usually dehisce.
annotations.comment: Capsule fruits (PO:0030091) are divided into subcategories depending upon the location of sutures in the pericarp (PO:0009084) that the seeds (PO:0009010) are released through.

annotations.label: loculicidal capsule fruit
annotations.definition: A capsule fruit (PO:0030091) which at maturity dehisces through fruit locules (PO:0025270).
annotations.comment: When annotating to a capsule fruit (PO:0030091) which dehisces through both the fruit septa (PO:0025268) and fruit locules (PO:0025270), annotate to coccarium capsule fruit (PO:0030094). When annotating to a capsule fruit which only dehisces through the fruit septa, annotate to septicidal capsule fruit (PO:0030093). Examples: cotton (Gossypium raimondii) and rose mallow (Hibiscus syriacus).

annotations.label: septicidal capsule fruit
annotations.definition: A capsule fruit (PO:0030091) which dehisces through a fruit septa (PO:0025268).
annotations.comment: When annotating to a capsule fruit (PO:0030091) which dehisces through both the fruit septa (PO:0025268) and fruit locules (PO:0025270), annotate to coccarium capsule fruit (PO:0030094). When annotating to a capsule fruit which only dehisces through the fruit locules, annotate to loculicidal capsule fruit (PO:0030092). Examples: dutchman's pipe (Aristolochia elegans) and black cottonwood (Populus trichocarpa).

annotations.label: coccarium capsule fruit
annotations.definition: A capsule fruit (PO:0030091) which at maturity dehisces through both the fruit septa (PO:0025268) and the fruit locules (PO:0025270).
annotations.comment: When annotating to a capsule fruit (PO:0030091) which only dehisces through the fruit septa (PO:0025268), annotate to septicidal capsule fruit (PO:0030093). When annotating to a capsule fruit which only dehisces through the fruit locules (PO:0025270), annotate to loculicidal capsule fruit (PO:0030092). Examples: physic nut (Jatropha curcas) and Manihot brachyloba.

annotations.label: poricidal capsule fruit
annotations.definition: A capsule fruit (PO:0030091) which at maturity dehisces through pores on a pericarp (PO:0009084).
annotations.comment: Example: poppy (Papaver spp.).

annotations.label: circumscissile capsule fruit
annotations.definition: A capsule fruit (PO:0030091) which at maturity dehisces through the release of a fruit operculum (PO:0025298).
annotations.comment: Example: rose gum (Eucalyptus grandis).

annotations.label: utricle capsule fruit
annotations.definition: A circumscissile capsule fruit (PO:0030096) which at maturity contains one seed (PO:0009010) that is not attached to a pericarp (PO:0009084).
annotations.comment: Example: common amaranth (Amaranthus retroflexus).

annotations.label: schizocarp fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a gynoecium (PO:0009062) and at maturity comprises a dry exocarp (PO:0009085), dry mesocarp (PO:0009087), and dry endocarp (PO:0009086) that separate into mericarps (PO:0020075).
annotations.comment: Each individual carpellate unit of a schizocarp fruit (PO:0030098) is a mericarp (PO:0020075). Example: maple (Acer spp.).

annotations.label: samara fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a carpel (PO:0009030) and at maturity comprises a dry exocarp (PO:0009085), dry mesocarp (PO:0009087), and dry endocarp (PO:0009086) that are elongated into a wing-like structure.
annotations.comment: The pericarp (PO:0009084) can be expanded laterally or radially. The wing-shaped fruits of maples (Acer spp.) are often mistakenly identified as samara fruits (PO:0030099), but are instead schizocarp fruits (PO:0030098) with samaroid mericarps (PO:0020075).\nExamples: green ash (Fraxinus pennsylvanicum) and tree-of-heaven (Ailanthus altissima).

annotations.label: legume fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a carpel (PO:0009030) and at maturity comprises a dry exocarp (PO:0009085), dry mesocarp (PO:0009087), and dry endocarp (PO:0009086) that dehisce along two fruit valves (PO:0000033).
annotations.comment: Legume fruit (PO:0030100) is characteristic of the plant family Fabaceae. Examples: pigeon pea (Cajanus cajan), chickpea (Cicer arietinum), peanut (Arachis hypogaea), soybean (Glycine max), and lentil (Lens culinaris subsp. culinaris).

annotations.label: loment fruit
annotations.definition: A legume fruit (PO:0030100) which at maturity separates into indehiscent, one-seeded segments.
annotations.comment: The pericarp (PO:0009084) of some legume fruit (PO:0030100), such as peanuts, is constricted between each seed, but these constrictions are due to a lack of support of the pericarp (PO:0009084) in the space between the seeds (PO:0009010). Contrastingly, the constrictions in the pericarp of loment fruits (PO:0030101) are sclerified and contain regions where cell death leads to disarticulation of the fruit (PO:0009001). Examples: some members of Fabaceae, including Mimosa and Desmodium spp.

annotations.label: nut fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a gynoecium (PO:0009062) and at maturity comprises a dry exocarp (PO:0009085), dry mesocarp (PO:0009087), and dry endocarp (PO:0009086) that are not fused to a seed coat (PO:0009088).
annotations.comment: The common names of some species do not accurately reflect their fruit type. For example, the fruit (PO:0009001) of the peanut plant is a legume fruit (PO:0030100) rather than a nut fruit (PO:0030102). Example: pecan (Carya illinoinensis), walnut (Juglans regia), pistachio (Pistacia vera), lotus (Nelumbo nucifera), and acorn (Quercus spp.).

annotations.label: drupe fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a carpel (PO:0009030) and at maturity comprises a fleshy exocarp (PO:0009085), fleshy mesocarp (PO:0009087), and a stony endocarp (PO:0009086).
annotations.comment: May develop from an inferior or superior carpel (PO:0009030) and may contain one or more seeds (PO:0009010). Examples: Amborella trichopoda (basal angiosperm), coffee (Coffea canephora), Cornaceae (Davidia spp.), cashew (Anacardium occidentale), coconut (Cocos nucifera), and almond, peach and plum (Prunus spp.).

annotations.label: caryopsis fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a gynoecium (PO:0009062) and at maturity comprises a dry exocarp (PO:0009085), dry mesocarp (PO:0009087), and dry endocarp (PO:0009086) that are fused to a seed coat (PO:0009088).
annotations.comment: Caryopsis fruit (PO:0030104) is characteristic of the Poaceae (the grass family). Caryopsis fruits develop from a pseudomonomerous gyneocium (PO:0009062), and contain only a single seed due to the abortion of two of the three carpels (PO:0009030). The pericarp of caryopsis fruits of some bamboo species is somewhat fleshy at maturity and is fused to the seed coat; these fruits are referred to in the literature as bacoid caryopses. Examples: barley (Hordeum vulgare), rice (Oryza spp.), wheat (Triticum spp.), corn (Zea mays), sorghum (Sorghum bicolor), oat (Avena spp.), zebra grass (Miscanthus spp.), Tausch’s goatgrass (Aegilops tauschii), stiff brome (Brachypodium distachyon), cutgrass (Leersia perrieri), and foxtail millet (Setaria italica).

annotations.label: follicle fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a carpel (PO:0009030) and at maturity comprises a dry exocarp (PO:0009085), dry mesocarp (PO:0009087), and dry endocarp (PO:0009086) that dehisce along a single fruit valve (PO:0000033).
annotations.comment: Examples: milkweed (Asclepias syriaca) and carolina larkspur (Delphinium carolinianum).

annotations.label: silique fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a gynoecium (PO:0009062) and at maturity comprises a dry exocarp (PO:0009085), dry mesocarp (PO:0009087), and dry endocarp (PO:0009086), that dehisce along two fruit valves (PO:0000033) and separate from a persistent fruit replum (PO:0025267).
annotations.comment: In contrast to legume fruit (PO:0030100), silique fruit develop from a syncarpous (multicarpellate) gynoecium (PO:0009062) separated by a false septum (replum, PO:0025267), which persists through fruit maturity. Examples: members of Brassicaceae, lyrate rockcress (Arabidopsis lyrata), mouse-ear cress (Arabidopsis thaliana), wild cabbage (Brassica oleracea), field mustard (Brassica rapa), and pink shepherd's purse (Capsella rubella).

annotations.label: achene fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which develops from a carpel (PO:0009030) or gyneocium (PO:0009062) and at maturity comprises a dry exocarp (PO:0009085), a dry mesocarp (PO:0009087), and a dry endocarp (PO:0009086) that are connected to a seed coat (PO:0009088) by a funicle (PO:0020006).
annotations.comment: Plant structures commonly referred to as ‘accessory’ (such as strawberry) or multiple fruits (such as figs..) which are comprised of accrescent receptacle (PO:0009064) or infructescence (PO:0006342) tissue, contain as parts achene fruits (PO:0030107). For example, when annotating for a gene involved in strawberry development, any gene of interest that is involved in the development of the receptacle tissue would be mapped to receptacle (PO:0009064); genes involved in sepal (PO:0009031) persistence or accrescence would map to sepal (PO:0009031), and those involved in fruit characteristics would be mapped to achene (PO:0030107). Examples: sunflower (Helianthus annuus),  strawberry (Fragaria spp.), fig (Ficus sp.), and marijuana (Cannabis sativa).

annotations.label: berry fruit
annotations.definition: A fruit (PO:0009001) which at maturity comprises a fleshy endocarp (PO:0009086), fleshy mesocarp (PO:0009087), and fleshy exocarp (PO:0009085).
annotations.comment: Examples: pepper (Capsicum annuum), papaya (Carica papaya), avocado (Persea americana), may apple (Podophyllum peltatum), date palm (Phoenix dactylifera), tomato (Solanum lycopersicum), potato (Solanum tuberosum), chocolate (Theobroma cacao), blueberry (Vaccinium spp.), and grape (Vitis vinifera).

annotations.label: hesperidium fruit
annotations.definition: A berry fruit (PO:0030108) which develops from a superior gynoecium (PO:0009062) and at maturity comprises a thickened exocarp (PO:0009085), thickened mesocarp (PO:0009087), and endocarp (PO:0009086), and has as parts juice sacs (PO:0006013) on a carpel adaxial epidermis (PO:0025618).
annotations.comment: A hesperidium fruit (PO:0030109) develops from a superior gynoecium (PO:0009062), and therefore is not surrounded by receptacle (PO:0009064) tissue. Examples: orange, grapefruit, lemon, and lime (Citrus spp.).

annotations.label: pome fruit
annotations.definition: A berry fruit (PO:0030108) which develops from an inferior gynoecium (PO:0009062) and at maturity comprises a fleshy exocarp (PO:0009085), fleshy mesocarp (PO:0009087), and hardened endocarp (PO:0009086) and has as a part a fleshy hypanthium (PO:0009065) which is indistinct from the exocarp (PO:0009085).
annotations.comment: A pome fruit (PO:0030110) is a specialized type of berry fruit (PO:0030108) which is encapsuled in persistent, accrescent receptacle (PO:0009064) and/or hypanthium (PO:0009065) tissue. Examples: Apple, crab apple, and pear (Malus spp. and Pyrus spp.)

annotations.label: pepo fruit
annotations.definition: A berry fruit (PO:0030108) which develops from an inferior carpel (PO:0009030) and at maturity comprises a fleshy exocarp (PO:0009085), fleshy mesocarp (PO:0009087), and fleshy endocarp (PO:0009086), and has as a part a hard receptacle (PO:0009064) which surrounds, and is indistinct from, the exocarp (PO:0009085).
annotations.comment: A pepo fruit (PO:0030111) is specialized type of berry fruit (PO:0030108) which is encapsuled by persistent, accrescent receptacle (PO:0009064) tissue. The rind, or tough outer covering, of pepo fruits comprises receptical tissue and at least the exocarp (PO:0009085) layer of the pericarp (PO:0009084). Examples: pumpkin (Cucurbita pepo), cucumber (Cucumis sativus), and watermelon (Citrullus lanatus) and  banana (Musa acuminata).

annotations.label: pedicel
annotations.definition: A shoot axis (PO:0025029) that bears only a flower (PO:0009046) or a fruit (PO:0009001).
annotations.comment: A pedicel has a single internode, determinate growth, and is the ultimate shoot axis (PO:0025029) in a plant. When annotating to this term, use the most specific subclass of pedicel: flower pedicel (PO:0030113), inflorescence flower pedicel (PO:0009052), fruit pedicel (PO:0030114), or infructescence fruit pedicel (PO:0004536).

annotations.label: flower pedicel
annotations.definition: A pedicel (PO:0030112) that bears a flower (PO:0009046).

annotations.label: fruit pedicel
annotations.definition: A pedicel (PO:0030112) that bears a fruit (PO:0009001).

annotations.label: raceme inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of pedicellate flowers (PO:0009046) on an indeterminate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104).
annotations.comment: Examples include mouse-ear cress (Arabidopsis thaliana, Brassicaceae), wild black cherry (Prunus serotina, Rosaceae), and pokeweeds (Phytolacca spp., Phytolaccaceae).

annotations.label: compound raceme inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of an indeterminate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104) and pedicellate flowers (PO:0009046) on indeterminate higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0009081).
annotations.comment: Examples include many species Brassicaceae, such as pepperweeds (Lepidium spp.), and Fabaceae, such as melilot (Melilotus officinialis) and hop trefoil (Trifolium campestre) species, as well as bananas (Musa spp.; Musaceae).

annotations.label: spike inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of sessile flowers (PO:0009046) on an indeterminate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104).
annotations.comment: The basal-most flowers of some spike inflorescences have short inflorescence flower pedicels (PO:0009052), such as in ladies'-tresses orchids (Spiranthes; Orchidaceae). Examples include black pepper (Piper nigrum; Piperaceae).

annotations.label: compound spike inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049), with a monopodial growth habit, which at anthesis is composed of an indeterminate first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104) and sessile (PATO:0001436) flowers (PO:0009046), or spikelets (PO:0009051), on indeterminate higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0009081).
annotations.comment: Examples include some amaranth species (Amaranthus spp., Amaranthaceae).

annotations.label: catkin inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616) is composed of indeterminate, first order inflorescence axes (PO:0025104) and sessile, unisexual flowers (PO:0009046) that are each subtended by conspicuous inflorescence bracts (PO:0009054).
annotations.comment: Examples include poplars (including Populus trichocarpa, Salicaceae) and birches (Betula spp., Betulaceae). Catkin inflorescences are often pendulate.

annotations.label: spadix inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of sessile flowers (PO:0009046) on a radially-enlarged, indeterminate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104), and a basal, enveloping inflorescence bract (PO:0009054).
annotations.comment: Examples include aroids (Araceae), palms (Arecaceae).

annotations.label: capitulum inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616) is composed of a radially-enlarged, determinate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104) with sessile flowers (PO:0009046).
annotations.comment: Many capitulum inflorescences have basal involucral bracts (PO:0009045) and are often discoid or globose in shape. Examples include sunflowers (Helianthus spp.; Asteraceae) and teasels (Dipsacus spp.; Dipsacaceae).

annotations.label: compound capitulum inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616) is composed of a radially-enlarged inflorescence axis (PO:0020122), and sessile flowers (PO:0009046) on determinate higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0009081) with basal involucral bracts (PO:0009045).
annotations.comment: Examples include many species of daisy family (Asteraceae). The second order inflorescences (PO:0025240) of compound capitulum inflorescences are often called glomerules.

annotations.label: panicle inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616) is composed of pedicellate flowers (PO:0009046) or spikelets (PO:0009051) on determinate inflorescence axes (PO:0020122).,
annotations.comment: Examples include the majority of species in the grass family Poaceae including oats (Avena spp.), brome (Brachypodium spp.), barley (Hordeum vulgare), cutgrass (Leersia perrieri), rice (Oryza spp.), foxgrass (Setaria spp.), wheat (Triticum aestivum), wild rice (Zizania palustris), and the staminate inflorescence of cultivated corn (Zea mays), as well as grapes (Vitis spp.; Vitaceae), and Polemonium caeruleum (Polemoniaceae). Panicle inflorescences are most often conical in shape.

annotations.label: corymb inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with both monopodial and sympodial growth habits, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of a determinate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104), and determinate higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0020122) with pedicellate flowers (PO:0009046) that collectively form a convex plane of flowers.
annotations.comment: Examples include highbush blueberry (Vaccinium corymbosum; Ericaceae).

annotations.label: anthela inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with both monopodial and sympodial growth habits, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of a determinate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104) and determinate, higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0020122) with pedicellate flowers (PO:0009046) that collectively form a concave plane of flowers.
annotations.comment: Examples include species of Filipendula (Rosaceae).

annotations.label: cyme inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of a determinate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104) and pedicellate flowers (PO:0009046) on paired, determinate higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0009081).
annotations.comment: Cyme inflorescence is used here to treat inflorescences that are commonly referred to as dichasial, pleiochasial, or polychasial cymes; each of which are named according to the number of higher order inflorescence axes at a single axis. Cyme is not used here to treat monochasial cymose inflorescences, which contain at least one repeating sympodial unit, such as cincinnus inflorescence (PO:0030132), compound cincinnus inflorescence (PO:0030133), bostryx (PO:0030134), rhipidium inflorescence (PO:0030131), drepanium inflorescence (PO:0030135), and compound drepanium inflorescence (PO:0030136). Examples include many species of the Figwort family (Scrophulariaceae, including green figwort Scrophularia umbrosa) and the Verbena family (Verbenaceae).

annotations.label: indeterminate thyrse inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of an indeterminate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104) and dichasial second order inflorescences (PO:0025240) with pedicellate  flowers (PO:0009046), and determinate higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0009081).
annotations.comment: The higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0009081) of an indeterminate thyrse inflorescence may have either a monopodial or sympodial growth habit depending on whether single or paired inflorescence axillary meristems (PO:0009105) develop at each axis. Examples include beardtongues (Penstemon spp.; Plantaginaceae).

annotations.label: determinate thyrse inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) which at flowering stage (PO:0007616) is composed of a determinate, first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104) and dichasial second order inflorescences with pedicellate flowers (PO:0009046) and determinate, higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0009081).
annotations.comment: The higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0009081) of an determinate thyrse inflorescence may have either a monopodial or sympodial growth habit depending on whether single or paired inflorescence axillary meristems (PO:0009105) develop at each axis. Examples include lilacs (Syringia spp.; Oleaceae), buckeyes (Aesculus spp.; Hippocastanaceae) and hydrangea (Hydrangea paniculata; Hydrangeaceae).

annotations.label: umbel inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of pedicellate flowers (PO:0009046) on a first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104) with compressed inflorescence axis internodes (PO:0006326).
annotations.comment: The flowers (PO:0009046) of an umbel inflorescence appear as clusters originating from a single point, due to compression of the inflorescence axis internodes (PO:0006326) of the first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104). Umbel inflorescences are characteristic of the the Ivy family (Apiaceae or Umbelliferae), including english ivy (Hedera helix), ginseng (Panax ginseng), and the onion family (Alliaceae).

annotations.label: compound umbel inflorescence
annotations.definition: An inflorescence (PO:0009049) with a monopodial growth habit, which at flowering stage (PO:0007616), is composed of a first order inflorescence axis (PO:0025104) with compressed inflorescence axis internodes (PO:0006326), and pedicellate flowers (PO:0009046) on higher order inflorescence axes, with compressed inflorescence axis internodes (PO:0006326).
annotations.comment: The flowers (PO:0009046) of a compound umbel inflorescence appear as clusters originating from a single point, due to compression of inflorescence axis internodes (PO:0006326) of the higher order inflorescence axes (PO:0009081). Examples include species of the Ivy family (Apiaceae or Umbelliferae), including dill (Anethum graveolens), fennel (Foeniculum vulgare, Apiaceae) and poison hemlock (Conium maculatum, Apiaceae).

annotations.label: rhipidium inflorescence
